title: Animal Production Through to Carcass Classification
intro: Current and future practices for meat production from farm to carcasses leaving the slaughter floor. Topics include animal growth and development, genetics and breeding, on-farm opportunities to influence carcass and meat quality, pre-slaughter handling of meat-producing animals, slaughter and carcass dressing procedures, carcass classification, animal welfare, occupational safety and health, and factory farming.
description: Year 1 Agriculture paper (15 credits, 280101). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

This will include a focus on learning trajectories for key mathematical concepts.

title: Te Marautanga o Te Aho Matua o ngā Kura Kaupapa Māori III: ko te tuitui i ngā kaupapa hei marautanga
intro: Kia tū motuhake te tauira e waihanga ana i te marautanga o te akomanga kei reira a ia e whakangungu ana.  He āta aro i ētahi akoranga tawhito, akoranga hou a te Māori hei tūāpapa i ngā tūtioka ā-marau.  Ka āta whakawhanakehia ki ngā marau me ōna mātauranga, ka whātoro hoki i ngā ariā o tā te taiao mō Te Aho Matua.        Independently create and implement the curriculum within the classroom the student is based for practicum.  An in-depth study of customary and contemporary Māori practices using an interdisciplinary approach.  Further development of subject and curriculum knowledge and the relevant pedagogy as applied to the Te Aho Matua environment.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 268341). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Taiao mō Te Aho Matua - te whakarite i te taiao ki te poipoi i te ako i roto i ngā Kura Kaupapa Māori III
intro: Ka tūhonotia te kawa, ngā ritenga, ngā whakahaere, ngā whakamaheretanga kia tupu te taiao e tika ana mō Te Aho Matua, hei painga mō ngā tamariki.  He āta arohaehae, he toro i ngā mahinga rangahau, i ngā whakaakoranga huritao, i ngā mahinga whakahaere  mō tētahi wā roa ki tā Ta Aho Matua.    The combination of the protocol, the preparations, administration and planning appropriate for the Te Aho Matua environment maximising benefits for the children.  An in-depth analysis and engagement in research-informed, reflective teaching and management practices for a sustained period using a Te Aho Matua framework.
description: Year 3 paper (30 credits, 268307). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Holistic Approaches to Communication Disorders
intro: This course uses holistic and biopsychosocial frameworks to describe and understand the nature and impact of communication disorders across the lifespan.  Environmental and conversational partner strategies are highlighted to promote communication access for children and adults with speech, language and communication support needs.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 271150). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Marautanga o Te Aho Matua o ngā Kura Kaupapa Māori II: ko te tuitui i ngā kaupapa hei marautanga
intro: Whai muri mai i Te Aro ā-Kapa i te tau tuatahi ka huri ke He tūhonohono tonu i ngā marau kei raro i te kaupapa matua e ai ki ngā Kura Kaupapa Māori.  Ka aro nui ki ngā tikanga tawhito me ngā tīkanga hou e hāngai pū ana ki ngā hangarau me ōna whakamahinga, ki tā te tirohanga Māori.  Ko te wai, ko te waka ama te tauira.    After the introduction to the integrated curriculum approach, students will continue to develop their ability to integrate subjects under a principal theme in ways Kura Kaupapa Māori do.  An emphasis on customary and contemporary Māori practices and their associated technologies.  Water and waka ama provide the example.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 268241). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: An exploration of language and communication support needs experienced by school-aged children and young people in educational and community contexts. Support for their participation and learning in these contexts is based on evidence, neurodevelopmental, ecological, and sociocultural factors.

title: Tū Rangaranga: Global Encounters
intro: The course explores our connections, impacts, and roles in the world, and our rights and responsibilities as global citizens. It examines what citizenship means in 21st century Aotearoa/NZ, given its history, cultural diversity, and place in the global arena. The course introduces the notion of global citizenship, and explores the relationship between individual and collective action in addressing global problems.
description: Year 2 Art History paper (15 credits, 230210). Prerequisite study in Art History is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Tū Tira Mai: Practising Engagement
intro: The questions of and possibilities for agency and action form the core of this course, through an enquiry-based exploration of the capacities of the humanities and social sciences for action, intervention and contribution in professional and community contexts. The course also covers the development and application of research skills, problem-solving skills, and ethical awareness in addressing practical issues.
description: Year 3 Art History paper (15 credits, 230310). Prerequisite study in Art History is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: An introduction to the concepts, methods and scope of the Social Sciences through study of contemporary social issues in Aotearoa/ New Zealand and in global contexts.

title: Research Methods in Engineering and Technology
intro: Research methods for students in engineering and technology. Topics include: statistical design of research experiments; hypothesis testing; use of statistical models to test engineering questions; data analysis; literature searches; development of research proposals; critical assessment of scientific literature.
description: Postgraduate Information Technology paper (15 credits, 228797). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Tū Arohae: Critical Thinking
intro: This course develops students’ foundational analytical and critical thinking skills. It is designed to provide students in any discipline with the ability to describe, evaluate, and generate reasoning / arguments effectively, appropriately, and sympathetically, alongside an understanding of the hidden complexities inherent in this approach and its limits when employed as a form of persuasion.
description: Year 1 Art History paper (15 credits, 230112). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Tū Kupu: Writing and Inquiry
intro: This course introduces students to cultures of writing and inquiry in the Humanities and Social Sciences. It is designed to help students write effectively at undergraduate level by practising a variety of writing tasks, including analytical, persuasive, and research-based writing and argumentation. Students will learn practices of writing, research, peer-review and revision that have application in the university and broader contexts.
description: Year 1 Art History paper (15 credits, 230111). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Tūrangawaewae: Identity & Belonging in Aotearoa NZ
intro: This course examines formations of identity and belonging in relation to concepts of place and turangawaewae (‘standing place’). The multiple factors shaping identity formation, citizenship and public engagement will be explored, and students will develop awareness of and reflect on diverse perspectives regarding identity and citizenship, and apply this understanding to analyse issues in contemporary New Zealand society.
description: Year 1 Art History paper (15 credits, 230110). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: A comprehensive study of a specific area of engineering or technology employing appropriate research practice to derive recommendations with respect to a pre-determined hypothesis.
description: Postgraduate Information Technology paper (30 credits, 228798). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Engineering Practice 4: Materials & Manufacturing
intro: The design and development of solutions to engineering problems with “concurrent engineering” capability is a core activity for engineers. A practical course with emphasis on integrating materials and manufacturing process selection into a structured design methodology based on the application of engineering and scientific principles.
description: Year 2 Information Technology paper (15 credits, 228212). Prerequisite study in Information Technology is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Engineering Practice 5: Engineering Design with Constraints
intro: Technology must be effectively interfaced with society and the environment to succeed. Overarching social and environmental constraints are applied, including impact assessment and efficiency analysis tools. Project based learning is undertaken on the sustainable design of a consumer product underpinned by appropriate technical knowledge.
description: Year 3 Information Technology paper (30 credits, 228311). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Engineering Practice 3: Product Development
intro: The development of new and improved products is a key role of most practising engineers. This course provides the structured process and tools required for successful product development in the context of an applied project.
description: Year 2 Information Technology paper (15 credits, 228211). Prerequisite study in Information Technology is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Special Topic
description: Postgraduate Information Technology paper (30 credits, 228792). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Foundation Humanities
intro: An introduction to humanities disciplines and subject areas through study of the underlying philosophies, concepts and perspectives of the humanities.
description: Foundation Art History paper (15 credits, 230001). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Practicum II
intro: Working in an industrial or commercial setting to gain real experience and apply knowledge, providing a foundation for a future career in industry. Continued development of the skills required to become a professional engineer and technologist.
description: Year 3 Information Technology paper (0 credits, 228310). Prerequisite study in Information Technology is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Engineering Practice 6: Design Capstone Project
intro: This course marks the culmination of the BE(Hons) degree, that integrates the skills and knowledge acquired throughout the programme. It places specific emphasis on a commercial basis for complex engineering problem solving where success will be measured not only by the elegance of the engineering solution but also by the appropriateness of that solution within a defined commercial context.
description: Postgraduate Information Technology paper (30 credits, 228711). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Statistical Modelling for Engineers and Technologists
intro: This is a core course that provides essential grounding in statistical inference and modelling for engineers and technologists. Students will learn how to develop statistical models to describe random phenomena, and use them to test engineering questions of practical interest.
description: Year 3 Information Technology paper (15 credits, 228371). Prerequisite study in Information Technology is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Food Technology
intro: An introduction to the fundamental technology and engineering skills required for professional engineers and technologists. Students will develop practical skills to design and solve engineering and technology problems carrying out simple design projects and creating new innovative solutions. Projects will include consideration of cultural, ethical and safety aspects and students will develop skills to effectively communicate design solutions.
description: Year 1 Information Technology paper (15 credits, 228115). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Engineering Practice 5: Process Engineering with Constraints
intro: Technology must be effectively interfaced with society and the environment to succeed. Overarching social and environmental constraints are responded to using a systematic application of process engineering. Project based learning is undertaken on supply of utility inputs to and waste treatment outputs from an industrial process.
description: Year 3 Information Technology paper (30 credits, 228312). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Practicum I
intro: Working in an industrial or commercial setting to gain real experience and apply knowledge, providing a foundation for a future career in industry. Continued development of the skills required to become a professional engineer and technologist.
description: Year 2 Information Technology paper (0 credits, 228210). Prerequisite study in Information Technology is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

description: Postgraduate Research English Literature paper (60 credits, 219889). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Engineering Mathematics 2
intro: This is a core course that provides key mathematical tools for modelling and analysing engineering problems.  These tools represent a balance of stochastic and deterministic modelling approaches along with their mathematical underpinnings.  Topics include random variables and distributions; analytical and numerical solution methods for linear systems of ordinary differential equations including Laplace transform techniques; sensitivity analysis, optimization, curve-fitting and interpolation for data sets.
description: Year 2 Information Technology paper (15 credits, 228271). Prerequisite study in Information Technology is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Veterinary Clinics and Public Health
intro: Tuition, demonstration and clinical experience in surgery, anaesthesia, medicine, epidemiology and theriogenology of domestic animals; health and management of production animals; diagnostic procedures, including imaging, necropsies and laboratory tests; and diagnostic reasoning.  Professional ethics and legislative obligations to the public and state; the role of veterinary professional organisations and veterinarians as communicators and educators, veterinary business management and the maintenance of physical and mental fitness as a veterinarian.  Principles and practical applications of veterinary public health, meat hygiene and quality assurance programmes to meet national and international standards will also be taught. Opportunities for students to gain further experience in chosen areas of interest.
description: Postgraduate Development Studies paper (120 credits, 227511). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: BVSc Veterinary Practical Work
intro: During this course, students will undertake practical work in external veterinary practices under the supervision of a registered veterinarian, to develop clinical skills and gain experience of client-based veterinary practice.
description: Honours Development Studies paper (0 credits, 227410). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: BVSc Farm Practical Work
intro: In this course students will undertake farm work in cattle, sheep,  horse and other livestock industries. Students will develop practical animal skills, and skills in observation, identification, analysis and communication.
description: Year 3 Development Studies paper (0 credits, 227310). Prerequisite study in Development Studies is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Spatial Design Research Project
intro: Application and development of research in an independent spatial design project.
description: Honours Economics paper (45 credits, 224454). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Farm Practical Training
intro: A practicum during which students will learn practical skills for working with livestock, farm safety and understanding farm-level agricultural economics.  Modules on Health and Safety and handling of Agrichemicals useful for practical placements are also provided.
description: Year 1 Development Studies paper (0 credits, 227110). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Spatial Design 3.3: Advanced Spatial Practices
intro: In this studio course students will further develop their understanding of spatial ecologies. Students will consolidate and apply their individual spatial practice towards the design of a comprehensive spatial design proposition. In addition to advancing theoretical and practical approaches, techniques and skills in the production of their creative work, students will attain transferable skills relevant to professional environments.
description: Year 3 Economics paper (30 credits, 224358). Prerequisite study in Economics is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Animal Behaviour and Welfare for Veterinary Science
intro: An introduction to common ethical frameworks for animal use with a focus on domestic animal species. Principles of the behaviour and welfare of domestic animal species. Theory of  safe and effective animal handling.
description: Year 1 Development Studies paper (15 credits, 227107). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Veterinary Biochemistry
intro: An introductory biochemistry course covering the fundamental concepts of protein structure and function as well as metabolism of carbohydrates, lipids and amino acids. The focus will be on energy transactions in a physiological context including glucose homeostasis and muscle metabolism in mammals. A lecture and problem-based tutorial course will be complemented by case studies relevant to animal health and disease.
description: Year 1 Development Studies paper (15 credits, 227106). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Journalism and Society
intro: Students study and critically reflect on the role and function of journalism in society. This includes the principles of media law and ethics.
description: Postgraduate English Literature paper (30 credits, 219714). Available at: Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Māori Art and Design Studio IV - Toi Atea
intro: An advanced course to focus and refine visual language and personal tikanga through negotiated projects that explore topics of direct reference to Māori people and their communities.
description: Honours paper (30 credits, 237417). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Visual & Material Culture
description: Doctoral paper (120 credits, 237900). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Māori Art and Design Studio IIIB - Toi Atea
intro: Further development of a range of visual responses to issues (social, political, environmental, global) of significance to Māori people and their communities.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 237318). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Critical Perspectives on Contemporary Art
intro: An exploration of diverse perspectives to develop critical responses relevant to contemporary art in Aotearoa. Students will examine cultural, theoretical, political, social, and ethical issues that are contributing to the shaping of contemporary art. The understandings gained will contextualise the student's own practice in relation to local and global shifts in cultural production that have taken place over the past two decades.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 237231). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) and Footprinting Principles
intro: The concept of life cycle thinking and its application to support decision-making for product systems. The course will provide an overview of the principles and methodology for Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) according to the ISO 14040 and 14044 standards, and introduce related environmental footprinting approaches (carbon and water footprinting).
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 238700). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Creative Exposition
intro: In this course students will situate their creative practice through writing. They will extend and hone their critical, analytical and reflective skills through written exposition that focuses on projecting themselves and their practice externally. This advanced programme of study will encourage discussion on diverse approaches to creative research exposition and its relevance for a range of academic and/or professional contexts.
description: Honours paper (15 credits, 237465). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Nanoscience
description: Doctoral paper (120 credits, 236900). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Māori Agribusiness Systems
intro: An introduction to Māori agribusiness systems including the values, science and business processes aligned to these. Particular emphasis will be placed on the concepts of land and resource utility, kaitiakitanga and the relationship of these kaupapa Māori based systems to the present day. These systems will be discussed in context with Te Tiriti o Waitangi and present day legislation.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 235211). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Māori Natural Resource Policy
intro: This course examines the key issues and practice of Māori resources policy.  The roles and responsibilities of policy agencies in relation to Māori resources policy research, development, implementation and monitoring are considered.  Alternative policy approaches to achieving Māori and natural resource management outcomes are examined through selected case studies.  These processes will be discussed in context with Te Tiriti o Waitangi.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 235707). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Māori Art and Design Studio IIIA - Toi Atea
intro: Developing visual responses to the exploration of issues (social, political, environmental, global) significant to Māori people and their communities.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 237317). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Critical Understandings of Contemporary Art
intro: This course examines and appraises ideas that contribute to a critical understanding of contemporary art. Students will evaluate a range of content in relation to their own creative practice as well as the wider contemporary art world. The relation between artists and their processes and approaches will be explored through topics such as race, gender, intersectionality, decolonisation, popular culture, politics, social justice, and others.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 237331). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Māori Art and Design Studio IIB - Toi Atea
intro: Further development of individual work that grows out of the investigation of a select range of Māori concepts, art forms, imagery, values and approaches that reflect a Māori world view.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 237218). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Māori Resource and Environmental Management - Fresh Water
intro: A course that offers the opportunity for postgraduate students to learn about the Māori concepts, values and science processes associated with the management of fresh water.  Particular emphasis is placed on the importance of Māori values as they apply to sustainable fresh water management. These values will be discussed in context with Te Tiriti o Waitangi and present day legislation. Case studies will be undertaken to develop skills in the management of Māori fresh water resources.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 235703). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Māori Resource and Environmental Management - Whenua
intro: This course offers the opportunity for postgraduate students to learn and apply Māori concepts, values and science processes to the management of whenua or land and whenua sustainability. These values will be discussed in context with Te Tiriti o Waitangi and present day legislation. Case studies will be undertaken to develop skills in the management of Māori whenua or land resources.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 235702). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Sport in the Social Context
intro: An advanced examination of the meaning and role(s) of sport and leisure in contemporary society with particular reference to selected topical issues and the implications that these have on the organisation and management of sport.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 234731). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Art, Culture and Contexts
intro: An introduction to critical thinking through key contemporary topics and debates related to the production and reception of art. Students will have the opportunity to investigate a range of contemporary art practices and texts, in order to recognise and gain insights of their own values and processes as creative practitioners. Examples will be drawn from both Aotearoa and international contexts.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 237131). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Case Studies in Māori Agribusiness
intro: This course explores Māori agribusiness through a series of case studies in the field with an emphasis on the temporal understanding of Māori agribusiness.  Each case study has an emphasis on analysis and decision making and is applied to a range of Māori agribusinesses.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 235312). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Māori Art and Design Studio I - Toi Atea
intro: An introduction to the development of personal forms of expression though an engagement with the values, concepts, traditions, art/design forms and structures of the whare whakairo.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 237117). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Investigating Sports Performance
intro: An introduction to research methods in sport and exercise including the research process, formulating a research question, data collection, analysis, and dissemination. Students will develop and undertake a project related to their area of interest within sports performance.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 234327). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report (30)
description: Postgraduate Classics paper (30 credits, 200798). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (120 credits, 233899). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Māori Resource and Environmental Management - Flora and Fauna
intro: This course offers the opportunity for postgraduate students to learn about the Māori concepts, values and science processes associated with the management of native flora and fauna.  Particular emphasis is placed on the importance of Māori values and practices as they apply to sustainable management of native flora and fauna resources. These values will be discussed in context with Te Tiriti o Waitangi and present day legislation. Case studies will be undertaken to develop skills in the Māori component of management of native flora and fauna.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 235704). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Māori Resource and Environmental Management - Foreshore and Oceans
intro: A course that offers the opportunity for postgraduate students to learn about the Māori concepts, values and science processes associated with the management of the foreshore and ocean resources.  Particular emphasis is placed on the importance of Māori values and practices as they apply to sustainable management of foreshore and ocean resources. These values will be discussed in context with Te Tiriti o Waitangi and present day legislation. Case studies will be undertaken to develop skills in the Māori component of management of foreshore and ocean resources.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 235705). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Sport & Exercise Science
description: Doctoral paper (120 credits, 234900). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Sport and Exercise Practicum
intro: The practicum is designed to provide practical experience in sport and exercise areas according to individual interests and experience. Students may choose to undertake a practicum in areas including, but not limited to, sport event management, sport coaching, sport coordination, sport performance, exercise prescription and athlete conditioning.
description: Year 3 paper (30 credits, 234338). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Applied Sport Coaching
intro: This course examines aspects of contemporary sport coaching through concepts of long-term athletic development and youth sport.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 234236). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Sport Management
intro: An examination of contemporary sport and recreation management practice in New Zealand and overseas.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 234732). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Maara kai - Traditional and Contemporary Māori Food Production
intro: A course that offers the opportunity for postgraduate students to learn about traditional and contemporary Māori food production including the values and science processes aligned to this activity.  Particular emphasis will be placed on the concepts of land and crop management systems, kaitiakitanga and the relationship of these kaupapa Māori based systems to the present day.  These systems will be discussed in context with Te Tiriti o Waitangi and present day legislation.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 235706). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Sport Psychology
intro: This course examines theory, research and practice in aspects of sport psychology.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 234360). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Sport and Community Development
intro: This course explores the different meanings and objectives of sport development and the variety of contexts and settings in which it takes place. Particular focus is placed on the role of sport in the community, including sport/health initiatives, policy implications, the use of sport for social, economic, and urban development, and the key skills required for grant-writing and funding applications.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 234216). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Ao Kori and Health Promotion
intro: Explores the development of physical activity and health promotion initiatives in school, workplace and community settings, and the practices of Te Ao Kori (The World of Movement).
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 234346). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Practice in Sport Management
intro: A comprehensive examination of the professional issues that impact on the practice of sport management. Professional leadership and interrelationships may be analysed using a case-study based approach. The focus is on the development of the knowledge and skills required by the sport management professional in practice.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 234832). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Māori Values and Resource Management
intro: In this applied course students will learn about Māori concepts and values associated with the management of natural resources. Students will gain an appreciation of the importance of indigenous values, and in particular Māori values and management approaches to sustainable resource management. Case studies will be undertaken to develop skills in the management of natural resources.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 235701). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Sport Pedagogy II
intro: This course examines the principles and practices associated with effective sport pedagogy especially as it is applied to the delivery of game instruction in school and sport contexts.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 234343). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Māori Policy and Agribusiness
intro: This course examines the key issues and practice of Māori agribusiness policy. The roles and responsibilities of policy agencies in relation to Māori policy, research and development, implementation and monitoring relative to agribusiness are considered. Alternative policy approaches to achieving Māori driven outcomes are examined and presented. These processes will be discussed in context with Te Tiriti o Waitangi and natural resource management.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 235311). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Exercise Psychology
intro: An examination of the association between psychological factors and participation in exercise and physical activity, and, in turn, how such participation affects personal functioning, health and well-being.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 234361). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Exercise Prescription and Therapy
intro: This course gives an in-depth knowledge of the role of exercise in rehabilitation.  Students will be able to develop and implement exercise programmes to assist with recovery from musculo-skeletal injury and various disease states, and to influence indicators of psychological health.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 234312). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Motor Learning and Control
intro: Students will study the principles and theories of motor control and their practical applications.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 234242). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fitness Assessment
intro: A practical application of fitness assessment methods for healthy individuals.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 234213). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Physical Education
intro: An introduction to health and physical education through the exploration of a range of teaching models, management techniques, contemporary issues, and diversity with consideration to the Treaty of Waitangi relevant to undergraduate students.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 234140). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Hispanic Culture and Heritage
intro: An introduction to significant aspects of modern Hispanic history, literature, art and culture in Spain and the Spanish-speaking world. No prior knowledge of Spanish is required.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 245103). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Reading and Writing about Current Japan
intro: Selected issues of contemporary Japanese society will be studied through the medium of texts in the Japanese language. Further development of Japanese skills in reading and writing.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 242304). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Spanish Conversation on Film
intro: An advanced study of contemporary Latin American cinema within the context of current socio-political debates.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 245305). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introductory Spanish Language II
intro: An extension of basic communication skills in spoken and written Spanish. Aspects of contemporary Hispanic culture and society are also studied.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 245102). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Chinese Studies
description: Doctoral paper (120 credits, 241900). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Chinese 2A
intro: Provides students with intermediate level of proficiency in Putonghua (Mandarin), building on skills in reading, writing, listening and speaking. Intermediate level structures, vocabulary and characters are reinforced through oral and written exercises. Students acquire the tools appropriate to this level to discuss topics relevant to themselves and to Chinese culture and society.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 241201). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: China under Transformation: Economy, Society and Diplomacy
intro: An introduction to the multifaceted nature of China’s recent transformation from economic, societal, political and diplomatic perspectives.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 241107). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Travellers' Tales: the Invention of Latin America
intro: An exploration of how Latin American people, landscapes and cultures have been defined through the writings of European, North American, Australasian and Latin American travellers. This course will be taught in Spanish.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 245304). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Japanese 1A
intro: This course provides students with introductory level proficiency in Japanese listening, speaking, reading and writing skills.  Students learn romanised script, the two phonetic scripts (hiragana and katakana) and approximately 60 Japanese characters (kanji), as well as basic sentence structures and vocabulary useful for everyday life in Japan.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 242101). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Genetics and Evolution
intro: This fundamental course for the biological sciences explores the genetic principles and evolutionary processes important for understanding the relationships among genetic diversity, phenotype variation, and biological evolution. Topics include sources of molecular genetic variation, the genetic basis of traits with simple and complex patterns of inheritance, evolutionary mechanisms and patterns, and molecular evolution.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 203210). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Translation from and into Chinese
intro: An advanced introduction to the theory and practice of translation from and into Chinese, with an emphasis on the development and application of written translation skills.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 241305). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Japanese 3B
intro: This course further extends students' Japanese proficiency in reading, writing, listening and speaking to advanced level, building on skills established in 242.301 Japanese 3A. Students will continue to further extend and refine their ability in Japanese through practising an increased range of vocabulary and idioms, grammatical structures, respect language (keigo) and approximately 208 new kanji. Students will be expected to develop their comprehension and communication skills for confident and accurate use in more varied situations.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 242302). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Latin American Rhythms and Politics: From Tango to Rock
intro: Study of representative twentieth century Latin American music styles and their links to key social and political events that have shaped Latin American history. This course will be taught in Spanish.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 245303). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introductory Spanish Language I
intro: An introduction to basic communication skills in spoken and written Spanish. Aspects of contemporary Hispanic culture and society are also studied.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 245101). Available at: Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S1 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Japanese 3A
intro: This course provides students with a pre-advanced level of reading, writing, listening and speaking proficiency in Japanese building on skills established in prior study. Students will continue to extend and refine their ability in Japanese through using a range of exercises practising more advanced vocabulary and idioms, grammatical structures, respect language (keigo) and approximately 244 new kanji. Students will be expected to develop comprehension and communication skills to deal confidently and accurately with various situations.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 242301). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Contrastive Study of Chinese and English
intro: A systematic study of Chinese grammar based on contrastive analysis as an approach to develop communicative skills through increased language awareness. Topics include basic concepts, sentence structure, special verbal constructions, tense and aspect, and complements.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 241304). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Contemporary French Popular Culture
intro: For students with Advanced level of French. Practical study of contemporary examples of French popular language and culture in a range of fields. An Advanced level review of written and spoken French, further developing skills in vocabulary, grammar, composition, comprehension and oral communication.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 243304). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Ancient Chinese World pre-republic (1912)
intro: A course of Chinese history, philosophy, literature and arts before 1912.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 241103). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Supply Chain Analytics
intro: Determining information required for management of the supply chain.  Includes a systems dynamics view of production management, process improvement methodologies, supply chain measurements and analysis.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 240753). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Classical Studies
description: Doctoral Classical Studies paper (120 credits, 201901). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Japanese 2B
intro: This course further develops students' reading, writing, listening and speaking proficiency in Japanese at pre-intermediate level, extending skills gained in 242.201 Japanese 2A. Student will continue to use a range of written and oral exercises to practise new grammatical structures, vocabulary and approximately 100 new kanji, and will improve their ability to discuss topics of relevance to themselves and Japanese culture and society at an appropriate level. The use of respect language (keigo) will also be introduced.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 242202). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Theory and Practice of Spanish Translation
intro: An advanced introduction to the theory and practice of translation from and into Spanish and the role and responsibility of the translator.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 245302). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced French Language
intro: For students with an autonomous level of oral and written French. An advanced-level course in French to further develop written and oral comprehension, expression and analysis, based on contemporary texts and recordings.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 243301). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Chinese 3A
intro: Provides students with pre-advanced level proficiency in Putonghua (Mandarin), building on skills in reading, writing, listening and speaking. Pre-advanced level structures, vocabulary and characters extend and refine linguistic capabilities through oral and written exercises, and students will acquire comprehension and communication skills to deal confidently and accurately with various situations.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 241301). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Logistics and Supply Chain Management
description: Doctoral paper (120 credits, 240900). Available at: Auckland. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (120 credits, 240899). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Chinese 3B
intro: Provides students with advanced level proficiency in Putonghua (Mandarin), building on skills in reading, writing, listening and speaking. Advanced level structures, vocabulary and characters extend and refine linguistic capabilities through oral and written exercises, and students will acquire greater comprehension and communication skills to deal confidently and accurately with various situations.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 241302). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Japanese Cinema
intro: This course will introduce Japanese cinema in English translation as an art form that strongly reflects its culture. Traditional samurai films, contemporary social problem films and animations are used to explore how films address issues of modernity and national identity. No previous knowledge of Japanese language, history, or culture is required.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 242205). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Value Chain Management
intro: Value chains are explored through a supply chain operations management lens. Value chain networks are examined in the context of supply chain collaboration and coordination. The purpose is to provide students with the core principles of value creation and management, and to introduce them to supply chain analytical approaches to facilitate business decision making.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 240756). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Intermediate Latin
intro: A development of reading proficiency in classical Latin by increasing core vocabulary key grammatical concepts and translation.
description: Year 2 Classical Studies paper (15 credits, 201212). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Earth Science Fieldwork
intro: This course provides a hands-on experience of geoscientific fieldwork, from data collection through to analysis and interpretation of these data to explain geological and geomorphological processes. Analyses and interpretations will be applied to land, resource, and hazard management issues in New Zealand.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 233213). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Design paper (60 credits, 232897). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Conservation Biology
intro: A study of the multidisciplinary aspects of conservation biology under three key themes: importance of biodiversity, threats to biodiversity, and conservation actions. Theories, practices, and new developments in conservation biology will be explored including New Zealand case studies.
description: Postgraduate Design paper (30 credits, 232701). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Conservation Biology
description: Doctoral Design paper (120 credits, 232900). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Public Health Praxis 2
intro: An integrative capstone practicum in the public health environment involving supervised and team-based work on defined complex contemporary public health issues.
description: Postgraduate Research Music paper (30 credits, 231819). Prerequisite study in Music is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Public Health Praxis 1
intro: A capstone practicum in the public health environment involving supervised and team-based work on defined and contemporary public health issues.
description: Postgraduate Research Music paper (30 credits, 231818). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Environmental Health
intro: Critical reviews or applied research on advanced topics in Environmental Health.
description: Postgraduate Music paper (30 credits, 231707). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Special Topic Humanities & Social Sciences
description: Postgraduate Art History paper (15 credits, 230794). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Environmental Geographic Information Systems
intro: The application of geospatial analytical techniques within the environmental sciences is required as a key decision support tool in land management. This course will explore the way cartographic modelling, data fusion, database query, geostatistical analysis and image integration are applied to solve common environmental and resource management issues drawing on a range of software currently used in industry.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 233712). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 2
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Design paper (60 credits, 232898). Prerequisite study in Design is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Earth Surface Processes
intro: This course explores surface processes across Earth’s dynamic landscapes. Focusing on mountain, hillslope, river and coastal environments, the course investigates how and why these landscapes change over time. The course introduces methods for analysing and predicting landform change, and provides a foundation for managing geomorphic issues impacting society and the environment.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 233212). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Earth Environments Through Time
intro: Principles and techniques for investigating and reconstructing past environments in a range of geological time frames and settings, with emphasis on the value of such reconstructions to understand our current environment and predict what may happen in the future.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 233311). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Public Health Thesis 90 Pt 2
intro: A supervised and guided independent study designed to lead to a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Music paper (45 credits, 231822). Prerequisite study in Music is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Natural Hazards
intro: An exploration and analysis of the nature, causes, impacts, and management of natural hazards, covering earthquakes, volcanic eruptions, landslides, weather, floods and coastal hazards. How climate change and human activity influence the hazards facing society.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 233312). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Design paper (45 credits, 232871). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 2 (60 credits)
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Design paper (60 credits, 232874). Prerequisite study in Design is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Public Health
description: Doctoral Music paper (120 credits, 231900). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Special Topic
description: Postgraduate Information Technology paper (15 credits, 228791). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Earth Systems
intro: Quantification, forecasting, visualisation and communication of geoscience system dynamics to solve a range of environmental problems. Problems explored range from environmental pollution and instability of geoengineering structures to problems posed by gravity currents, landslides, tsunamis, floods and coastline changes. Students will learn to solve these geoscience problems quantitatively using modern computational, experimental, and analytical techniques.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 233313). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Wildlife Management
intro: A problem-based course involving fieldwork, data analysis, and report writing. A range of research methods relevant to conservation managers will be explored using selected real-world problems. The problems will cover a diversity of techniques with a strong emphasis on gaining experience in conservation application and quantitative analytical methods.
description: Postgraduate Design paper (30 credits, 232703). Prerequisite study in Design is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Earth Materials
intro: This course explores the materials that make up planet Earth, and the processes integral to their genesis and distribution. The course covers the principles of stratigraphy and sedimentation, the processes and products of tectonics, and how mineralogy is key to understanding processes operating on Earth.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 233211). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Design paper (90 credits, 232875). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Special Topic Humanities & Social Sciences
description: Postgraduate Art History paper (15 credits, 230793). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Pacific Health
intro: This course provides an overview and analysis of Pacific health. Topics include: Pacific concepts of health; health effects of migration, urbanisation, demographic transition and economic development on Pacific people in Aotearoa and the Pacific; Pacific health promotion models and Pacific health policies. Pacific case studies will illustrate how providers can contribute effectively to Pacific development and improved Pacific health outcomes.
description: Postgraduate Music paper (30 credits, 231705). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Hauora Māori
intro: This course provides an overview and analysis of hauora Māori perspectives. Historical and contemporary contexts and challenges are used to discuss Māori approaches in public health, with reference to Māori knowledge and Māori leadership. The course focuses on developing critical consciousness for becoming advocates and champions for Māori health through Te Tiriti o Waitangi-led practice.
description: Postgraduate Music paper (30 credits, 231704). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Special Topic Humanities & Social Sciences
description: Postgraduate Art History paper (30 credits, 230792). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Public Health Practice
intro: This course aims to provide students with the conceptual, analytical and strategic planning basis for effective public health practice. The course focuses on the theory and practice of designing, developing, implementing and evaluating practice.  It draws upon analysis of determinants, stakeholders, Te Tiriti o Waitangi-led and community development approaches, and intervention research.
description: Postgraduate Music paper (15 credits, 231728). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Public Health
intro: An introduction to public health principles, perspectives, stakeholders and practices that are both internationally recognisable and of local importance to New Zealand.
description: Year 1 Music paper (15 credits, 231106). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Sport and Physical Activity in the Social Context
intro: An examination of the social, cultural, political, economic and educational contexts of sport and physical activity.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 234331). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Sport Pedagogy
intro: A critical examination that extends students' knowledge of the skills and methodologies relating to the learning and instruction of a range of physical activities and sports within educational contexts.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 234243). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Exercise Physiology
intro: A study of the function of human biological systems as they relate to sport and exercise.  Acute responses and the chronic adaptations to sport and exercise, specifically in relation to the core topics of human energetics, muscular, cardiovascular and respiratory systems.  A strong laboratory focus will reinforce the theory and develop laboratory skills relevant to exercise physiology.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 234223). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fitness and Athletic Conditioning
intro: This course provides an understanding of various exercise techniques and methods to developing fitness and conditioning programmes for the athletic population.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 234215). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Exercise Prescription
intro: A study of prescribing exercise and physical activity for the general population.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 234214). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Sport Biomechanics
intro: An introduction to the study of the kinematics and dynamics of the human body and the factors that influence skilful and efficient performance of bodily movements in sport and everyday life.  Students will be introduced to the techniques and methods required for qualitative analysis of complex skills.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 234222). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Functional Anatomy
intro: An introduction to the structure and function of the musculoskeletal, neuromuscular and cardiopulmonary systems and their importance to health, movement and exercise.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 234121). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Human Movement and Hauora
intro: An introduction to human movement (Te Ao Kori) and its application to the concept of Hauora (holistic health and well-being).
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 234141). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Sociology of Sport and Exercise
intro: An introduction to sociological concepts, principles and theories as applied to sport and exercise.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 234135). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Sport Organisations and Development
intro: An introduction to the provision of sport, fitness, and physical activity, including the roles of, and relationships between, key organisations and agencies
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 234131). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Environmental and Geological Hazards
intro: One of the biggest issues facing the developed and developing world is exposure to environmental and natural hazards. Practical solutions to recognising, managing and mitigating environmental/natural hazard, risk and disasters will be explored in this course. Advanced quantification of natural hazards and how this informs modern day risk assessment, mitigation and management will be explored.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 233715). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Environmental Remote Sensing
intro: Remote sensing is the process of detecting and monitoring the Earth by measuring its reflected and emitted radiation, usually from satellites or aircraft. The course will focus on the use of digital analysis of remotely sensed imagery and data. The integration of Geographic Information Systems (GIS) and digital elevation models (DEMs) with remotely sensed data will be used to highlight practical solutions in land management.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 233713). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Design paper (120 credits, 232899). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: GIS and Spatial Statistics
intro: Introduction to handling and analysis of digital geospatial data. Operation of GIS software, including collection, processing and understanding of data, production of maps and geospatial projection systems. Integration of spatial statistical software with GIS. Introduction to appropriate spatial statistics techniques including kernel smoothing, kriging, point processes and spatially correlated areal data.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 233214). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Exercise and Training
intro: An introduction to factors that influence the health and performance outcomes of exercise and training.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 234111). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Our Dynamic Earth
intro: An introductory course exploring the dynamics of Earth and how it changes through time, from the core to the atmosphere, plate tectonics to volcanoes, glaciers to rivers, mountains to oceans, and mass extinctions to the evolution of life. Through exploration of these processes and their interrelationships with environments and society, including natural hazards, climate change, and earth resources, students will develop their practical, problem solving, and communication skills.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 233105). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 2
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Design paper (45 credits, 232872). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 1 (30 credits)
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Design paper (30 credits, 232873). Prerequisite study in Design is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Public Health Thesis 90 Pt 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study designed to lead to a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Music paper (45 credits, 231821). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Political Thought
intro: An advanced study of significant and diverse scholarships and debates that frame and contest contemporary political events.
description: Postgraduate Classics paper (30 credits, 200701). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Geoscience Techniques
intro: Studies utilising modern Geoscience techniques require the collection and analysis of complex data and datasets.  In this advanced course experience will be gained in modelling and solving complex geoscience processes through advanced geological mapping and the collection of samples and data that will be analysed using specialised equipment and methods.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 233714). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Remote Sensing and Earth Observation
intro: Principles and applications of Earth observation, remote sensing and spectroscopy in earth and environmental sciences. Spatial mapping and image classification of imagery from a variety of Earth observation and airborne sensors and other observations that contribute to monitoring terrestrial environments. Students will learn how to access, interpret and utilise such satellite and airborne imagery.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 233314). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: The Sound of Spanish: Diction, Dialects and Diversity
intro: An introduction to socio-linguistic aspects of the use of Spanish around the world, including dialectal variations and bilingualism, through a study of basic Spanish phonetics and phonology.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 245203). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Spanish Language
intro: Advanced level skills in reading, free composition, listening comprehension and oral communication in Spanish, illustrating aspects of contemporary Spanish and Latin American culture and society.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 245301). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Intermediate French Language II
intro: For students with intermediate level of French. An Autonomous level review of written and spoken French, further developing skills in vocabulary, grammar, composition, comprehension, and oral communication.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 243202). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Latin American Voices
intro: An introduction to 20th century Latin American literature and its historical and political contexts through short literary works in Spanish.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 245204). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Intermediate French Language I
intro: An intermediate-level review of written and spoken French, further developing skills in vocabulary, grammar, composition, comprehension, and oral communication.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 243201). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Japanese Culture
intro: An overview of the cultural development of Japan from early times to the present.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 242103). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Japanese 2A
intro: This course provides students with a pre-intermediate level of reading, writing, listening and speaking proficiency in Japanese building on skills established in prior study. Student will use a range of written and oral exercises to practise new grammatical structures, vocabulary and approximately 80 new kanji, and will acquire the tools to discuss topics of relevance to themselves and Japanese culture and society at an appropriate level.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 242201). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introductory French Language II
intro: For students with beginners level of French. Extending skills in spoken and written French, including vocabulary acquisition, grammatical competency, reading and listening comprehension, and oral practice.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 243102). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Contemporary Chinese Society in Literature and Film
intro: An integrated account of social and historical developments of contemporary China (from 1912 to the early 1990s) through a study of selected literary texts and films. No knowledge of Chinese is required.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 241208). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Chinese Diaspora
intro: This course will examine the emergence and transformation of Chinese international migration in the global context of China’s interactions with the outside world and diaspora’s changing patterns of political, social and cultural adaptations in various regions in the world.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 241207). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Global Logistics
intro: A study of international logistics and its role in modern supply chains. Focus will be placed on international trade routes and global partnerships. Both the strategic effects of international free-trade agreements and operational level import/export processes will be explored in relation to international logistical flows.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 240366). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Japanese 1B
intro: This course builds on the introductory level proficiency in Japanese established in 242.101 Japanese 1A developing further basic competence in Japanese listening, speaking, reading and writing skills. An additional 87 kanji are learnt. An increased range of sentence structures and vocabulary useful for everyday situations and interactions in Japan are introduced.
