description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 242102). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Chinese 2B
intro: Aims to further develop intermediate level proficiency in Putonghua (Mandarin), building on skills in reading, writing, listening and speaking. Entails a further set of intermediate level structures, vocabulary and characters that are reinforced through oral and written exercises. Students discuss topics appropriate to this level and that are relevant to themselves and to Chinese culture and society.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 241202). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Chinese 1B
intro: The course builds on the proficiency in Putonghua (Mandarin) that is developed in 241.101 Chinese 1A. Upon successful completion of this course, students will have acquired the phonetics, a further set of characters and vocabulary, and more complex sentence constructions.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 241102). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Methods and Skills
intro: The scientific method will be explored through discussion of alternative research methodologies and the capabilities required to conduct academic research. The course has two interrelated goals; to develop postgraduate study skills and to prepare to undertake a quality or supply chain management research project. Students will develop the skills to synthesise literature, design research processes and structure a research report. During the course students will decide to focus on either a practical application or a more theoretical research project.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 240758). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report
intro: An empirical supply chain management research investigation. During this course students will develop intellectual independence by conducting supply chain research. Supervision will be provided to guide students, however, the responsibility for framing and conducting the investigation will be the student’s alone. Students will develop the skills to articulate, analyse and evaluate their research findings.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 240887). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Purchasing and Supply Management
intro: A study of the scope of purchasing and procurement and the principles, structures and key variables of supply management.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 240364). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Supply Chain Integration
intro: A study of the functioning of modern supply chains, their design, coordination, management and the crucial role of collaboration. Focus will be placed on inter-organisational integration and internal process alignment.
description: Year 3 paper (30 credits, 240363). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Transportation Systems
intro: This course studies transportation systems with an emphasis on international intermodal transportation. The economic and operating characteristics of differing transportation systems will be examined, along with costs and regulations of transportation services and the role of information technology on transportation systems.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 240262). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (45 credits, 239885). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Health Systems and Policy
intro: This course uses systems thinking approaches to examine and compare the Aotearoa New Zealand health system within the international context, how it is structured and operates, and how this impacts on public health responses. Health policy and public health advocacy is examined as an instrument to enhance the health system and broader responses to major public health challenges.
description: Postgraduate Music paper (15 credits, 231726). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Epidemiology and Biostatistics
intro: An exploration of the core principles and practice of epidemiology and biostatistics in assessing and responding to population health need. Students will learn the skills necessary to analyse and interpret data, disseminate information, and critically appraise quantitative literature. The focus of the course is on the application of epidemiology and biostatistics for policy development and decision-making across the health system.
description: Postgraduate Music paper (30 credits, 231727). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Social Determinants of Health
intro: This course explores the social determinants of health, the complex range of social, cultural and economic interactions that influence individual and population health and help explain health disparities amongst populations.
description: Year 1 Music paper (15 credits, 231107). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Foundations of Public Health
intro: This course presents the overarching framework, principles and core functions of public health in Aotearoa New Zealand. It utilises a multidisciplinary lens to investigate the key determinants of health status across population groups in Aotearoa New Zealand and globally. The course provides the necessary foundation for students to engage in advanced approaches essential for public health practice.
description: Postgraduate Music paper (15 credits, 231725). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Health Promotion Advocacy
intro: An analysis of approaches to advocacy in health promotion with a particular focus on practical facilitation, engagement and reflection.
description: Year 3 Music paper (15 credits, 231308). Prerequisite study in Music is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Special Topic Humanities & Social Sciences
description: Postgraduate Art History paper (30 credits, 230791). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Intervention Design
intro: A practical exploration of approaches and tools for designing interventions in public health and health promotion.
description: Year 3 Music paper (15 credits, 231307). Prerequisite study in Music is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Qualitative and Participatory Research in Public Health
intro: This course provides a foundation in qualitative and participatory research methodologies, and the different approaches to knowledge that inform qualitative and participatory research. Students will develop competencies in qualitative and participatory research processes including design, data collection, analysis, dissemination, and critique of existing literature. The course foregrounds social, cultural, political and ethical considerations in the practice of public health research.
description: Postgraduate Music paper (15 credits, 231729). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Techniques and Methods in Evaluation Research
intro: An in-depth examination of a range of qualitative, quantitative, mixed and innovative methods, skills and techniques as they would be applied to practical, 'real-life' evaluation questions.
description: Postgraduate Art History paper (30 credits, 230703). Available at: Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Navigating Health Promotion
intro: Students will explore the broad range of ways in which health can be promoted. Focusing on the application of models and theories, the course will guide students in understanding health promotion strategies at the individual, community, and population levels.
description: Year 2 Music paper (15 credits, 231200). Prerequisite study in Music is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Science and Sustainability for Engineering and Technology
intro: A project-based, interdisciplinary course introducing students to the applied scientific thinking and theories that underpin the relationship between applied science and sustainability. Students will explore the intersection of science and community through exemplars of partnership between industry and Te Ao Māori (the Māori world) in Aotearoa New Zealand. By examining the interactions between human, cultural, environmental and technological systems, students will develop their critical thinking, communication and information literacy skills as they develop solutions to contemporary challenges in sustainability in a team-based project.
description: Year 1 Foundation Studies paper (15 credits, 247114). Prerequisite study in Foundation Studies is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Science and Sustainability for ICT
intro: The pursuit of environmental sustainability is a complex societal issue. This is a problem-based course, where students will develop their critical thinking, communication and information literacy and management skills as they evaluate interdisciplinary approaches to the contemporary sustainability challenge of climate action. Students will explore the intersection of science and community through exemplars of partnership between research and Te Ao Māori (the Māori world) in the context of land, water and wellbeing in Aotearoa New Zealand.
description: Year 1 Foundation Studies paper (15 credits, 247112). Prerequisite study in Foundation Studies is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Foundation Biological Sciences
intro: An introduction to cells as the unit of life, evolution as biology's unifying theme, DNA and molecular genetics; the diversity of life, and organisms and their environment.
description: Foundation Foundation Studies paper (15 credits, 247004). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Foundation Mathematics A
intro: An introductory course designed to increase understanding and application of basic mathematics and statistics concepts and skills.
description: Foundation Foundation Studies paper (15 credits, 247005). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introductory French Language I
intro: A communicative approach to developing skills in spoken and written French, including vocabulary acquisition, grammatical competency, reading and listening comprehension, and oral practice.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 243101). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Intermediate Spanish Language I
intro: Intermediate level written and oral comprehension and communication skills in Spanish, providing a broad linguistic base for the study of Spanish and Latin American culture, literature and civilisation.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 245201). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Readings in Modern Japanese Literature
intro: A study of selected extracts in the original Japanese from the work of major modern writers.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 242305). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Individual Research Project in Chinese Studies
intro: An advanced-level research project on an approved topic about Chinese language, literature, history, politics or other cultural aspect.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 241395). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Intermediate Spanish Language II
intro: An extension of intermediate skills in reading, free composition, listening comprehension and oral communication to illustrate aspects of contemporary Spanish and Latin American culture and society.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 245202). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Chinese 1A
intro: The course provides the students with a basic proficiency in Putonghua (Mandarin Chinese). Upon successful completion of this course, students will have acquired the phonetics, knowledge of a set of characters, and the vocabulary and basic sentence constructions.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 241101). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 2 (60 credits)
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 239890). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 1 (30 credits)
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (30 credits, 239889). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 2
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (45 credits, 239886). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Logistics Operations
intro: A study of the role and structure of integrated logistics systems, including performance measurement and the application of information technology. Fundamental operational trade-offs are explored in relation to inventory, warehousing and capacity utilisation.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 240263). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Executive Supply Chain Management
intro: The operational disciplines of the successful organisation are responsible for developing and managing its response to market opportunities and supply chain management is the key to a successful response.  This course looks at the role supply chain management has in supporting organisations to create value within supply nets, webs and chains through the relationships created downstream with customers and upstream with suppliers.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 240755). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Integrated Logistics
intro:
Logistics is a foundation block within any successful business. Unless an organisation can manage and coordinate in an integrated
manner the movement of materials, information and cash through its business, and those of partner organisations, it is unlikely to
outperform its competitors.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 240759). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Demand Chain Management
intro: A study of distribution management concepts and strategy together with an exploration of demand chains. Methods to align supply chain strategies with market drivers are examined. A range of distribution channel approaches are also discussed in regard to alternative customer demands.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 240365). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Supply Chain Trends and Applications
intro: A study of the impact of current industry trends on how businesses can engineer and operate their logistics and supply chains to create greater value for the organisation.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 240362). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Logistics and Supply Chain Fundamentals
intro: A study of logistics and supply chain management fundamental concepts. The supply chain management paradigm that underpins modern business is fully explored with a specific focus on customer service, forecasting, process integration, relationship management, and performance measurement.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 240260). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: AgriCommerce Capstone
intro: An integrative course designed to capture the breadth of knowledge gained in the degree and utilise the value of teams in addressing international food and agribusiness challenges to New Zealand industries. Emphasis will be placed on the application of knowledge to understand and analyse international environments and identify sustainable, competitive export strategies for an agribusiness firm.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 239373). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Aviation Special Topic
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 190299). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Design of Airways and Air Traffic Systems
intro: An examination of air traffic and air navigational systems and the principles which apply to their operational infrastructure, including the development of visual and instrument flight procedures.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 190309). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Airport Operational Management
intro: An examination of the management of airport systems, such as air-side and land-side facilities, technical and support services which contribute to the operational effectiveness of modern airports. The course will further examine the relationship between airport authorities and the local community.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 190308). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Aerobatic Aircraft Handling
intro: The theory and practice of advanced aircraft handling techniques, aerobatics and advanced stalling and spinning.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 190285). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Airport Planning
intro: A study of demographic, environmental and economic considerations which apply to the design and evaluation of airport facilities.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 190307). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Basic Air Safety Investigation
intro: The aim of this course is to give students an insight to aircraft accident investigation. The course will also  benefit those who need to understand the investigation process, such as managers of airlines and regulatory authorities. The course covers the basic requirements and procedures involved in air safety investigation.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 190222). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Aviation Law
intro: This course examines the application of aviation law beyond the initial professional licence issue. The course meets the requirements for the NZ Civil Aviation Authority Air Transport Pilot Licence (ATPL) qualification and introduces the student to international aviation systems, including the International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO). A special emphasis is placed on the responsibilities and accountabilities of aviation personnel and management systems under the CAA Rules regime.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 190283). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Flight Instruction
intro: A theoretical and practicum-based study of flight instructional and teaching skills required for employment as Flight Instructor.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 190282). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Airline Strategic Management
intro: A review of the current state of strategic management in the airline industry. The approach is both multidisciplinary in focus and international in scope. Attention will be focused on the Asia-Pacific region, as the potential location of the world's largest aviation market.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 190306). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Climatology
intro: A study of the global climate and of regional climates at the levels at which modern jet aircraft fly and at lower levels..
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 190221). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Aeroscience
intro: This course is an examination of the principles of mathematics, mechanics, and physic as they apply to aeroscience.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 190164). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Aero Systems Development
intro: An advanced theoretical and practicum based study of the regulatory requirements and operational competencies of the New Zealand Commercial Pilots Licence and Instrument Rating.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 190303). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Air Power
intro: This course examines military air power, its characteristics, history and development, including selected air campaigns and use of Remote Piloted Aerial Systems (RPAS).
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 190240). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Turbo-Prop and Jet Handling
intro: The theoretical and practical requirements for the safe ground and flight operation of gas-turbine powered aircraft will be examined.
description: Year 2 paper (30 credits, 190298). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Air Traffic Control/Aviation Law
intro: The integration of theory and practice for Flight Operations and Flight Standards. This course will enable pilots to use the operational procedures and facilities required by civil aviation and air traffic control organisations. Students will be introduced to international aviation systems, including the Covenants of the International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO).
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 190203). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Aviation Human Factors
intro: The course provides an overview of the basic concepts of human factors in aviation, human performance, and issues relating to judgement and decision-making in this high risk environment. Communication and other aspects of social psychology in various aviation environment are also explored.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 190216). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Basic Gas Turbine Operations
intro: A study of the principles of operation and construction of aircraft gas turbine engines, including identification and rectification of abnormal operation. Students will practice engine starting, shutdown, and normal and emergency procedures in simulated environments (such as in the Hawker Pilot Trainer HPT flight training device). The course is designed to meet the theoretical requirements for CAA examination leading to the award of a Gas Turbine Rating.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 190189). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Aviation Strategic Management
intro: An examination of the strategic management processes employed in the aviation industry, and how the business environment shapes strategy formulation and implementation in the aviation industry.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 190211). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Aircraft Maintenance Management
intro: An examination of knowledge appropriate to the management of an aircraft maintenance organisation.
description: Year 2 paper (30 credits, 190249). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Human Factors
intro: The application of information processing strategies for improving performance in learning, problem-solving, decision-making, interpersonal interrelations, coping in situational anxiety and mental rehearsal.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 190117). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Managing Airline Systems
intro: An examination of the management of airline organisations.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 190220). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Aircraft Performance
intro: The study of aircraft performance management for single-engine aircraft, including propeller theory, and operational performance requirements.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 190124). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Aircraft Handling
intro: This course covers the theory and practice of aircraft operations to an advanced level. The course’s emphasis is on the development of non-technical skills applicable to a pilot working in a multi-crew environment. The student will be introduced to safety management systems (SMS) and optimal decision making under normal, abnormal, and emergency phases of flight. The course bridges the gap between traditional General Aviation Operations and Air Transport Operations.
description: Year 2 paper (30 credits, 190288). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Heavy Jet Aircraft Performance
intro: This course introduces performance theory as it applies to modern heavy jet aircraft. This course covers NZ ATPL performance theory requirements.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 190237). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Research Methods in Aviation
intro: An introduction to the theories and methods of research in the aviation industry. A range of both quantitative and qualitative methodologies is explored, and various techniques for aviation research are examined.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 190225). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Aerodynamics 1
intro: This course introduces principles of incompressible fluid statics and fluid dynamics, basic concepts of low and high speed aerodynamics, and lift theory.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 190206). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Management in Aviation
intro: An overview of management concepts in the aviation industry.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 190116). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Aeronautics
intro: An introduction to the non-practical components of airline and commercial pilots’ aeronautical knowledge. Emphasis is placed on general aircraft technical knowledge, principles of flight, aeronautical meteorology, flight procedures and aviation law.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 190115). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Soil Science
description: Doctoral paper (120 credits, 189900). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work

title: Aviation Studies
intro: An introduction to the interaction of components in the aviation system, including human resources, aircraft, airports and airways systems.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 190109). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Aircraft Systems 3
intro: This course is an examination of advanced multi-engine aircraft systems, including structures of heavy aircraft.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 190201). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Environmental Impacts of Aviation
intro: Advanced study of the environment of aviation, the impacts of aviation on the environment and the methods for reducing the impacts.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 190224). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Aeronautical Legislation
intro: This course includes the integration of theory and practice for aviation regulations and law pertaining to  Visual Flight Rules (VFR) and Instrument Flight Rules (IFR).
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 190120). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Flight Planning and Advanced Navigation (Part 1)
intro: First of two courses on the development of practical flight planning competencies including route planning, fuel planning, load planning and flight plan amending for instrument flight operations. The use of inflight navigation techniques, instrument flight management, and search are included.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 190204). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Flight Planning and Advanced Navigation (Part 2)
intro: Second of two courses on the development of practical flight planning competencies, including route planning, fuel planning, load planning and flight plan amending for instrument flight operations, and for long distance heavy transport operations for B777 aircraft.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 190254). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Principles of Navigation I
intro: An introduction to navigational principles and operational requirements for visual flight.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 190104). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 189895). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Principles of Navigation II
intro: An in-depth study of Air Navigation principles; and the construction, properties and usage of aeronautical charts.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 190154). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Aviation Meteorology 1
intro: An introduction to aviation meteorology for pilots.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 190113). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Natural Resource Management
description: Doctoral paper (120 credits, 188900). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 2 (60 credits)
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 189874). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Aviation Safety Management
intro: To provide a comprehensive understanding of safety management in civil flight operations environments, particularly relating to international standards and recommended practices prescribed by the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) for the design and implementation of safety management systems in aviation.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 190210). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Aircraft Systems 2
intro: A study of aircraft systems for light aircraft and their performance management.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 190123). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Crew Resource Management
intro: The development of practical competencies in crew resource management (CRM) in the professional aviation environment.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 190205). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Aircraft Systems 1
intro: An Introduction to aircraft systems for pilots
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 190114). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Aviation Meteorology 2
intro: An examination of fundamental concepts and processes in meteorology and climate.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 190121). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (90 credits, 189875). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (120 credits, 189899). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Integrated Farm and Environmental Management
intro: This course examines farm physical resources, nutrient management and budgeting and contaminant loss processes in the context of Te Mana o te Wai. Students will integrate their knowledge to produce a freshwater Farm Environment Plan which identifies soil, pathogen, nutrient and greenhouse gas loss pathways on multiple land uses and justifies mitigation practices which are appropriate for the farm system and to protect freshwater.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 189362). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Ornithology
intro: This course examines the diversity of birds through consideration of their evolution, taxonomy, morphology and behaviour. The recognition of New Zealand birds by sight and sound is developed through tutorials. Practical work in this course focuses on research techniques employed in studies of anatomy, wing moult, plumage colouration and vocalisations.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 199330). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Industrial Design Digital Representation
intro: Digital design processes and their integration into design research.
description: Honours paper (15 credits, 198463). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Vertebrate Zoology
intro: An exploration of the diversity and origins of fish, amphibians, reptiles, mammals, birds, and other chordate animals. Form, function and evolutionary drivers of body systems in vertebrate animals are examined, especially in practical work. Evidence for evolution is emphasised in comparative anatomy laboratories. Applications of vertebrate zoology knowledge for wildlife conservation and natural history research are highlighted.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 199212). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Entomology
intro: An advanced course of study involving literature reviews, tutorials and personal research on selected aspects of insect evolution, physiology, behaviour and ecology. Topics could include the evolution of insect flight and sociality, pollination of native plants, courtship and mating behaviour, reproductive hormones and pheromones and insect dispersal, pest management and post-harvest disinfestation.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 199717). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Industrial Design Research and Development
intro: Research methods, processes and practices for industrial design and their application through a research project.
description: Honours paper (30 credits, 198453). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 2
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 197812). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Evolutionary Principles of Animal Behaviour
intro: This course explores how genetics, the environment, and humans shape the evolution of animal behaviour and inter- and intraspecific interactions. Practice sessions develop skills on the description, quantification, comparison and statistical analysis of animal behaviour using a variety of animals (vertebrate and invertebrate, wild and domestic). The use of scientific writing to communicate research findings is emphasised.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 199203). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Product Development
intro: In this studio course students will explore and articulate issues and design considerations particular to the development of products for manufacture. Through a design and build project, students will explore and apply their individual and collaborative practices required to develop a product from concept to manufacture.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 198315). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Modelling and Prototyping
intro: In this studio course students will explore issues and design considerations particular to modelling and prototyping for three-dimensional design. Through the application of skills and techniques, this course draws on a range of strategies and methods for exploring and representing the form and function of products.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 198221). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: MPhil Thesis 120 Credit Part 2
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 197809). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Animals and the Environment
intro: An introductory biology and natural history course presented within an evolutionary framework that investigates the diversity of animal life, human-animal interactions, nutrient and energy flows, conservation and sustainability. This course places emphasis on wild animals and ecological processes operating within New Zealand and globally.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 199103). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Product and Industrial Design 3.3: Cultivating Practice
intro: In this studio students develop and realise a complex project to demonstrate proficiency in Product and Industrial Design. Students work within a defined thematic using appropriate practice methods to advance and resolve complex design challenges to produce high-quality tangible outputs. In this milestone project students gain independence, demonstrate multifaceted problem solving skills and showcase their design skill set and practice.
description: Year 3 paper (30 credits, 198358). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Industrial Design Visualisation
intro: Advanced rendering and visualisation for industrial design, focusing on developing rendering, concept development and presentation skills across a wide range of digital and traditional media.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 198362). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Studio Praxis I
intro: Through the generation and presentation of a body of advanced creative work, contextual writing and participation in masterclass sessions, students identify key issues in their practice and critically respond to the research context in which they are working.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 197821). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Product Design Visualisation
intro: Introduction to visualisation techniques and processes that are used for the design of tangible products that focus on developing drawing, rendering, concept development and presentation skills across a range of software and traditional media.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 198212). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Open Design & Digital Fabrication
intro: In this studio course students will utilise open design methodologies and digital fabrication tools to produce an open design project. They will explore the possibilities and articulate issues related to an open design philosophy whilst developing skills in making and digital fabrication.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 198320). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Creative Research Special Topic B
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 197704). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Furniture Design
intro: In this studio course students will develop skills in the design, development and production of furniture in one-off, batch or mass production. By gaining an understanding of the theoretical, historical and practical applications of the discipline, they will produce a project for a specific design context.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 198213). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Product and Industrial Design 1.1: Materialising Form
intro: Focused on the development of product through a sustainability context, shaped by materials exploration, this studio provides an introduction to product and industrial design practice. Students develop creative and analytical thinking, idea generation, visualisation and making, central to three-dimensional design practice. Students will develop techniques and processes in critique, form-giving, problem solving, user-experience and affective design.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 198157). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: MPhil Thesis 120 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 197808). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

description: Postgraduate Research paper (120 credits, 197800). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Studio Praxis II
intro: Students reflect on the advanced creative work they are undertaking and the critical perspectives they have explored to develop a practical and written framework for a significant body of new work. Students develop and present a research proposal for their Thesis Project alongside a selection of creative work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 197822). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

Serving their communities as creative leaders, students will apply research and innovation theories to business and social-enterprise practices in order to conceptualise, develop, and deliver a strategic offering for a defined audience.

title: Design Awards and Competitions
intro: In this studio course you will develop a response to a top-level national or international design brief, award, or competition. You must apply for a specific offered module appropriate to your design specialisation or propose a competition brief to a supervising faculty member. All modules extend your skills in design research, conceptual development and advanced technical application, so that you can produce a high calibre award or competition entry.

title: Creative Ecologies
intro: In this studio course students will investigate how understanding “ecology” can catalyse research that may transform a current social or environmental issue. This course will engage in practices that include behavioural change frameworks, transformation design and sustainable design. A student will self-select a topic that is independently researched and results in a speculative proposal.
description: Honours paper (15 credits, 197470). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 190720). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Flight Instruction Fundamentals I
intro: Principles and techniques for theory, simulator and flight instruction for primary visual flight and day operations, including a review of aircraft performance management requirements. The course will incorporate the theoretical issues which underpin aviation science and aircraft systems.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 190315). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Contemporary Issues in Aviation Security
intro: This course explores aviation security with a particular emphasis on terrorism.  Students explore key incidents, areas at risk, and the legislation and practical means by which risks are managed.  The trade off between an erosion of civil liberties and increased security will be explored, as will the reasons why risks will always remain.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 190340). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report

description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 190895). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Human Factors for Professional Aviation
intro: An in-depth study of the latest developments in human factors policies and regulations made by the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO), with the aim of enhancing the safety, security and reliability of all areas of air transport. Contemporary research and research applications associated with the human factor aspects of aviation complements above study.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 190701). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Aerodynamics 2
intro: This course introduces principles of stability and control of aircraft, high speed aerodynamics, and methods for analysing aerodynamic properties of high speed aircraft.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 190356). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Aviation Human Factors
intro: This course explores the role of human behaviour and physiological limitations in aviation, how they fit with complex systems, and how behaviour and systems may be modified to improve safety.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 190313). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Air Transport Economics
intro: This course covers economic aspects of air transport and airline operations and provides insights into the methods employed to manage  and evaluate the economic viability of these operations.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 190318). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Legal Issues in Aviation
intro: General principles of law as applied in the context of the airline transport industry. The course will focus on the application of law to flight crew, airline operations and civil aviation authorities in terms of both international conventions and treaties and of national legislation and law. Issues such as contractual and tortious liability will be considered.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 190314). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: The clinical sciences are studied at an advanced level in one of the specialty areas of: Diagnostic Clinical Biochemistry & Immunology, Diagnostic Microbiology, Clinical Laboratory Haematology, Transfusion Science and Molecular Diagnostics.  The role of the Medical Laboratory Scientist in the generation of clinical laboratory results used in the diagnosis and the treatment of disease/disorders and the maintenance of standards of quality and laboratory safety.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 202705). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Greek Tragedy, Then and Now
intro: This course examines ancient Greek tragedy in translation. It looks at the transformation of myth into tragedy in the fifth century BCE and reflects on why these classical dramas remain popular today.

The role of the haematologist and the laboratory in the diagnosis of diseases such as anaemia, haemophilia, thrombosis, leukaemia and other blood cell neoplasms.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 202701). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: An examination of the behavioural adaptations of animals to their environment with particular emphasis on the evolution of the behaviour. Topics include foraging, reproduction, parental care, sociality, communication, and the importance of integrating behavioural ecology into conservation and co-management. The practical work includes project work, some of which takes place outside scheduled lab hours.

intro: A largely marine-based course that introduces the spectacular diversity amongst invertebrates. An appreciation of the major phyla is gained through learning about their diversity, anatomy, feeding, ecology and reproduction. Practical work focuses on identifying invertebrates, their morphology, and how they function.

title: Animal Behaviour
intro: How do animals choose a mate, rear their young, avoid predators, find a meal and communicate with each other? These problems and applications of behavioural ecology to pest control and conservation are investigated in detail through reading current literature and class discussions. Topics change from year to year and according to the interests of the participants.

intro: A critical investigation of the ways that violence, conflict, and peacebuilding are understood within politics, and the resulting sub-discipline of peace studies.

title: Ecology and Conservation
intro: Terrestrial ecology and the application to conservation biology, including evolutionary ecology, population biology, species interactions, community, ecosystem and landscape ecology. New Zealand and overseas case studies are considered throughout the course. An analytical approach is taken in the field trips and laboratory work including the use of statistics to test ecological hypotheses and to identify patterns in plant and animal distributions. There is one compulsory weekend field trip.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 196205). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Marine Mammalogy
intro: This course examines the zoogeography of marine mammals and the morphological, physiological and behavioural adaptations which have enabled this diverse group to successfully colonise all of the world’s oceans and some freshwater systems. An understanding of the underlying ecological principles not only provides interesting insights into marine mammal biology but also yields consequences for marine mammal conservation and management.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 196327). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Molecular Ecology
intro: This course explores the application and analysis of molecular markers to address questions within basic and applied ecology.  The diversity of genetic techniques, metrics, and analyses used in molecular ecology will be demonstrated.  Examples will address how molecular approaches can be applied to gain insights into ecology, demography, behaviour, biodiversity, and conservation.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 196318). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introductory Marine Biology
intro: An introduction to Marine Biology - the scientific study of life in the sea. First principles of marine biology from history of Marine Biology to use of the sea as a fundamental resource. Studying the ocean as a habitat and examining the form and function of marine taxa within marine biomes, students will discover how the biology, behaviour and ecology of organisms differ between contrasting marine environments.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 196225). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Ecology, Evolution and Behaviour
intro: An introductory biology course on the way organisms react, adapt, and interact. The course investigates the relationships between genotype, phenotype and environment, and the diversity of life on Earth within a phylogenetic framework. The course includes an examination of the dynamic interactions within and between abiotic and biotic components of ecosystems, including a focus on the evolutionary ecology of behaviours such as foraging, mating, parenting, and cooperation.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 196101). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Biodiversity of New Zealand
intro: An introduction to the biodiversity of New Zealand in a world context. This course considers the origins and relationships of New Zealand biota, species’ distributions, adaptive features, behaviours and ecology, along with a consideration of characteristic New Zealand ecosystems. Practicals include compulsory field work.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 196201). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Freshwater Ecology
intro: The study of freshwater ecosystems, including the physical and chemical processes that occur in streams, lakes, and their interactions with the biota.  Ecological concepts are applied to problems of water quality, ecosystem health, monitoring and the restoration of freshwater systems.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 196223). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Evolutionary Biology
intro: Understanding the processes and patterns of evolution is central to developing insight into questions of how and why in biology.  This course introduces students to the core concepts in evolution, including the geological, biological, phylogenetic and rational evidence for evolution by common descent.  Core concepts - including the origins of genetic and phenotypic variation, and micro and macro-evolutionary processes and patterns - will be reinforced and explored in the lab via computer-based simulations and real evolution experiments with digital organisms.  Students will be introduced to current theories of human evolution and topics of special interest including the evolution of cooperation and conflict; game theory; the origin of sex; and microbial experimental evolution.  Theory will be illustrated, where appropriate, with examples from New Zealand’s native species.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 196217). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Physiological Control Systems
intro: The principles of control systems involving nerves and hormones are examined. Control at the cellular, tissue, organ system and whole-body levels is explained with reference to the basis of cell excitability, basic functions of the nervous system, muscle contraction, actions of hormones, the immune system and the renal system.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 194241). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Physiological Strategies for Survival
intro: Survival of individual animals, and species of animals, depends on effective physiological mechanisms that allow animals to live in different environments and to respond to changes in their environment, whether benign or extreme. These mechanisms are examined in relation to environmental factors that may include photoperiod, temperature, altitude, and latitude, and in relation to life in the air and underwater.

It allows students to learn about the principles of immunology including innate immunity, cell and antibody mediated immunity, the major histocompatibility complex, transplantation immunology, hypersensitivities, immunodeficiency and autoimmunity. The course provides an introduction to vaccines, clinical immunology and immunological laboratory tests.

intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (45 credits, 196872). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Explorations in Modelling
intro:
Gain three-dimensional literacy and confidence as you model, sculpt, 3D sketch and fabricate objects. Throughout the course you will experiment with a variety of iterative practices and processes, including sculpture and modelmaking, prototyping and testing. These approaches are applicable to a range of different creative specialisations.
 
Throughout the course you will gain foundational skills and understanding in:
•	Articulating creative work and ideas as 3-dimensional objects 
•	A range of analogue and digital 3-dimensional materials and methods.
•	The processes associated with different forms of 3D making
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 197193). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Explorations in Visualisation
intro: Explorations in Visualisation explores how creative practitioners comprehend, generate, and communicate ideas. The course provides students with foundational skills and techniques for representing ideas in traditional and digital media. The course will be valuable for students from any creative area who want to communicate their work and thoughts visually.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 197123). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Community and Ecosystem Ecology
intro: A theoretical perspective to the study of community and ecosystem ecology examining the role of interactions between two or more species and their environment. Topics covered include techniques of community description, abiotic and biotic controls of community structure, the effects of disturbance, food web theory, ecosystem function and biodiversity. The emphasis will be on understanding the models and theories relating to this area of science, although examples of the application of these principles will also be given. Practical classes will involve a small research project emphasising the statistical and writing skills important in community and ecosystem ecology.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 196317). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Ecology
intro: This course explores contemporary issues in ecology, emphasising a functional outlook on the role of species in communities. It will focus on biodiversity and the interactions of organisms as stimuli for biodiversity. Topics include diversity and ecosystem function, patterns of species diversity, keystone species, herbivory, community structure, assembly roles and food web studies.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 196713). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Creative Collaboration
intro: In this course, students from a broad range of creative practices will work together to conceptualise and create innovative offerings to experience and reflect upon collaboration as creative citizens.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 197288). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Ecology
description: Doctoral paper (120 credits, 196900). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Marine Ecology
intro: Students will develop an in-depth understanding of the life histories of marine organisms and the mechanisms that determine the distribution and abundance of biodiversity in our oceans. Topics include: marine organism life histories, connectivity and dispersal, community ecology, trophic ecology, and anthropogenic impacts including climate change.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 196329). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 196897). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Interaction and Interface I
intro: In this studio course students will focus on issues and design considerations particular to the interface between technology and people. Principles of usability and interaction will be applied to a range of situations including hand-held, web located, environmental and spatial.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 197238). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Digital Fabrication
intro: In this studio course students will be introduced to open source and consumer software as a means to produce objects with digital fabrication tools. Through an iterative making process, students will develop an understanding of design considerations, workflows and new methods of creating using contemporary digital technologies.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 197122). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Coding for Creative Practice
intro: In this studio course students will be introduced to the fundamentals of computer programming as an art and design tool. Students will acquire basic knowledge and fundamental techniques for the creative uses of coding across a range of art and design contexts.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 197119). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis Doctor of Social Work
intro: This course requires the student to undertake research and produce a doctoral thesis for examination.
description: Doctoral Criminology paper (240 credits, 179930). Prerequisite study in Criminology is required. Available at: Auckland. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

description: Honours paper (15 credits, 197442). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: In this studio course students will expand their knowledge and proficiency with specific aspects of human-technology interaction. Students will intensify their focus on areas such as small-screen interface, web-based networking, touch control, reactive environments and spatial interaction.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 197338). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Internship
intro: In this course, and in partnership with the course coordinator, the student will identify an academic researcher, external group, mentor or professional to exercise and develop their creative practice while working within a direct collaborative relationship.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 197383). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1 & S2 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Aquatic Ecology
intro: This course presents an historical and theoretical perspective to the study of freshwaters, building on the general introduction in 196.313. Topics covered include stream ecosystem structure and function, aquatic macrophytes, plankton, freshwater fish, disturbance and land use impacts, and the implications of the Resource Management Act to aquatic ecology.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 196712). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Service and Experience Design
intro: In this studio course students will learn and apply human-centred design processes to create a multi-dimensional response to a specific brief. Using a human-centred approach, students will negotiate the opportunities and tensions inherent in the design of services and experiences, offering original solutions that explore current paradigms (including the dynamic interplay of production and consumption).
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 197379). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Conservation Science
intro: The principles and practice of conservation and management of populations. Ecological principles are applied to the sustainable use of natural resources including conservation of threatened species and fisheries management. The practical component includes statistical analysis and modelling. There is a compulsory field trip day.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 196315). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Entomology
intro: Insect diversity, anatomy, physiology, behaviour, plant-insect relationships, biosecurity, and integrated pest management in agriculture, horticulture and forestry. Insect identification and curation skills are taught in the laboratories. An insect collection is required.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 199310). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: A study of the history of political thought from antiquity to the nineteenth century.
description: Year 2 Classics paper (15 credits, 200215). Prerequisite study in Classics is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Explorations in Digital Asset Creation
intro:
Learn foundational skills for creating digital 3d assets.  In this course, you will be guided through hands-on creative exercises to develop the skills and knowledge required to produce digital assets that can then be inputted into various software for object-building, world-building and character building across a variety of digital platforms.  The approaches and outputs in this course will benefit any creative arts students working in digital technologies. 

The core 3D skills you will gain include:
- Understanding the creative process to make 3D assets
- UV mapping – initial process of adding texture/colour to 3D work
- Creating textures/colour
- Digital lighting
- Preparing your assets for animation

Explorations in Digital Assets can be used to take your creativity to the next level - especially when paired with other Exploration and studio courses.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 198124). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: New Zealand National Security
intro: A study of security theory and the domestic and international factors affecting New Zealand's strategic environment.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 294701). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Intelligence in the Security Environment
intro: This course provides a critical examination of intelligence focusing on the security environment. It looks at contemporary global and New Zealand-specific security risks, the intelligence cycle, and intelligence models that collect and analyse information to produce intelligence products that inform  decision making.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 294272). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

description: Honours paper (45 credits, 293455). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: In this course students produce a creative work through practical, critical and technical enquiry.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (45 credits, 293812). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Integrated Design Research and Development
intro: Using integrated design strategies, explore and apply methods, processes and practices through a research investigation that incorporates diverse design approaches. Students will undertake advanced research on a project of their choice.
description: Honours paper (30 credits, 293453). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Biosecurity
intro: This course exposes students to a range of contemporary biosecurity issues and practices and incorporates a preliminary understanding of conceptual approaches to the management of pest and diseases.

title: Assessment and Evaluation in Tertiary Education
intro: This course offers an in-depth examination of principles, theories and practices underpinning diverse assessment tools, models and approaches in tertiary contexts to facilitate student learning. The course will enable participants to examine assessment policy in context, and create assessment approaches within their practice.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 273785). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Practicum in Te Kaupapa o Te Kōhanga Reo
intro: Professional and field-based practice in the area of Te Kōhanga Reo.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 249759). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: War and Society
intro: An examination of the nature of war and its impact on society. The course explains how contemporary armed conflict and the use of force affects the world order, people and their communities, human behaviour, culture, and the environment.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 294115). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Contextualising Creative Enterprise Practice Part 1
intro: An exploration of advanced critical and enterprise frameworks, and ways they may be applied to creative practice. Students work towards a proposal of a plan to take their Major Project to market, along with a text through which their Major Project is critically framed.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 293731). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: Realise and manifest a research-led independent project that communicates integrated design strategies and methods.

description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 292101). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Foundation Sound and Image
intro: In this course students will be introduced to working in a creative studio environment, learning techniques and processes which will enable them to create their own video project. Students will be guided to set their own brief, developing initially from their own soundtrack and storyboard plan.
description: Foundation paper (15 credits, 293002). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: This capstone internship involves extensive work within a retail business to analyse and critique current practices against retail and business theories and international best practice.
description: Year 3 paper (30 credits, 290301). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Production
intro: Students will produce and finalise an advanced work demonstrating highly executed production standards informed by their research. Students will be required to demonstrate and sustain an professional creative and technical level of proficiency together with professional production management of workloads and deadlines.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 289803). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Production Planning and Development
intro: Students gain a professional knowledge and develop expertise in a range of creative research practices, tools and techniques for organising large scale productions. Students will apply these skills toward planning and communicating an Advanced Production Project.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 289701). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.
