title: Algorithms and Data Structures
intro: An introduction to the analysis and implementation of algorithms and data structures including linear data structures, trees, graphs, hash tables, searching algorithms, sorting algorithms, optimization problems and complexity analysis. The course includes a significant practical component covering the implementation and application of important data structures and algorithms.
description: Year 2 Computer Science paper (15 credits, 159201). Prerequisite study in Computer Science is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Film Studies
intro: This course engages students with a variety of critical and theoretical approaches to cinema, mapping their development across history and examining their usefulness in understanding the social, cultural, economic, political and aesthetic significance of film.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 154702). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Embedded Programming
intro: A study of embedded programming using low-level programming languages. The course covers the hardware architecture of an embedded system and the techniques needed to write programs for simple peripherals such as switches, LEDs, timers and accelerometers.
description: Year 2 Computer Science paper (15 credits, 159236). Prerequisite study in Computer Science is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Software Engineering Capstone Project
intro: Management of the software engineering process. A group project will form a significant component of this course.
description: Year 3 Computer Science paper (15 credits, 159356). Prerequisite study in Computer Science is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Programming Project
intro: A capstone programming project for students in their final year of study. Students will develop a piece of software or conduct a research project under the supervision of an academic staff member. Students must produce a written technical report and give an oral presentation demonstrating their work.
description: Year 3 Computer Science paper (15 credits, 159333). Prerequisite study in Computer Science is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Programming Language Paradigms
intro: An introduction to different programming paradigms with a focus on the functional paradigm – lists, recursion, lazy-evaluation, higher-order functions, currying and lambda expressions.  The course includes a significant practical component designing and implementing functional solutions to problems using both pure and multi-paradigm languages.
description: Year 2 Computer Science paper (15 credits, 159272). Prerequisite study in Computer Science is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Programming for Engineering and Technology
intro: This course provides an introduction to computer programming for Technology and Engineering disciplines. The language is C++ which is a "real world" language heavily used in games, real-time applications and the control of devices. The course guides students through all the steps needed to write, compile and debug simple C++ programs with lots of practical examples. No previous programming experience is required.
description: Year 1 Computer Science paper (15 credits, 159100). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Programming for Computer Graphics
intro: An introduction to the programming principles, methods and techniques of computer graphics. The course covers general graphics methods and techniques, graphics transformations, drawing algorithms, 3D rendering and animation. These are implemented using basic approaches as well as modern shader-based pipelines.
description: Year 2 Computer Science paper (15 credits, 159235). Prerequisite study in Computer Science is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Object-Oriented Programming
intro: An introduction to the principles of object-oriented programming. This course covers both object-oriented programming techniques and their application to program design including classes, objects and their lifecycle, abstraction, encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism and generic programming, as well as some advanced programming features. The course includes a significant practical component involving designing and implementing object-oriented programs in Java and C++.”
description: Year 2 Computer Science paper (15 credits, 159234). Prerequisite study in Computer Science is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Data Science - Making Sense of Data
intro: A study of the science of drawing knowledge and insights from data, including the concepts and techniques of data mining, machine learning and natural language processing. The course covers both theoretical and practical aspects using a range of software tools and algorithms.
description: Postgraduate Information Systems paper (15 credits, 158755). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Information Technology Professional Project
intro: Candidates will undertake a practical and/or research IT project including some or all of the following: requirements engineering, product modeling, user interface design and evaluation, systems security, software testing, and quality assurance.  This may be conducted either individually or in groups and assessment may be in a variety of ways including written and oral presentations and software demonstrations.
description: Postgraduate Research Information Systems paper (60 credits, 158888). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Mobile Application Development
intro: The course covers many practical aspects of designing, creating, testing and publishing mobile applications. Students will develop mobile applications using a real software development toolkit and have the option of publishing their applications to an app marketplace.
description: Year 3 Computer Science paper (15 credits, 159336). Prerequisite study in Computer Science is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: Students begin the transition to registered nurse practice and meet Nursing Council of New Zealand competencies.
description: Year 3 paper (45 credits, 168374). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Psychological Interventions in Mental Health
intro: This practical course enhances students' clinical nursing practice through the teaching of advanced treatment planning and evidence-based mental health interventions. Key concepts and skills are drawn from psychological interventions such as Motivational Interviewing, Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavioural Therapy (DBT), and Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT). Emphasis is also placed on reflective practice and clinician self-care.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 168720). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Physiology and Pathophysiology
intro: Translational and clinically relevant physiological and pathophysiological concepts as they apply to nursing practice are examined. Selected signs/symptoms are considered in terms of physiological processes at biochemical, cellular and functional levels. The emphasis is on normal physiological processes and their possible subsequent development to pathophysiological phenomena.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 168733). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Evidence-Based Practice
intro: Evidence based practice (EBP) is an approach to problem solving and service provision based on the integration of “best evidence” with expert opinion and client/consumer perspectives. Students will develop EBP skills needed to critically evaluate information available from research findings, professional consensus statements and other sources of information, as well as communicating the results.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 168713). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: The multidimensional aspects of pain management including physiological and psychological components are examined.  This knowledge is applied to refine nursing interventions in order to manage the pain experienced by people with acute or chronic ill-health conditions and those receiving palliative care.

intro: Leadership in professional practice is explored within the broader context of health care. The course critically examines the political, legislative, economic, ethical and cultural issues influencing nursing leadership and the provision of health services within particular scopes of practice.

It is an applied course with an emphasis on software construction using basic algorithmic methods and simple data structures.  The course introduces the C++ programming language and is an essential preparation for advanced programming courses.
description: Year 1 Computer Science paper (15 credits, 159101). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: Security and privacy are important features of information systems, in particular with the case of free access, as in Web-based services or E-commerce systems.  The goal is to restrict the access of information to legitimate users only.  For this purpose techniques from cryptography and information theory have to be studied.
description: Postgraduate Information Systems paper (15 credits, 158738). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

It examines the commercial pressures which promote methodologies such as Agile and the theoretical frameworks which underpin a desirable methodology. Practical assignments will use appropriate software environments.

It will emphasise development processes that result in interfaces that provide a superior user experience. Examples of real-life interfaces, from sources such as desktop, multimedia, web, novel, and mobile technologies will be used. There will be a significant practical work component.

The constitution of the pre-thesis studio will consist of a catalogue of works presented for exhibition together with a presentation of ideas both in situ and through a formal public presentation.
description: Postgraduate paper (120 credits, 150720). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Rangahau Whakairo: Pre-Thesis Practicum (Part II)
description: Postgraduate paper (60 credits, 150726). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Theory and Process in Educational Leadership
intro: A critical examination of discourses, policies and practices of educational governance, leadership and reform.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 259772). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Educational Leadership in Action
intro: A study of leadership in action including reflective practice, and professional and curriculum leadership, based on selected organisational and policy contexts.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 259771). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Ako: Psychology of Learning and Teaching
intro: A study of learning and teaching processes within an ecological and developmental framework emphasising what learners bring to their learning and factors that support and hinder engagement. A focus on the use of psychological tools for learning/teaching (use of language, literacy and numeracy) allows in depth consideration of complex higher order processes and how they can be developed.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 256701). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Learning English as Another Language
intro: A specialised study of the skills and processes involved in learning English as another language (ESOL) and the factors affecting success. Consideration will be given to the variety of learners in New Zealand; the interrelationship between language, culture and world-view; current theories on second language learning; and the analysis of the spoken and written language of new learners of English.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 257375). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Positive Learning Environments
intro: This course provides in depth exploration of the strategies and interventions for maintaining positive learning environments that support effective school engagement and achievement.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 256304). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Teaching Learners of English as Another Language
intro: A reflective approach to developing effective skills in the teaching of English as another language (TESOL), including assessment, planning, and the design of resource materials. Students will explore strategies for facilitating language learning in a range of specific contexts.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 257370). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Ahurei: The Psychology of Unique Differences
intro: A study of how learners and teachers bring their diversity (defined by sociocultural, medical, developmental, and psychological frameworks) to their learning and teaching. A critical analysis of the way society and government systems defines and responds to diversity is central to this course, as is consideration of how contemporary inclusive education and positive psychology can better support learning for all.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 256702). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Evidence-based practice in educational psychology
intro: Evidence-based practice and application to formal and informal learning contexts. Developing a professional practice portfolio with reference to the Core Competencies of the New Zealand Psychologists’ Board.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (30 credits, 256843). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Flight Instructor Human Factors
intro: Theory and  application of human factor issues in decision-making, interpersonal relations, communication styles and personality characteristics as they affect flight deck performance in the instructional environment.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 190301). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Mānuka Tākoto - Behaviour Challenges
intro: A study of the challenges experienced by teachers, specialists, children and whānau when individual or group behaviours challenge the ability to learn, socialise, retain relationships and impact on culture and identity, and to provide the tools to pick up the challenge for all involved to  address these.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 256707). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Special Topic
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 250704). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Independent Study
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 250310). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Applied Research in Educational Psychology
intro: A supervised and guided research study presented in a research report.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 256847). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Understanding and Addressing Literacy Needs
intro: A critical study of evidence-based practices for identifying and working with learners with literacy learning needs. This includes understanding the learner within a context to respond appropriately. Opportunity is provided for exploration of teaching practices that are effective in enhancing progress and equity.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 258722). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Educational and Developmental Psychology Assessment
intro: Advanced study in contemporary assessment in educational and developmental psychology.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (30 credits, 256854). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Foundations of Literacy Education
intro: An advanced study of the critical language skills and cognitive processes underpinning literacy and language acquisition. Consideration is given to distal factors that influence literacy and language acquisition, along with relevant policy and foundational documents. Implications for effective literacy practices are critically examined through current literature.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 258720). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Politics of Education
intro: This course considers the politics of education in Aotearoa New Zealand, using current social issues and education policy reforms to examine role of education in a democracy.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 254340). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Whanaungatanga: Psychology of Relationships in Ako
intro: A study into space for relationships and relationship trust that underpin development and learning. The role of tikanga and psychological approaches that rely on relationships, such as interviewing, counselling, mediation, conflict resolution are explored, along with the multi-disciplinary situations that are inherent in educational and developmental psychology.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 256705). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Learning in the Digital World
intro: This course is an exploration of what it means to be a learner in digital social worlds.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 254200). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Educational Psychology
intro: An examination of the contribution of psychology to an understanding of educational processes with a focus on the learner, learning processes and instruction. Issues of theory, research and application will be studied in a variety of educational situations.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 256201). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Special Topic
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 250318). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Leadership in Educational Psychology
intro: Professional Leadership and application of discipline knowledge, scholarship and research in supervised professional practice in educational and developmental psychology
description: Postgraduate Research paper (30 credits, 256846). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Competencies for Educational Psychologists
intro: Advanced study and supervised professional practice in educational and developmental psychology with a focus on competencies and critical professional reflection.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (30 credits, 256844). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Learning Pathways
intro: Bespoke learning modules for professional educational practice
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 254766). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: He Taiao Ako: Psychology of Educational Settings
intro: A study of formal and informal educational settings, beginning with the teaching-learning relationship that is parenting, and moving through formal child and adolescent educational contexts into adult education. The psychological frameworks that help us understand the complexity of these settings and the interactions that occur within them are examined, along with how best to approach professional work in these settings.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 256703). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Critical Theories of Education
intro: Education as an everyday practice is the focus for critical theories of education. This course explores ways education has been theorized as a practice of liberation as well as a practice of oppression. Students will use these concepts to examine the role of education in shaping the social structure of Aotearoa New Zealand.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 254304). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Pacific Peoples and Education in New Zealand
intro: A critical examination of current issues in education which affect Pacific Islands peoples in New Zealand. Students will consider the place of Pacific Islands peoples in New Zealand society and design strategies for meeting their educational needs within specified professional fields of interest.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 254744). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Educational and Developmental Psychology Practice
intro: Advanced study of professional practice in educational and developmental psychology. Principles, frameworks, competencies, attitudes and dispositions that underpin professional practice are critiqued and developed. Particular attention is given to bicultural issues, professional ethics, professional leadership, accountability and reflective practice.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (30 credits, 256855). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Whakapiki: Psychology of Change and Development
intro: A study of models and approaches that support the instigation and facilitation of change at the system, classroom, home, or individual levels. Contemporary practice in talking therapies, motivational interviewing, and brief therapy are explored and consideration is also given to specific issues such as disaster response, the impacts of trauma, principles of psychological recovery and the development of resilience.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 256706). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Development in Counselling II
intro: A continuation and integration of personal and professional development undertaken in Professional Development I whilst pursuing field-based practice and campus-based workshops.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 253762). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Industrial Microbiology
intro: An industry focused course in microbiology with particular reference to the importance of microorganisms and their application in selected industries. This programme of study examines the growth and control of industrially important microorganisms, the role of microbes in the production of food products, their application in both waste treatment and in industrial fermentation, and the role of microbes in the health sector. A laboratory course.
description: Year 2 Agriculture paper (15 credits, 280201). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Elementary Meat Mathematics
intro: Elementary mathematics applicable to the operation of a meat processing plant including mathematical principles as linear equations and statistics.
description: Foundation Agriculture paper (15 credits, 280017). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Automation & New Technology in the Meat Industry
intro: Introduction to automation, new technology and product development methods in the international meat industry including consideration of drivers and constraints and case studies. Technologies covered will include but are not limited to: sensors and machine vision, data capture, traceability, factory farming, biotechnology, cultured meats, and advances in packaging, preservation, decontamination.
description: Year 1 Agriculture paper (15 credits, 280107). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Signals and Systems
intro: A study of signals and linear systems and their applications in modern engineering. Representation of continuous and discrete time signals and systems in the time and frequency domains. Stability, feedback, sampling and aliasing. Introduction to design of analogue and digital filters. Provides a foundation for later studies in control engineering, advanced signal processing and communications.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 281272). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Process Control
intro: Practical process control techniques including tuning single proportional integral derivative controllers, choosing appropriate control loops for a processing plant and measurement instrumentation. An overview of advanced control techniques. Programmable logical controllers. A practical course.
description: Postgraduate Agriculture paper (15 credits, 280702). Prerequisite study in Agriculture is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Research Methods paper (60 credits, 279816). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Theorising Today’s Social Policy for the Future
intro: A critical examination of social policy and its current developments. Utilising conceptual tools and new theories of social policy including sustainability, decolonisation and crisis theory to analyse social policy.
description: Postgraduate Research Methods paper (30 credits, 279703). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Social Policy and Government
intro: An exploration of government systems, policy and legislative making processes with relevance to social policy.
description: Year 2 Research Methods paper (15 credits, 279203). Prerequisite study in Research Methods is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Social Policy: An Introduction
intro: A foundational knowledge of social policy, providing a broad introduction introducing students to the history of social policy in Aotearoa New Zealand and key theories and concepts informing the development of social policy.
description: Year 1 Research Methods paper (15 credits, 279101). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Development in Childhood
intro: An examination of physical, cognitive and psychosocial development during infancy and childhood in diverse contexts.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 275203). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Research Methods paper (60 credits, 279802). Prerequisite study in Research Methods is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Social Policy
description: Doctoral Research Methods paper (120 credits, 279900). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

description: Postgraduate Research Methods paper (60 credits, 279798). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Quality Assurance for the Meat Industry
intro: Food safety from farm to the consumer’s plate will be examined using methods for product and process control and improvement including, HACCP, risk management, ante- and post-mortem inspection, auditing principles, general food safety principles and occupational safety and health. Consideration will also be given to current legislation and OMAR requirements that impact on the meat processing industry.
description: Year 1 Agriculture paper (15 credits, 280105). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Reaction Technologies and Process Modelling
intro: A systematic approach to modelling processing operations in terms of heat, mass and momentum transfer. Modelling reactions and reactor systems to predict the progress of reactions in food preservation and processing operations, chemical and enzymatic catalysis, and biochemical reaction systems. A laboratory course.
description: Year 3 Agriculture paper (15 credits, 280372). Prerequisite study in Agriculture is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Process Improvement
intro:
This course critically appraises key industrial processes by establishing
their core reaction engineering and thermodynamics, identifying theoretical
limits in terms of material and heat balances across product life cycles and
by defining their current effectiveness. ‘Potential for further improvement’ is
evaluated, such as new emerging competitor processes, alternative reaction
pathways, process intensification concepts, hazard mitigation, or emerging life
cycle custodianship practises.
description: Postgraduate Agriculture paper (15 credits, 280721). Prerequisite study in Agriculture is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Added-Value Processing of Meat and Meat Products
intro: The chemistry, microbiology and processing of the production of a wide range of added value meat products will be covered in the course.  Topics include restructured meat technologies, hamburger manufacture, ready-to-eat meals, packaging, canned products, surimi processing, bone and mechanically recovered meat processing, tallow refining, fine chemical processing, additive properties and uses, yields and product costing.
description: Year 1 Agriculture paper (15 credits, 280106). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Elementary Meat Science
intro: Elementary chemistry, biology and physics as they apply to meat science. Atomic and molecular structure, bonding, chemical equilibria, acids and bases as well as an introduction to organic chemistry. Biological systems including single and multicellular organisms. The study of force, work, energy, heat, light and electricity.
description: Foundation Agriculture paper (30 credits, 280016). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Social Policy: Political Theories and Approaches
intro: Exploring political theories and conceptual tools for analysing policy-making and the processes for engaging with the policy process in Aotearoa.
description: Year 3 Research Methods paper (15 credits, 279301). Prerequisite study in Research Methods is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Curriculum and Pedagogy A (Primary)
intro: This course introduces students to the theory and practice of curriculum, pedagogy and assessment in the context of primary school settings in Aotearoa New Zealand guided by commitment to Te Tiriti o Waitangi and tangata whenuatanga.
description: Honours Education paper (30 credits, 278412). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report Social Policy (60)
description: Postgraduate Research Research Methods paper (60 credits, 279873). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Policy Research and Evaluation
intro: An examination of the relationship between policy and research and the concepts, techniques and issues involved in policy and programme evaluation. The course includes practical experience in evaluation research.
description: Year 3 Research Methods paper (15 credits, 279302). Prerequisite study in Research Methods is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Bioseparation and Purification Processes
intro: The principles and practice of bioseparations and purification processes. The following unit operations will be included: distillation, leaching, liquid/liquid extraction, protein fractionation, flocculation and chromatographic separations. A laboratory course.
description: Year 3 Agriculture paper (15 credits, 280304). Prerequisite study in Agriculture is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Research Methods paper (60 credits, 279817). Prerequisite study in Research Methods is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Co-products Derived from Meat Animals
intro: Technology including the applied microbiology and chemistry involved in the processing of hides and pelts to the finished leather stage, inedible and edible processing of blood, foetal blood and co-products, casings, specialised chemicals, collagen and yields for the various co-product options.
description: Year 1 Agriculture paper (15 credits, 280104). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Food Process Engineering Operations
intro: The application of engineering principles to operations used in the food or chemical processing industries.  Operations such as evaporation,  drying , membrane technologies, refrigeration and process cooling systems will be used as examples of how the underlying principles of thermodynamics,  conservation and transport of heat, mass and momentum can be used to select, design and optimise industrial processes.
description: Year 3 Agriculture paper (15 credits, 280371). Prerequisite study in Agriculture is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Preparation and Preservation of Fresh Meat
intro: The microbiology, biochemistry and technology involved in the production of fresh and frozen meat and meat products as well as ways of increasing their chilled and frozen shelf life.  Includes a review of hazardous microbes carried by fresh meat and meat products and their control in a modern processing facility.
description: Year 1 Agriculture paper (15 credits, 280102). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Meat Plant Services and Utilities
intro: Theory and application of services into the meat plant, including refrigeration, steam, hot water, air and the supply of potable water and the handling of wastewater as well as other waste streams. Issues such as waste minimisation, legislation environmental management, and resource optimisation will also be considered.
description: Year 1 Agriculture paper (15 credits, 280103). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Practice 1 (Primary)
intro: This course provides an introduction to professional practice in primary school settings in Aotearoa New Zealand, through observation, teaching, critical self-reflection and evaluation of practice.
description: Honours Education paper (30 credits, 278410). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (45 credits, 233872). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Kupu Tā - Te Pānui Tuhituhi i Te Kura Kaupapa Māori III
intro: He āta rangahau i ngā whakaakoranga, i ngā aromatawai, i ngā whakahaere rerekē noa e hāngai ana ki te pai o te ako i te reo pānui, reo tuhituhi ki ngā ākonga rerenga kē i tētahi taiao Māori.    An in-depth study of differentiated instruction, assessment and management as it applies to effective practice of teaching literacy skills for diverse learners, within a Māori context.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 268322). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Tikanga Pāngarau I
intro: Ka tīmata te ako i ngā whakamārama me ngā tukanga o te akoako pāngarau ki tā te Māori titiro, ki tā te Pākehā titiro, me tōna whakamahinga ki te reo Māori.    An introduction to the processes of numeracy learning in te ao Māori and western contexts and its application to numeracy learning in te reo Māori.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 268120). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Tikanga Pāngarau II
intro: Ka whakawhanakehia ētahi mātauranga pāngarau Motuhake me ngā ariā pāngarau hei kawe i te pāngarau i roto i te ako reo Māori.     Development of specific mathematical concepts and pedagogical knowledge for teaching of mathematics in the Māori medium.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 268220). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Tikanga Pāngarau III
intro: Ka āta rangahau i te mātauranga pāngarau me ngā ariā pāngarau me te āta aro ki te tika o te whakaako i te pāngarau i ngā ako Māori.    Advanced study of mathematical concepts and pedagogical content knowledge for primary teachers with a focus on effective pedagogical practices in Māori medium settings.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 268320). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Puanga
intro: He āta mātakitaki, he tauriterite i ngā kawa me ngā tikanga o tauiwi, ki ērā o ngā iwi taketake hei aromatawai i ngā pātanga o tā tauiwi aro ki te mātauranga me ngā pūnaha mātauranga. An examination and comparison of western perspectives of knowledge with Māori and indigenous world views in order to assess the implications of contemporary western perspectives on education and educational systems.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 268310). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Kupu Tā I - Pānui Tuhituhi i Te Kura Kaupapa
intro: He tīmatanga kia mārama ki ngā tukanga o te ako i te pānui ki tā te Māori, ki tā tauiwi, me tōna whakamahinga ki Te Reo Māori. An introduction to the processes of literacy learning in Te Ao Māori and western contexts and their application to literacy learning in Te Reo Māori.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 268121). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Kupu Tā II - Te Pānui Tuhituhi i Te Kura Kaupapa Māori
intro: He whakawhanake i ōna māramatanga ki ngā kawenga mō te reorua, te reo-ā-tini ki tā te ao Māori rangahau mō te whakaako i te kupu tā, arā ko te reo ā-pānui, ā-tuhituhi i roto i te Reo Rangatira. Kia hāngai ki te taiao o Te Aho Matua.    Development of an understanding of the processes for bi-lingual, multi-lingual literacy learning in Te Ao Māori and its research-based application to literacy teaching in Te Reo Māori.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 268222). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Hā
intro: He titiro ki te āhua o te tū i runga i te marae, me te ako i ngā tikanga me ōna reo.      An introduction to the knowledge, language and customary practices pertaining to the elements of marae as a context for education.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 268110). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Tiringa
intro: He whakawhānui i te mātauranga i te reo me ngā tikanga o te marae hei ako, hei whakaako i ēnei mātauranga.  He āta titiro kia mārama te ahurei o te tamaiti me tōna whānau i ngā taiao ako. Further development of the knowledge, language and customary practices pertaining to the marae as a particular context for educational exchange and knowledge transfer. There is an emphasis on the uniqueness of the individual child and their whānau within diverse learning settings.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 268211). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: An advanced realisation of proposed research, subsequent analysis and scholarly report.

intro: A study of Māori research frameworks, philosophies and processes and an examination of their contribution to the growing body of indigenous scholarship as relevant to educational settings.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 267741). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Inquiry

description: Postgraduate Research paper (45 credits, 267861). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Specialist Teaching Professional Inquiry
intro: An advanced analysis of a professional practice in an area of specialist teaching to inquire into the impact of  that practice. Addresses the inquiry skills fundamental to quality culturally responsive professional thinking and action in an area of specialist teaching.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (45 credits, 267875). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Taiao mō Te Aho Matua o ngā Kura Kaupapa Māori II
intro: Ka ako te tauira ki te whakariterite i te taiao ako mā te whakamahi i ngā akoaranga katoa o te tau tuatahi.  Me whakawhanake te ākonga i ngā pukenga whakahaere, i ngā rautaki whakaako, i ngā mahinga aromatawai ki tā Te Aho Matua tauira.    Students will learn to prepare the learning environment by drawing on their learnings from the first year.  Using Te Aho Matua as a framework, students develop management and pedagogical strategies and assessment practices.
description: Year 2 paper (30 credits, 268207). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Informed Practice in Early Years Education
intro: This course offers a critical study of the notions of quality in learning settings from infancy through to early school years. Content focuses on how to engage with and interpret different forms of evidence, including research, to inform practice.  Opportunity is provided for exploration of key issues and implications for pedagogical leadership.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 265736). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Curriculum and Pedagogy A (ECE)
intro: This course introduces students to the theory and practice of curriculum, pedagogy and assessment in the context of early childhood education settings in Aotearoa New Zealand guided by commitment to Te Tiriti o Waitangi and tangata whenuatanga.
description: Honours paper (30 credits, 265412). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Practice 2 (ECE)
intro: This course provides further development of professional practice in early childhood education settings in Aotearoa New Zealand, through observation, teaching, critical self-reflection and evaluation of practice.
description: Honours paper (30 credits, 265411). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Practice 1 (ECE)
intro: This course provides an introduction to professional practice in early childhood education settings in Aotearoa New Zealand, through observation, teaching, critical self-reflection and evaluation of practice.

description: Postgraduate Strategic Studies paper (30 credits, 263704). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Assessment and Learning

description: Year 3 Strategic Studies paper (15 credits, 263331). Prerequisite study in Strategic Studies is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: How People Learn
intro: An examination of how people learn in formal and informal settings
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 264200). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Teaching as an Act of Influencing Others
intro: An exploration of the ethics, principles, and key processes of everyday teaching and learning as a basic form of social interaction, giving students the opportunity to explore teaching and learning across the lifespan.
description: Year 2 Strategic Studies paper (15 credits, 263200). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Learning and Teaching 2
intro: This course uses a research informed, evidence-based approach to examine how learning happens in its social, cultural and historical context and what this means for effective teaching for all learners. The multiple factors shaping identity will be explored, and students will develop awareness of, and critically reflect on, diverse perspectives regarding identity.
description: Honours Strategic Studies paper (15 credits, 263401). Prerequisite study in Strategic Studies is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Teaching and Learning in Everyday Settings
intro: A study of informal teaching and learning practices in a workplace, volunteer, leisure, cultural or community organisation setting. Focus is on the application of everyday teaching and learning theory in a real community context where access is self-negotiated by the student.
description: Year 3 Strategic Studies paper (15 credits, 263315). Prerequisite study in Strategic Studies is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Nga Miro Whakaaturanga: Thesis Practicum
intro: An individually supervised and approved study involving research into Māori or indigenous visual culture to realise an individually conceived body of work that allows for the design and/or coordination of an exhibition, together with an exhibition report that resolves research and practice, or a database that contextualises the body of work within the student's personal oeuvre.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (120 credits, 150821). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Sociologically Imagining Education
intro: This course explores the sociology of education by critically inquiring into the relationship between personal experience, education and society. Students will be introduced to primary sociological concepts like race, class and gender to critically reflect on education.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 254203). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Teaching and Learning with Pasifika Peoples in NZ
intro: An examination of the educational experiences of Pasifika peoples in New Zealand in formal and informal settings both in the past and present with a focus on effective teaching and learning practices.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 254337). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Philosophy of Education
intro: This course explores the philosophical complexities of education in its personal, institutional and social dimensions. Students will be introduced to some of the major debates in philosophy of education and engage in the critical and continual practice of applying philosophy across the many contexts of education.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 254201). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Health and Safety Auditing
intro: This course examines issues relating to measuring safety performance in organisations and in health and safety auditing in particular. Emphasis is placed on the use of the commonly utilised management systems audit promoted by the ACC, the Accredited Employer Programme (AEP) audit system. The course also examines other audit frameworks, and critically examines the role of auditing in safety performance evaluation.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 251370). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Development in Counselling I
intro: The development of attitudes and skills for effective counselling practice, informed by current research and undertaken in the context of campus-based workshops and field-based supervised practice.
description: Postgraduate paper (45 credits, 253761). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Professional and Ethical Practice in Education and Health Contexts
intro: An introduction to the practice of professionals working with individuals and families who have multicultural backgrounds and diverse needs within education and health settings.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 254102). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Sleep, Health and Performance
intro: This course introduces fundamental concepts in sleep and circadian science, emphasising their vital importance in a person’s overall health and functioning. It explores the role of environmental, personal and social factors in sleep health and circadian health. The course examines relationships between sleep loss and circadian disruption, and mental and physical health and neuro-behavioural performance. It explores the challenges for sleep and the circadian system that are created by shift work.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 252301). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fatigue Risk Management for Occupational Health and Safety
intro: The application of sleep and circadian science when considering fatigue risk management, shift work, and occupational health and safety. An examination of approaches to fatigue management, including fatigue risk management systems.  Consideration of the regulatory requirements for the management of fatigue to ensure occupational health and safety.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 252702). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Family and Couples Counselling
intro: A study of the theoretical bases of family and couples counselling.  Attention is given to the concepts of 'family' and 'couple' and to systems theory.  The processes of counselling families and couples are examined with regard to various approaches.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 253754). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Guidance in Education
intro: A study of guidance in educational settings. Attention is given to the philosophical foundations of guidance, its social implications, organisation and roles. Intervention procedures for individuals and groups are examined.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 253753). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

The course will explore the complex relationship between health and safety, factors in the working environment affecting health and safety and systems intended to regulate and manage the working environment.

title: Quantitative Methods in Public Health
intro: The course provides advanced training in the selection and application of quantitative methods in public health. Topics will include: the analysis of national, regional and local health data systems, coding of disease and injury, survey design and analysis, sampling and questionnaire development, critical evaluation of statistic literature, bivariate and multivariate methods for analysing continuous and discrete public health data, and privacy and ethical considerations in the storage and analysis of health data. The course will involve using the software packages such as SAS and Stata as well as online statistical analysis software.
