title: Principles of Measurement and Estimation
intro: The course covers the principles of measurement of building works. Students will integrate and apply preliminary and detailed estimating, and cost planning principles to make cost-related decisions.
description: Year 2 Creative Writing paper (15 credits, 218203). Prerequisite study in Creative Writing is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Planning and Digital Built Environment for Quantity Surveyors
intro: Students develop construction project-planning skills and apply these to the production of cost-related information for decision-making. Students will cover practical application of cost information to the digital approach to and digital simulation of design, construction and operation of buildings.
description: Year 2 Creative Writing paper (15 credits, 218226). Prerequisite study in Creative Writing is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Facilities Management for Construction Managers
intro: The course covers the concepts and principles of managing built facilities. It covers strategic aspects of facilities management, space planning management, support services, building performance, and sustainability. Review of software applications in facility management to also enable 6D digital asset management.
description: Year 3 Creative Writing paper (15 credits, 218329). Prerequisite study in Creative Writing is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Social Interventions Through Design
intro: In this studio course students will establish a critical position through theme based experimental projects exploring the intersection between diverse design practices. Students will work collaboratively and interdisciplinary to explore and combine unconventional communication design materials, methods and practices to creative interventions in public spaces.
description: Honours paper (15 credits, 197471). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Sport Communication
intro: An examination of the theoretical background of sport communication, which includes an application of skills and principles within the topic.
description: Year 2 English Literature paper (15 credits, 219230). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Media Law and Ethics
intro: Students learn how to publish safely by navigating the legal and ethical challenges of writing and publishing in today’s digital age.
description: Year 3 English Literature paper (15 credits, 219335). Prerequisite study in English Literature is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: International and Intercultural Communication
intro: This course uses communication theory and research to explore ways of developing cross-cultural effectiveness, with an emphasis on communication in organisational settings.
description: Postgraduate English Literature paper (30 credits, 219704). Prerequisite study in English Literature is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Dissertation
intro: A detailed examination of a specific researchable topic within the field of construction. The course provides the opportunity for the student to demonstrate independence and originality to solve a construction-related problem requiring analytical, design and experimental effort.
description: Postgraduate Research Creative Writing paper (60 credits, 218820). Prerequisite study in Creative Writing is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Media Skills
intro: Understanding and working with the news media combining practical skills with critical and ethical perspectives on media use.  Skills include media strategy, writing media releases, broadcast interviewing and running a media conference.
description: Year 1 English Literature paper (15 credits, 219101). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Communication Internship
intro: This course provides students with a period of workplace experience with knowledge of communication concepts and terminology gained throughout their degree, in a series of analytical assessments.
description: Year 3 English Literature paper (15 credits, 219311). Prerequisite study in English Literature is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Intercultural Communication
intro: This course compares the communication behaviours of different cultures. Students will develop skills that help them analyse and address intercultural conflicts and issues, and gain skills that allow them to communicate effectively in intercultural contexts.
description: Year 3 English Literature paper (15 credits, 219304). Prerequisite study in English Literature is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised independent research in the built environment resulting in a research thesis.
description: Postgraduate Research Creative Writing paper (45 credits, 218828). Prerequisite study in Creative Writing is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Construction Financial Administration
intro: Post- contract financial administration of construction projects including construction procurement and tendering, interim payments, variations, claims, and final accounts.
description: Postgraduate Creative Writing paper (15 credits, 218781). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Methods in Built Environment
intro: This course develops skills critical to the analysis, synthesis and review of literature; discusses a range of research methodologies; reviews tools and techniques for data collection; examines data analysis techniques and their application in construction research; and outlines the evaluation of research findings.
description: Postgraduate Creative Writing paper (15 credits, 218717). Prerequisite study in Creative Writing is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Global, Investigative and Data Journalism
intro: Explore the diversity of journalism as it is practised around the world, including investigative, undercover and data journalism.
description: Year 3 English Literature paper (15 credits, 219336). Prerequisite study in English Literature is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Current Affairs Journalism
intro: The theory and practice of covering current affairs, including national and local politics and other issue-based and breaking news stories in either print or multi-media formats.
description: Year 3 English Literature paper (15 credits, 219325). Prerequisite study in English Literature is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Perspectives in Business Communication
intro: An exploration of the theory and practice of workplace communication in diverse organisational settings. Theory is linked to practice through the analysis of contemporary case studies.
description: Postgraduate English Literature paper (30 credits, 219703). Available at: Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Construction
description: Doctoral Creative Writing paper (120 credits, 218900). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Organisational Communication
intro: This course draws on current research and theory to examine the communication process, and practices in the context of organising.
description: Year 3 English Literature paper (15 credits, 219303). Prerequisite study in English Literature is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 1 (30 credits)
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Creative Writing paper (30 credits, 218831). Prerequisite study in Creative Writing is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Public Relations Campaigns
intro: An introductory study of public relations and its use to create and maintain relationships between organisations and publics. Emphasis is placed on public relations campaigns and the theory and practice of campaign planning.
description: Year 2 English Literature paper (15 credits, 219209). Prerequisite study in English Literature is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Energy-Efficient Building Design
intro: This course examines approaches to reducing energy consumption in residential and commercial buildings resulting from the design and use of the building and its energy services. The course covers the principles of energy-efficient building design as well as use and interpretation of energy and sustainability audits. Identification of options for reducing energy use through renovation and retrofitting as well as occupant behaviour are also covered.
description: Postgraduate Creative Writing paper (15 credits, 218769). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report (90 credits)
intro: This course provides the opportunity to undertake research work that demonstrates independence and originality to solve a construction-related problem requiring analytical, design and experimental effort.  Students will be guided through a series of supervised tutorials, workshops, presentations, seminars and written reports to document and disseminate their research.
description: Postgraduate Research Creative Writing paper (90 credits, 218811). Prerequisite study in Creative Writing is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Quantity Surveying Practice
intro: Pre contract Quantity Surveying practice including feasibility, preliminary estimates, cost plans, cash flow forecasting, life cycle costing and measurement.
description: Postgraduate Creative Writing paper (15 credits, 218780). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Creative Arts Special Topic 4A
intro: This course focuses on a particular aspect or aspects of creative arts. Offerings change from year to year and the course may not be offered in a particular year.
description: Honours paper (15 credits, 213411). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fine Arts Internship
intro: A negotiated programme of study that provides students with the opportunity to gain experience and practice by working alongside visual arts professionals. Students work through self-directed inquiry and collaboration (as appropriate) towards the development of a project and assigned tasks within the professional environment.  Prior approval by the course Coordinator is required.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 213358). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Fine Arts Research Methods and Practices
intro: An introduction to a range of methods and practices for undertaking research in a fine art context.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 213357). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Creative Arts Project 2C
intro: Engagement with new thinking in creative research through a specific lecturer-led project.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 213258). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Creative Arts Project 3C
intro: Engagement with new thinking in creative practice research through a specific lecturer-led project.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 213356). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fine Arts Studio 3.3: Practice
intro: In this studio course students will extend their art practice. In consultation with lecturers, they will devise, undertake and document a substantial individual or collaborative project. They will explore content, context, methods, and the role of critical dialogue in the production of contemporary art.
description: Year 3 paper (30 credits, 213341). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Drawing the Body II
intro: A creative response to representation of the human body through a prescribed project. Students apply a range of drawing practices and media to the development of individual work.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 213255). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Creative Arts Project 2D
intro: Engagement with new thinking in creative research through a specific lecturer-led project.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 213262). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 203897). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fashion Design Research and Development
intro: Research methods, practices and processes for fashion design and their application through a research design project.
description: Honours paper (30 credits, 212453). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Contemporary Sculpture
intro: In this studio course students will be introduced to aspects of contemporary sculpture with a focus on object making, the exploration of physical materials and applied processes. While working to a brief, students are expected to undertake independent investigations and develop individual responses.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 213151). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fine Arts Studio 1.2: Media and Processes
intro: In this studio course students will be introduced to a range of art practices. Making artworks in relation to a prescribed project, they will explore art strategies and processes, idea generation and creative and analytical thinking.  They will learn to discuss and evaluate their work and the work of their peers.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 213158). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Creative Arts Project 2B
intro: Engagement with new thinking in creative research through a specific lecturer-led project.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 213257). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Contemporary Drawing
intro: In this studio course students will be introduced to the potential for drawing to become more than a traditional practice of representation. The course will focus on expanding students' observation skills, and modes of expression and representation, in an expanded field of contemporary art and design practice.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 213154). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Painting
intro: In this studio course students will be introduced to aspects of contemporary painting focusing on colour, surface and structure and developing an awareness of the diversity of painting mediums and techniques. While working to a brief, students are expected to undertake independent investigations and develop individual responses.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 213150). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Contoured and Knit Apparel
intro: In this studio course students explore the specialised aspects of pattern development and production in the area of contoured and stretch apparel design.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 212309). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Drawing in Practice
intro: In this studio course students will be guided by experts, each with a focus on particular approaches and technologies of drawing. Students will experiment with these different methodologies, and critically reflect on the role of drawing within their own practice.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 213254). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fine Arts Studio 2.3: Practice
intro: In this studio course students will begin to establish an individual art practice. They will respond to a provocation and develop an individual project to explore ideas, processes, context, and critical thinking in the production of contemporary art.
description: Year 2 paper (30 credits, 213241). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fine Arts Studio 1.1: Art Lab
intro: In this studio course students will be introduced to a range of art practices. Making artworks in relation to a prescribed project, they will explore specific techniques and processes, idea generation, and creative analytical thinking.  They will learn to discuss and evaluate their work and the work of their peers.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 213157). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Explorations in Drawing
intro: Explorations in Drawing introduces students to foundational techniques and practices of observational drawing, drawing as thinking, drawing for ideation.  The course guides beginners and skilled practitioners alike through a variety of exercises, introducing students to practices from across the creative arts.  Skills taught in this course will be useful for students in all areas creative practice.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 213155). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Spatial Design Studio Project
intro: Development and realisation of an advanced spatial design project.
description: Honours Economics paper (45 credits, 224455). Prerequisite study in Economics is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Textile Print Project
intro: In this studio course, students explore the design and development of printed textiles for a specific performance, installation or exhibition.
description: Year 3 International Relations paper (15 credits, 223312). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Spatial Design Research and Development
intro: Research methods, processes and practices for Spatial Design and their application through a research project.
description: Honours Economics paper (30 credits, 224453). Prerequisite study in Economics is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Contemporary Wallcoverings
intro: In this studio course students will design and develop collections for wallpaper and wall coverings, with emphasis on creative investigation and forecasting.
description: Year 3 International Relations paper (15 credits, 223301). Prerequisite study in International Relations is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Embroidered Textiles
intro: Students will explore embroidery and advanced technologies for the production of innovative fashion textiles.
description: Honours International Relations paper (15 credits, 223411). Prerequisite study in International Relations is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Sustainable Colouration
intro: Students will explore contemporary design concepts using sustainable dye processing and advanced technology to colour and pattern textiles.
description: Honours International Relations paper (15 credits, 223412). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Design for Display
intro: In this studio course students will investigate concepts for display and design strategies for exhibitions as temporary interventions in public space.
description: Year 2 Economics paper (15 credits, 224205). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Spatial Design Independent Study
intro: Supervised independent student work on a negotiated topic related to spatial design involving the setting of objectives, research and/or project work and the presentation of appropriate written and/or practical outcomes.
description: Honours Economics paper (15 credits, 224495). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Spatial Design 1.1: An Introduction
intro: In this studio course students will be introduced to skills and processes to design physical, temporal, and digital spatial environments. They will explore core spatial design principles of site, time, light, and movement to create a digital spatial design. Students will develop creative and analytical thinking as well as idea generation and visualisation skills for spatial design practice.
description: Year 1 Economics paper (15 credits, 224157). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced News Journalism
intro: Students study the theory and practice of gathering and writing news journalism for publication, including generating news-story ideas, gathering the relevant information, writing these news stories, and publishing them in mainstream media outlets. Students critically reflect on their experience, making links to the relevant scholarly literature.
description: Postgraduate English Literature paper (30 credits, 219712). Available at: Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Psychology and the Changing Nature of Work
intro: This course examines psychological aspects of the changing nature of work. It considers societal and organisational factors driving change and models of both planned and unplanned change at work, illustrating these issues through the use of case studies of change at work and psychologists' accounts of their experiences as change agents.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175748). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Clinical Assessment Skills
intro: This is a workshop based course that requires students to engage in learning activities across a full range of applied skills, of clinical psychology assessment across the lifespan, including interview skills, skills in case conceptualisation and psychological formulation, and skills in the selection, administration, scoring, interpretation and reporting of psychological and neuropsychological tests. Training will also include compulsory attendance at workshops and other training opportunities as they arise.
description: Postgraduate Research Psychology paper (30 credits, 175811). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Health Psychology: The Social Context
intro: A critical examination of health psychological theory and research on how the social context shapes illness experiences, treatment, and outcomes. Emphasis is placed on personal understandings of health and lived experience of illness, and a critical consideration of research methods appropriate for examining these issues.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175743). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Indigenous Psychologies
intro: The course provides foundational content of indigenous psychologies, including the rationale for their inclusion in the discipline of Western Psychology, as well overviewing their application across multiple domains (e.g., health, education, social, policy, etc) in Aotearoa New Zealand specifically, and more generally in other cultural contexts across the globe.
description: Year 1 Psychology paper (15 credits, 175103). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Clinical Intervention Skills
intro: This is a workshop based course that requires students to engage in learning activities across a full range of applied skills, of clinical psychology interventions across the lifespan, including behavioural and cognitive behavioural interventions, family and systems approaches and neurorehabilitation interventions.  Training will also include compulsory attendance at workshops and other training opportunities as they arise.
description: Postgraduate Research Psychology paper (30 credits, 175812). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Available at: Auckland, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Language and Communication in Late Modernity
intro: This course examines the linguistic construction of some key themes that have emerged in late modernity such as globalising panic, emotions and the imperative to feel, the discursive construction of the body, discourse and the late modernity.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 172713). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Psychological Research: Principles of Design
intro: An advanced study of the principles of research design in psychology.  The course will provide a conceptual orientation to the integrated components of the design process.  Through a meta-theoretical framework, various approaches to design will be explored to enable advanced engagement with psychological research from scientific, interpretivist and/or indigenous perspectives.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175738). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Qualitative Methods in Psychology
intro: The course provides advanced understanding and skill development to enable students to undertake qualitative research in psychology.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175750). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Health Psychology Practicum
intro: Practical experience of at least 120 hours working in a community health agency under supervision.  Students will participate in the design, implementation or evaluation of a health intervention, and critically reflect on their practice in relation to relevant theories and methods.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175749). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Understanding Mental Distress
intro: This course builds on undergraduate study of clinical psychology to provide an advanced understanding of mental distress across the lifespan. The focus is on recognising the most common experiences of mental distress and understanding their etiology using evidence-based theoretical frameworks, emphasising psychological approaches. Attention is given to recovery and lived experience perspectives, and contextual and cultural influences on understanding the experience of mental distress. A case study approach is taken to provide opportunities for applied learning.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175781). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Organisational Psychology
intro: This course examines the relationship between people and organisations. Adopting a psychological perspective, it examines strategic aspects of staff development, job, social and cognitive approaches to task and role analysis, recruitment, selection, the training process, equal employment opportunities, industrial relations and performance assessment.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175737). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Applied Criminal Psychology
intro: An advanced study of the aetiology of recurrent criminal behaviour with reference to cultural and ethnicity issues, familial and societal factors, and cognitive and psychopathological correlates. The underlying emphasis of the course is offender rehabilitation.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175719). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Psychological Well-being in Organisations
intro: This course examines the influence of organisational and work characteristics on staff well-being and performance. It covers stress and stress management, designing salutogenic workplaces, implications of disaster and crises for staff, families and communities, psychological aspects of promoting health and safety in organisations.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175732). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Postmodernism and Psychology
intro: The course will focus on some of the ways in which the cultural phenomenon labelled as 'postmodernism' has impacted on/in psychology, with particular emphasis on the poststructuralist theories through which postmodern psychology is developing.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175718). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: The Psychology of Sport and Exercise
intro: The course will examine current psychological theories and related research in the sport and exercise domain.  Specifically, the focus of the course is on how psychological factors influence involvement and performance in sport and exercise.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175747). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Clinical Psychology Practica
intro: Provides structured supervision of the student’s clinical activities. It includes observation of the work of clinical psychologists, supervised practice of designated clinical psychology skills, and exploration of individualised psychotherapy approaches and formal treatment protocols in a selected domain of practice. And provides opportunities for supervised practice of all the professional activities of a clinical psychologist, including working in professional teams, conducting assessments under supervision, and using the empirical literature to guide clinical decision making.
description: Postgraduate Research Psychology paper (60 credits, 175813). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Available at: Auckland, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Child Clinical Neuropsychology
intro: This course is designed to enable students to understand and use theories, research and skills involved in child neuropsychology. An emphasis will be placed on functioning in interdisciplinary teams.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175734). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Psychological Research: Quantitative Data Analysis
intro: An examination of how psychologists use quantitative data analysis techniques to address complex research problems involving multiple variables. Techniques including multiple regression, factor analysis and structural equation modelling are explored, with an emphasis on the relationships between analyses, research questions and design issues.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175746). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Applied Social Psychology
intro: This course adopts an applied orientation to social psychology in relation to complex socio-cultural issues.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (30 credits, 175725). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Psychological Assessment in Organisations
intro: This course investigates the application of the principles and theory of psychological measurement in organisational contexts. It covers the development of psychological tests, the implications of organisational factors for latent psychological constructs, test characteristics and test evaluation, and the application of psychological tests for processes such as selection, promotion, performance appraisal.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175741). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Health Psychology
intro: This course consists of an introduction to and critique of general theories and models that have been used to understand, describe, predict and change behaviours related to health and illness and health promotion.  It has a strong focus on the application of health psychology in the New Zealand context.
description: Year 3 Psychology paper (15 credits, 175317). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Sustainable Livelihood
intro: According to the UN, Sustainable Livelihoods include not only access to shelter and food but also security, dignity, societal participation for groups and individuals. This course examines how workplaces potentially enable such capabilities, e.g., by respecting cultural foundations, enabling work-life-balance, providing decent work, and reducing inequality, through equitable partnerships between organizations along global supply chains, fair trade, living wages.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175733). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: People, Management and Careers at Work
intro: Study of applied aspects of individual differences in organisations. Issues such as selection and training, performance evaluation, occupational health and vocational and career development will be studied.
description: Year 3 Psychology paper (15 credits, 175343). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Language, Discourse and Power
intro: An introduction to critical discourse analysis, exploring how language is used in the reproduction of social inequalities.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 172237). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Forensic Psychology
intro: The focus of this course is to study the issues and controversies in contemporary relationships between psychology and law.  The diversity of the field of forensic practice is reflected in the topics covered, and the themes of violence and justice are used to represent the multidisciplinary influences at the nexus of psychology and law within mental health and legal systems.
description: Year 3 Psychology paper (15 credits, 175309). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Practice in Psychology
intro: The course provides an in-depth examination of the professional issues that impact on the practice of psychology. Models of practice, ethics, the statutes that affect practitioners, professional interrelationships and cultural issues are all analysed using a case-based approach.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175730). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Applying Health Psychology
intro: This course offers a critical perspective on health psychology practice. Students will examine theories and methods for promoting, maintaining, and restoring health and well-being at micro/individual, meso/community, and macro/population levels. Framed by a social model of health, the course emphasises a holistic approach, evidence-based practice, and theoretically informed interventions, drawing on applied examples and case studies.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175744). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Psychology and Culture
intro: This course examines the wide-ranging influence culture has in people's lives, from their ideological beliefs and values to their behaviour patterns.  How cultural influences have been considered in the theory, research, and practice of psychology will be examined.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175729). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Conducting Psychological Research
intro: Students will develop and apply hands-on skills for conducting research in psychology. They will design and conduct research for a range of psychological studies and develop capabilities in the ethical application of both qualitative and quantitative research and science communication. The course is designed to prepare students for postgraduate level study and/or a career that requires research skills.
description: Year 3 Psychology paper (15 credits, 175318). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Contemporary Work Psychology
intro: The application of psychological principles and theory to contemporary biopsychosocial issues and hazards at work. Students will learn critical skills in psychological evaluation, appropriate for the environmental, cultural and political contexts of the New Zealand workplace.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175740). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Community Psychology
intro: Community psychology focuses on people within the contexts of social settings and systems, that is, in ecological relationships with social and physical environments. Conceptual frameworks, the roles of research and practice, and specific intervention strategies developed and used by community psychologists will be among topics considered.
description: Year 3 Psychology paper (15 credits, 175301). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Work Psychology
intro: An introduction to key concepts within Work Psychology, concentrating on the interplay between theoretical issues and practical concerns. Students will be enabled to critically evaluate the implications which different psychological perspectives have for understanding workplace problems.
description: Year 3 Psychology paper (15 credits, 175345). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: The Psychology of Security
intro: An exploration of how psychology can be utilised to improve our understanding of, and responses to, the various security challenges posed by the contemporary environment.
description: Year 3 Psychology paper (15 credits, 175304). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: E-Health and Technology in Nursing
intro: Critical evaluation of the desired and unseen effects of technology in nursing including the status of technology and its role in creating an information-literate nursing workforce.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 168745). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report Museum Studies (60)
intro: Students intending to enrol in this course should write to the programme coordinator of Museum Studies at the earliest opportunity. A dissertation proposal must be submitted by 1 April in the year of enrolment and approved by the Museum Studies Research Committee. A dissertation of not more than 20,000 words must be submitted by 10 October in the year of enrolment.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 167896). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 2
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Applied Statistics paper (60 credits, 162898). Prerequisite study in Applied Statistics is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Health Psychology: Understanding Health and Illness
intro: A critical examination of how conceptualisations of health and illness shape symptom interpretation, help-seeking, diagnosis, and healthcare relationships, as well as wider social reception. Sociopolitical and cultural factors are given particular emphasis, along with an exploration of the notion of person-centred care.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175739). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Linguistics
description: Doctoral paper (120 credits, 172900). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Principles of Clinical Neuropsychology
intro: This course will focus on the principles of neuropsychological assessment, as they apply across the life span. Factors which influence neuropsychological functioning will be considered including neuroanatomy, cognitive functioning and the conditions that influence these factors.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175722). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Indigenous Psychologies
intro: The course provides advanced content of indigenous psychologies, including their specific application in the theory, research and practice of psychology across multiple domains (e.g., health, education, social, policy, etc) in Aotearoa New Zealand specifically, and more generally in other cultural contexts across the globe.
description: Year 3 Psychology paper (15 credits, 175314). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Ngā Tirohanga Rua o te Taha Hinengaro: Bicultural Perspectives in Psychology
intro: This course engages with the concept of culture, and its relevance and importance in a bicultural country such as Aotearoa. The interplay between non-Western cultural perspectives and the dominant form of psychology situated within a predominantly Western scientific paradigm, is explored. Indigenous perspectives in general and Māori worldviews specifically, are examined within and juxtaposed to, the discipline of psychology.
description: Year 2 Psychology paper (15 credits, 175210). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Experimental Psychology
intro: This course provides an advanced overview of the theoretical and methodological issues that define contemporary experimental psychology with particular focus on affective, cognitive, and social psychology.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (30 credits, 175724). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Memory and Cognition
intro: The central goal of the course is to consider how knowledge is represented and processed in the brain. Students will be introduced to the mental processes involved in thinking and knowing, studied within a converging methods framework that includes evidence from experimental psychology, neuroscience, neuropsychology, and cognitive science.
description: Year 2 Psychology paper (15 credits, 175206). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Multimodal Discourse Analysis
intro: This course examines communication as a multimodal phenomenon, assessing space and time as key variables in the production of written and spoken utterances.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 172712). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Managerial Economics
intro: Economic principles applied to decision problems of managers in a business organisation. Analysis of costs, revenues and profits in relation to a firm's objectives. Emphasis on pricing policy, investment decisions, advertising and promotion expenditure.
description: Year 2 Sociology paper (15 credits, 178240). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Markets, Firms and Consumers
intro: Microeconomic analysis will be applied to problems in consumer behaviour, business strategies and investment choice, resource allocation and regulation.
description: Year 3 Sociology paper (15 credits, 178307). Prerequisite study in Sociology is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Competition and Industry
intro: The modelling of microeconomic decision-making of competing firms in industries, with application to real-world issues.
description: Year 3 Sociology paper (15 credits, 178301). Prerequisite study in Sociology is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Macroeconomic Policy and Applications
intro: This course incorporates financial markets into macroeconomic models and analyses government policies that address current macroeconomic events.
description: Year 3 Sociology paper (15 credits, 178300). Prerequisite study in Sociology is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Regression and Predictive Analysis
intro: An introduction to regression and predictive analysis, using real world data to provide actionable insights that aid organisational decision-making processes.
description: Year 2 Sociology paper (15 credits, 178280). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Project Evaluation
intro: Theoretical and empirical aspects of economics (benefit-cost analysis) and financial project evaluation. Consumption/investment decisions; discounted cash-flow techniques; cost of capital financing; risk and uncertainty; and shadow pricing for economic policy. Emphasis is on practical applications, using microcomputers, to projects and investments in developed and developing countries.
description: Year 3 Sociology paper (15 credits, 178328). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report (30)
description: Postgraduate Counselling paper (30 credits, 176799). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Economic Analysis of Money, Banking and Financial Markets
intro: This course analyses the causes, characteristics and consequences of business cycles. A neo-classical framework is applied to situations commonly seen in small open economies such as New Zealand. The role and importance of financial markets is highlighted and used to prescribe optimal government policy in many frequently observed macroeconomic situations.
description: Year 3 Sociology paper (15 credits, 178308). Prerequisite study in Sociology is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Economic Policy
intro: Macroeconomic and microeconomic policies and problems of unemployment, inflation, balance of payments, growth, market distortions and public goods. Justification for government intervention and its effectiveness. Application of economic principles will be stressed and social considerations incorporate emphasis on the New Zealand experience.
description: Year 2 Sociology paper (15 credits, 178210). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 2
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Counselling paper (45 credits, 176882). Prerequisite study in Counselling is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Principles of Economic Policy
intro: The course provides students with a basic understanding of economic policy at industry, national and international levels.
description: Year 1 Sociology paper (15 credits, 178100). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis Part B
intro: Supports continued research activities, normally involving selecting and testing participants; other data collection strategies as needed by the research design; statistical analysis of data, conceptualisation of the findings, and the development of tentative conclusions.
description: Doctoral Psychology paper (90 credits, 175992). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Sociology
description: Doctoral Counselling paper (120 credits, 176900). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Land Economics
intro: The treatment of land resource questions in economics, including changing theoretical approaches to land and the income from the land, factors influencing the behaviour of land markets, environmental economics and land use, specific models of land use patterns, the question of the efficient use of land resources, estimating changing land use requirements, taxation in relation to land markets, location decision.
description: Year 2 Sociology paper (15 credits, 178242). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Society, Climate Change and the Environment
intro: This course examines the interconnections between society and the environment from a social sciences perspective, emphasising the roles of science and politics in the creation of environmental knowledge and practice. We examine the causes and consequences of environmental problems, including climate change. We also consider possibilities for transformative change through activism, Te Ao Māori and other Indigenous perspectives, markets, government, and community-led initiatives.
description: Year 3 Counselling paper (15 credits, 176308). Prerequisite study in Counselling is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Sport Economics
intro: An introduction to fundamental economic principles and concepts and their application to sports.
description: Year 2 Sociology paper (15 credits, 178239). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Globalisation in a digital world
intro: This course examines the intersecting material, social, and digital complexities of late capitalism in the context of increasing conditions of globalisation and glocalisation. Using a variety of sociological optics, the course aims to assist students to develop critical understandings that will enable them to be constructive contributors to their future worlds.
description: Year 2 Counselling paper (15 credits, 176216). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Psychology
description: Doctoral Psychology paper (120 credits, 175900). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Professional Issues in Psychological Practice Part I
intro: This course is Part I of the core curriculum for students completing the PGDipPsychPrac.  The course covers the advanced study of professional psychology theory, models, methods, and principles as they relate to those who are establishing their professional identity and beginning to practice.  The integrated curriculum develops student’s knowledge of the New Zealand Psychologists Board Cultural and Core Competencies and provides the scientific and reflective frameworks for safe and ethical practice.
description: Postgraduate Research Psychology paper (30 credits, 175851). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Professional Issues in Psychological Practice Part II
intro: This is Part II of the core curriculum for students completing the PGDipPsychPrac.  The course covers the advanced study of professional psychology theory, models, methods, and principles as they relate to those who are consolidating their professional identity and attaining entry level competence.  The integrated curriculum deepens students’ knowledge of the New Zealand Psychologists Board Cultural and Core Competencies and reflective practice, and addresses the complexities of safe and ethical practice with individuals and systems.
description: Postgraduate Research Psychology paper (30 credits, 175852). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Counselling paper (45 credits, 176881). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Microeconomics
intro: The development and application of models of consumer optimisation, producer behaviour, and competition in markets.
description: Year 2 Sociology paper (15 credits, 178201). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Racism and Colonisation
intro: A review of the development of ideas of race and nation from the early modern era in Europe through to their world-wide crisis of the twentieth century including contemporary attempts to move beyond race via the concepts of ethnicity and indigeneity in Aotearoa New Zealand.
description: Year 2 Counselling paper (15 credits, 176218). Prerequisite study in Counselling is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Current Issues and Theories
intro: An examination of key concepts, theories and debates in contemporary social theory.
description: Postgraduate Counselling paper (30 credits, 176701). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report Psychology (60)
description: Postgraduate Research Psychology paper (60 credits, 175873). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Methods of Economic Analysis
intro: An introduction to the techniques of quantitative economic analysis. The use of linear algebra and calculus in economics. Constrained optimisation. Simple dynamics.
description: Year 2 Sociology paper (15 credits, 178221). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Design and Practice
intro: A practical course that introduces the principles of research design, quantitative and qualitative methods and research dissemination, and students undertake a small sociological research project under supervised conditions.
description: Year 3 Counselling paper (15 credits, 176302). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Environmental Sociology
intro: An exploration of the interrelations between society and the environment, with an examination of major contemporary environmental issues from a sociological point of view. Among the major issues covered are consumerism, population growth, resource limits, development, political conflicts, environmental groups and environmental values.
description: Postgraduate Counselling paper (30 credits, 176718). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis Part A
intro: Thesis research builds the student's ability to carry out independent scientific inquiry which represents a significant contribution to knowledge and understanding in clinical psychology. In the initial stages of thesis preparation, students participate in a group seminar to review a number of specialised methodological issues that exist within clinical psychology.
description: Doctoral Psychology paper (90 credits, 175991). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Clinical Psychology Practicum B
intro: Provides further supervision of the student's clinical activities in the workplace to fulfil clinical psychology practicum requirements. It includes supervised practice of all the professional activities of a clinical psychologist, including working in professional teams, conducting assessments under supervision, and using the empirical literature to guide clinical decision making.
description: Doctoral Psychology paper (15 credits, 175921). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report Sociology (60)
description: Postgraduate Research Counselling paper (60 credits, 176873). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Macroeconomics
intro: The development and application of macroeconomic models to globally-oriented economies, and the assessment of real-world policy problems from a practical perspective.
description: Year 2 Sociology paper (15 credits, 178200). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: Integrates the student's emerging ability to carry out independent research of direct relevance to conceptual foundations of clinical psychology. The candidate integrates the major study with clinical follow-up study or studies of a practical nature, derived from the concurrent internship experiences.
description: Doctoral Psychology paper (60 credits, 175993). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Counselling paper (60 credits, 176816). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: The Politics of Truth
intro: An enquiry into the social contexts and power relationships that influence the production of knowledge, drawing on situations of current political significance.  ‘Truth-claims’ are deconstructed in order to identify underlying ideological and political impulses.
description: Year 3 Counselling paper (15 credits, 176325). Prerequisite study in Counselling is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Psychology paper (60 credits, 175896). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Social Inequality and Justice
intro: An examination of how social structures create inequalities with a focus on gender, class, and ethnic diversities and identities and how claims for social transformation are made.
description: Year 2 Counselling paper (15 credits, 176223). Prerequisite study in Counselling is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Social Inquiry
intro: An advanced study of methodological issues pertinent to social research. The course is designed to assist the planning of postgraduate theses and reports.
description: Postgraduate Counselling paper (30 credits, 176702). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Skills in Clinical Assessment
intro: Provides advanced training in applied skills of clinical psychology assessment across the lifespan, including interview skills, skills in case conceptualisation and psychological formulation, and skills in the selection, administration, scoring, interpretation and reporting of psychological and neuropsychological tests.
description: Doctoral Psychology paper (15 credits, 175935). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Available at: Auckland, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Practicum in Psychological Practice Part I
intro: This is Part I of the individualised curriculum for students completing the PGDipPsychPrac.  For this course students undertake supervised practice with clients in an approved setting.  The focus is on the ethical, safe, respectful application of psychological knowledge/theory in practice, and demonstration of the foundations of Core and Cultural Competence.  The integrated curriculum allows students to apply their learning in the workplace, establish their professional identity, and attain a strong understanding of the role and obligations of a psychologist in Aotearoa New Zealand.
description: Postgraduate Research Psychology paper (30 credits, 175853). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report (30)
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (30 credits, 175799). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Clinical Psychology Internship
intro: Supervised experience in all aspects of clinical psychology service delivery in a professional service setting, with increasing emphasis on independent ability to offer specialised assessment and treatment programmes to a diversity of clients in a safe, culturally appropriate, and ethical manner.
description: Doctoral Psychology paper (60 credits, 175922). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: AI and the World of Work
intro: This course provides an in-depth exploration of the changed nature of work in contemporary society. It examines the broader context of change, contemporary forms and patterns of work and occupations and issues and perspectives on global and local labour markets.
description: Year 3 Counselling paper (15 credits, 176322). Prerequisite study in Counselling is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Quantitative Methods for Economics
intro: This course provides a foundation for students to develop their quantitative and analytical skills in the context of the Economics discipline.
description: Year 1 Sociology paper (15 credits, 178111). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Imagining Other Worlds
intro: A study of contemporary utopian sociological approaches to social change, based upon models of economy, administrative governance, and social reproduction that differ from those of colonial capitalism.
description: Year 3 Counselling paper (15 credits, 176324). Prerequisite study in Counselling is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: The Sociological Imagination
intro: A foundation course in Sociology covering the key substantive aspects of contemporary society e.g. individual and social processes, globalisation and social inequalities.
description: Year 1 Counselling paper (15 credits, 176101). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: Provides structured supervision of the student's clinical activities in the workplace to fulfil clinical psychology practicum requirements. It included observation of the work of clinical psychologists, supervised practice of designated clinical psychology skills, and exploration of individualised psychotherapy approaches and formal treatment protocols in a selected domain of practice.
description: Doctoral Psychology paper (15 credits, 175920). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: The Stuff of Everyday Life
intro: An introduction to the sociology of everyday life and everyday material objects in both local and global contexts.
description: Year 1 Counselling paper (15 credits, 176106). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Practicum in Psychological Practice Part II
intro: This is Part II of the individualised curriculum for students completing the PGDipPsychPrac.  For this course students continue supervised practice with clients in an approved setting.  The focus is on preparing for independent practice and consolidating all of the Core and Cultural Competencies.  The integrated curriculum allows students to apply their learning in the workplace, effectively manage complexity, and demonstrate attainment of all competencies required to register as a psychologist in Aotearoa New Zealand.
description: Postgraduate Research Psychology paper (30 credits, 175854). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: This course is designed to enable students to understand and use theories, research, and skills involved in neuropsychological rehabilitation. This course will review interventions that arise from neuropsychological evaluation. An emphasis will be placed on functioning in interdisciplinary teams.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175751). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.

title: The Practice of Psychological Research
intro: Study of the practice of psychological research. Students undertake a range of exercises and class projects to develop practical research skills.

title: Psychology as a Social Science
intro: An introduction to psychology as the scientific study of human behaviour, with emphasis on individual differences and social influences. The course aims to develop an awareness of the issues, terminology, methods and techniques involved in the study of human behaviour.
description: Year 1 Psychology paper (15 credits, 175101). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report
intro: The supervised undertaking of an original piece of research into discourse and communication from a linguistic perspective.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 172799). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Sounds and Structures
intro: Introduces theoretical approaches to phonology and syntactic/grammatical structures and their interfaces.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 172330). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Languages of the Pacific
intro: An examination of the three major language subgroups spoken in the Pacific, focussing on their formal elements, the relationship between language and society, and the linguistic consequences of the encounter between Pacific peoples and speakers of non-Pacific languages.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 172336). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Social Psychology
intro: A survey of contemporary experimental social psychology. Against this backdrop critical perspectives are introduced with particular emphasis on the practice of discursive psychology in the New Zealand context.
description: Year 2 Psychology paper (15 credits, 175201). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Language and Society in New Zealand
intro: An introduction to sociolinguistics with particular reference to New Zealand: focussing on language and social interaction, regional and social variation in language use, bilingualism and the status of minority languages, and the sociolinguistics of te Reo Māori.

title: Historical and Comparative Linguistics
intro: An advanced study of historical language change and language relationships. The course examines reasons for and types of language change, and methods for reconstructing earlier language forms.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 172337). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Topics in Nursing
intro: Critical reviews, complex case studies, advanced study and/or research into selected aspects of Nursing.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (30 credits, 168802). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Language and Identity
intro: An advanced sociolinguistic study of how language is used to represent and construct aspects of identity relevant to particular social interactions, including the individual’s ability to negotiate multiple forms of identity and belonging.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 172335). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Clinical Psychology
intro: This course provides an introduction to clinical psychology and mental distress, with an emphasis on current thinking and research in the field. The course examines: the core concepts in defining and classifying mental distress; some of the major psychological disorders across the lifespan; empirically supported approaches to explaining mental distress; and treatments aimed at supporting people towards recovery.
description: Year 3 Psychology paper (15 credits, 175302). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Economic Growth, International and Development Economics
intro: A critical assessment of topics from economic growth, international economics and the nature and measurement of development. There is an emphasis on the empirical and policy implications of development strategies including the relationship between managing development, role of the State, international economy, globalisation, trade theory and policy, global crises, and macroeconomic policy.
description: Postgraduate Sociology paper (30 credits, 178755). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: An Introduction to Social Research for Social Work and Social Policy
intro:
An introduction to social research and an examination of application of
evidence to inform social policy and social work practice.

intro: An introduction to advanced methods used in agricommerce research. A selection of the basic research principles illustrated with examples from agricultural production and marketing, agribusiness management and consumer economics. This course has a practical orientation.
description: Postgraduate Sociology paper (15 credits, 178741). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: International Monetary Economics
intro: This course is concerned with the monetary and macroeconomic relationships between countries.  It deals with such issues as balance-of-payments problems and policies, the functioning of foreign exchange rate markets, the determination and causes of exchange-rate movements, the international monetary system, and derivative instruments including swaps, options and futures.
description: Postgraduate Sociology paper (30 credits, 178712). Prerequisite study in Sociology is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

Professional Practice may be conducted either individually or in groups and assessment of the outcome of the investigation may be in a variety of ways including written and oral presentations and case studies
description: Postgraduate Research Sociology paper (60 credits, 178896). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.
