title: Health Economics
intro: Subject areas to be covered include microeconomics for healthcare, the economics of politics and bureaus, demand for healthcare, the supply and organisation of healthcare, hospital behaviour, moral hazard, cost-benefit analysis in health, private versus public provision of healthcare and contemporary policy issues.
description: Postgraduate Sociology paper (30 credits, 178718). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: The Economics of Knowledge and Innovation
intro: An introduction to the complex process of innovation and its many dimensions in advanced and emerging knowledge-based economies. The nature of knowledge, innovation and intellectual property, the microeconomics and macroeconomics of innovation, and policy at the firm and macro levels are examined in the context of a globalised world economy that faces major economic and environmental challenges.
description: Postgraduate Sociology paper (30 credits, 178775). Prerequisite study in Sociology is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Econometrics
intro: This course introduces students to advanced methods used in econometrics and forecasting.  Topics include time-series analysis; testing and model selection; simultaneous equations; nonstationarity; vector autoregressive models; causality and exogeneity; binary choice models and panel data analysis.
description: Postgraduate Sociology paper (30 credits, 178732). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: The course provides an advanced exposition and critical assessment of selected topics from macroeconomic theory and policy.  The general focus will be on the issues of short and long term effects of macroeconomic policies on the economy.  The relationship between theory and empirical research is also examined.
description: Postgraduate Sociology paper (30 credits, 178702). Prerequisite study in Sociology is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 168744). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Primary Health Care in Practice
intro: Primary health care is central to improving equity. This course critiques the primary health care system and clinical practice on a continuum from health promotion and disease prevention to treatment, rehabilitation, and palliative care. By prioritising equity and complying with guarantees of Te Tiriti o Waitangi health professionals can achieve equitable health outcomes for Māori and other underserved populations.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 168724). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Health Research Design and Method
intro: Philosophical, ethical and methodological issues in relation to health research are examined. Selected quantitative and qualitative methods are explored in depth, in preparation for developing a research proposal for a thesis, or research project.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 168711). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Contemporary Approaches to Clinical Teaching and Learning
intro: An examination of a range of theoretical perspectives and sociomaterial and sociocultural aspects influencing clinical teaching and learning. Sociomaterial and sociocultural perspectives recognise that learning is influenced by the context, by human and non-human aspects, and by the variety of interactions occurring in the learning space. Alternative approaches to the design, delivery, and assessment of practice-based education are explored.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 168709). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Kawa Whakaruruhau and Cultural Safety in Clinical Practice
intro: This course explores the culture change needed for the health system to be pro-equity, Tiriti-compliant, and anti-racist. Kawa Whakaruruhau and cultural safety are ways to embed justness within health care and improve outcomes for Māori and other populations. Practitioners have the potential to dismantle systemic racism and change structures, policies, practices, norms and values to achieve health care without discrimination.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 168721). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

Particular attention is brought to nurses’ role as moral agents in ‘everyday’ practice. Significant themes include social justice; links between biculturalism, cultural safety and ethics; and the ethical implications of the socio-political context of contemporary healthcare.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 168717). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

An in-depth case study approach will be used to develop students' skills in working with children and families. The course is designed to build on the students prior knowledge of developmental and abnormal psychology.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175721). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Psychology of Women
intro: This course examines contemporary theoretical studies in the psychology of women.  It is an advanced study of the history of women in psychology and critiques traditional approaches to research on women and girls.  It includes three contemporary approaches focusing on specific areas of concern in the sub-discipline at present.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175720). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: A theory and research-based examination of the Psychology of Women as a sub-discipline with particular focus on how research in the field challenges gender biases and social assumptions. The history of the field is illustrated by classic and contemporary examples of research methodologies suitable for critical studies of sexuality, mothering and gender-based violence.
description: Year 3 Psychology paper (15 credits, 175311). Prerequisite study in Psychology is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

The course covers behavioural and cognitive-behavioural interventions, experiential and brief dynamic psychotherapies as well as other factors in therapy (e.g., the roles of therapist, client, and culture).
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175783). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Clinical Psychology Assessment
intro: This course will examine a variety of standard objective and subjective clinical assessment instruments, and their use in diagnosis and treatment across the lifespan. Topics covered include cognitive, emotional and behavioural assessment; diagnosis-specific and population-specific instruments; and diagnostic interview systems. Principles of test administration, scoring and interpretation as well as ethical issues surrounding psychological testing will also be covered.
description: Postgraduate Psychology paper (15 credits, 175782). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Clinical Psychology Internship
intro: Supervised experience in all aspects of clinical psychology service delivery in a professional service setting, with increasing emphasis on independent ability to offer specialised assessment and treatment programmes to a diversity of clients in a safe, culturally appropriate, and ethical manner.    The internship comprises a minimum of fifteen hundred hours of supervised direct clinical experience in an approved setting. The internship involves supervision, videotaped assessment of work with clients, seminars and workshops designed to deal with specialised clinical issues such as client safety, legal concerns, sophisticated diagnostic methods, bicultural practice and so on, culminating in a practical-oriented exam involving external examiners.
description: Postgraduate Research Psychology paper (120 credits, 175814). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

Students will focus on the use of digital and mobile media technologies, and consciously negotiate decisions regarding the cultural, social, political, economic, ethical and environmental implications of their own media production, distribution and consumption.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 154747). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

This course examines these practices in relation to the maintenance and negotiation of cultural identity in the contemporary world.

title: The Communication Field
intro: This course provides an advanced-level overview of the communication discipline, its history, key concepts, competing traditions, and noted scholars.  Critical, creative and organisational theories and concepts from a broad communication studies body of knowledge are historicised, compared, and critically evaluated.  Throughout the course, communication knowledge is applied to illuminate communication questions from media, industry and society.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 154721). Available at: Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

Students will plan and produce short documentary films. NOTE: Students require access to their own video camera and editing facilities.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 154224). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.

Barriers to development and the facilitation of positive development are explored using criteria relevant to indigenous self-determination. There is a focus on land, fisheries, social policy, health, Treaty settlements and opportunities for positive development.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 150701). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Whānau ora: Principles of Flourishing Whānau
intro: Approaches for the advancement of Māori health will be reviewed with a particular focus on health promotion, whānau ora and the link between Māori health initiatives and positive Māori development.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 150302). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Marautanga o Te Aho Matua: The Kura Kaupapa Māori Curriculum
intro: He whakataki me te tatari i nga paearu ngaio o Te Marautanga o Te Aho Matua me te mahi i nga wahanga ako, a, nga anga aromatawai hoki ki ta te kura tirohanga whanui no roto mai i nga Kura Kaupapa Māori.  An   introduction   to   and   analysis   of   the   professional   requirements   of   Te Marautanga o Te Aho Matua and the application of learning areas, and assessment frameworks to a whole school curriculum approach within Kura Kaupapa Māori.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 150733). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Reo o te Ao Whanui: Māori as an Official Language
intro: The use of Māori language in specialised areas and in sectors such as health, education, justice and commerce will be explored. Students will be required to demonstrate high levels of written and oral competence in one or two specialist areas, to undertake translations of contemporary technical documents as well as the compilation of an extended glossary.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 150713). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Mata Oho: Studio IIB
intro: An examination of the significance of Māori visual culture with a view to understanding the interrelationships between form and function within 'traditional' and 'contemporary' contexts. Advanced development of media and processes within three-dimensional frameworks.
description: Year 2 paper (45 credits, 150208). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Tō Te Kura Kawa: Pedagogy and Practice in Kura Kaupapa Māori
intro: He ata tirotiro me te whakamahi i nga ahua akoranga o Te Aho Matua me Te Marautanga o Te Aho Matua ki roto ki nga Kura Kaupapa Māori.  An examination and application of Te Aho Matua pedagogies and Te Marautanga o Te Aho Matua curriculum within Kura Kaupapa Māori settings.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 150734). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Taonga Tuku Iho: Heritage Aotearoa
intro: An examination of the dynamics of Māori culture and custom as part of the Aotearoa/New Zealand heritage. Particular emphasis is placed on the significance of land, language, oral tradition, the marae, art, and the Treaty of Waitangi, as well as an examination of the role of government in heritage through a study of legislation, policy and programmes. Case studies will focus on conservation, maintenance, sustainability and revitalisation.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 150715). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Uiui ā Akomanga: Inquiry-Based Approaches in Kura Kaupapa Māori
intro: Ko nga rautaki pakirehua Kaupapa Māori me nga iwi taketake e tirotiro ana ki nga wero e puta mai ana i te mahi whakaako ki nga Kura Kaupapa Māori.  Māori and global indigenous inquiry methods that address existing or emerging challenges of teaching practice in Kura Kaupapa Māori learning environments
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 150732). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Tā Te Māori Rangahau Kōrero: Māori Research Methodologies
intro: An examination of methodologies appropriate for research within Māori communities, iwi, hapu and whanau.  Emphasis will be placed on accessing relevant information held in public repositories, on the internet and on computerised databases.  The identification of frameworks for research in Māori contexts, ethical issues, and research design form important aspects of the course.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 150714). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Mata: Studio IIIA
intro: Advanced development of 150.207 in selected areas. An approved, individually conceived programme with content related to personal strengths and interests within two-dimensional frameworks.
description: Year 3 paper (30 credits, 150307). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Ao Hurihuri: Contemporary Māori Issues
intro: A critical examination of contemporary Māori cultural, political and human rights issues through colonisation to present day renaissance of Māori and indigenous peoples.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 150304). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Mauri ora: Māori Wellbeing and Vitality
intro: Cultural understandings of health form the basis for an exploration of cultural, biological, social, economic, environmental and political interactions and their impacts on Māori health.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 150202). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Kura Mai Tawhiti: Māori Knowledge
intro: An examination of heritage and contemporary Māori knowledges exploring the origins and relevance of traditional belief systems and the contemporary cultural-political contexts of mātauranga paradigms in areas such as research, education, justice, science, business, social development and the environment.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 150205). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Mata: Studio IIIB
intro: Advanced development of 150.208 in selected areas. An approved, individually conceived programme with content related to personal strengths and interests within three-dimensional frameworks.
description: Year 3 paper (60 credits, 150308). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD History
description: Doctoral paper (120 credits, 148900). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Matatau: Studio IVB
intro: Advanced conceptual development of 150.308 in selected areas. An approved individually conceived programme with content related to personal strengths and interests within three-dimensional frameworks.
description: Honours paper (60 credits, 150408). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Tau-Ihu o te Reo: Advanced Māori Literature
intro: Tuatahi he ako i te taumata momo reo, te kawa o tātau marae pēnei i te tangihanga, whakataukī, pepeha, kōrero pūrākau, waiata tawhito.  Tuarua he kaupapa mai i te rēanga poropiti Māori o ērā atu rau tau.  Ka tātaritia hoki ngā kōrero mo tērā poropiti a Te Kooti Ārikirangi Te Tūruki i ahu mai ai ngā kaupapa maha e pā ana ki te mana motuhake.  Tuatoru kā tātaritia ngā kōrero e pā ana ki tēnei kaupapa hohonu, e taukapo ai ōnā kaupapa ko te poroporoaki tēnā.  This course explores language forms used in marae procedure, and language associated with tangihanga, whakataukī, pepeha, kōrero pūrākau, waiata tawhito.  In addition the prophecies and aspirations of Te Kooti Ārikirangi Te Tūruki are explored.  There is also an examination of the oral narrative and written literature associated with poroporoaki.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 150711). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Tataitanga Matatau: Advanced Studio Practice
intro: An individually designed programme of advanced studio practice in Māori or indigenous visual culture development, with content related to personal strengths and interests.
description: Postgraduate paper (90 credits, 150707). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Mana Wahine: Māori Women
intro: A theory and research based examination of issues that concern Māori women in all contexts, including the roles that Māori women assume both within a Māori social framework and beyond.  Theories of mana wahine and the ways mana is maintained, enhanced or lessened will be examined.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 150303). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Mana Māori: Māori and Politics
intro: The study of the nature of Māori politics and political self-determination in contemporary Aotearoa New Zealand. It will explore the avenues through which Māori have sought to realise their political aspirations, particularly in relation to national political institutions, and theoretical perspectives that can assist in the analysis and strategic development of Māori political self-determination.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 150204). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Reo o Te Akomanga: Language of the Classroom
intro: He tatari, he whakahangai i ta te Māori titiro ki nga paearu mahi katoa mo te ao matauranga me Te Aho Matua e whai mahi ai i roto i nga Kura hei kaiako, ara, e taea ai e nga tauira.  A critical analysis and application of Māori perspectives applied to performance criteria in the education context and Te Aho Matua that are essential for teaching within kura kaupapa Māori.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 150731). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Mata Oho: Studio IIA
intro: An examination of the significance of Māori visual culture with a view to understanding the interrelationships between form and function within 'traditional' and 'contemporary' contexts. Advanced development of media and processes within two-dimensional frameworks.
description: Year 2 paper (30 credits, 150207). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Mana Te Kawanatanga: Māori Policy and the State
intro: Recent policies, legislation and judicial outcomes that impact on Māori people are examined as well as interaction between Māori and the State in formulating policies for Māori. A framework for analysis derived from Māori perspectives serves as a basis for understanding policy development and is applied to specific policy areas including Māori land, broadcasting, fishing, employment, health, the environment and Treaty settlements.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 150301). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Hauora Tangata: Foundations of Māori Health
intro: An introduction to understanding Māori health within Aotearoa/New Zealand.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 150112). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Nga Hanga Whakairo: Traditional Māori Visual Art
intro: An introduction to the scope of Māori art with a view to recognising traditional elementary forms and their significance. Social and cultural dimensions will be considered and Māori art forms from pre-contact times to the present will be examined within the context of a dynamic and changing society.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 150106). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Kawenata o Waitangi: The Treaty of Waitangi in New Zealand Society
intro: A study of the Treaty of Waitangi background, texts, principles, and application to contemporary New Zealand. There is a particular focus on land, legislation, court decisions, social policies, the environment, constitutional matters, claims to the Waitangi Tribunal and Treaty settlements. Differing perspectives of hapū/iwi/Māori and the Crown, as well as opportunities for resolution, are explored.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 150201). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Whakamārama: Approaches to Māori Mental Health and Addiction
intro: Principles underpinning Māori mental health and addiction work in Aotearoa New Zealand will be applied in the development of a social inclusion approach to mental health and addiction work across the life-course and in a range of settings. A hauora approach is used to focus on engagement, prevention, and early intervention with tangata whai ora, whānau and communities.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 150235). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Foundation Māori Culture
intro: An introduction to the significance of Māori culture, including the principles and implications of biculturalism and Te Tiriti o Waitangi.
description: Foundation paper (15 credits, 150001). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S1 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Matatau: Studio IVA
intro: Advanced conceptual development of 150.307 in selected areas. An approved individually conceived programme with content related to personal strengths and interests within two-dimensional frameworks.
description: Honours paper (60 credits, 150407). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Nau mai e noho: Engaging with Māori
intro: This course will equip students with a range of skills to engage with Māori communities including common expressions in te reo, an understanding of key traditional concepts, customary practices (tikanga), the importance of the Treaty of Waitangi and the nature and structure of Māori social and political organisations.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 150103). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Mata Puare: Studio IB
intro: An exploration of the principles and elements of design and their application within three-dimensional contexts. The cultural significance of Māori design processes will be examined in order to understand interrelationships between form and content.
description: Year 1 paper (45 credits, 150108). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Mata Puare: Studio IA
intro: An exploration of the principles and elements of design and their application within two-dimensional contexts. The cultural significance of Māori design processes will be examined in order to understand interrelationships between form and content.
description: Year 1 paper (30 credits, 150107). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Ngā Momo Whakairo: Contemporary Māori Visual Art
intro: An interpretation of the design structures that constitute Māori art from a bicultural perspective together with an examination of the factors that determine stylistic change.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 150206). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: An analysis of Māori knowledge, custom and economic foundations.  Customary lifestyles will be examined within a context of ritual, philosophy, technology, economic principles, and social organisation in order to understand Māori culture.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 150114). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: The course examines the power, propaganda and art of European states, focusing on the courts of Burgundian, Valois and Tudor rulers in the late medieval era.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 148339). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: A supervised and guided independent study.

intro: A study of the theory and practice of historical research methodology.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 148730). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Alcohol, Other Drugs and Addiction
intro: A focus on alcohol, other drug use and addiction in Aotearoa New Zealand, emphasising harm reduction and health promotion as intervention tools. Students will develop a critical understanding of the aetiology and epidemiology of drug use and addiction, the co-existence of conditions with addictions, and effective legal, public policy and treatment responses to reducing harm.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 147302). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: An examination of the relationship between ideas about the nature and meaning of history and the writing of history. The course concentrates on groups of historians who have propounded a philosophy of history and have been practitioners of the historian's art.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 148720). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Histories of Sport, Leisure and Recreation
intro: The course explores ideas of recreation and leisure from the medieval period onwards, and particularly the emergence of organised sports in the modern era, with reference to empire, colonisation, women and commercialism.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 148334). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: Based on a close reading of an issue of a contemporary social anthropology journal, students in this course will explore current issues, topics and debates in social anthropology.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 146708). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Science, Culture, and Politics
intro: Cross cultural understandings of how scientific ways of ‘knowing’ operate [politically] in the world.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 146310). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Mental Health Promotion
intro: The course covers the rationale and principles of promoting mental health in Aotearoa New Zealand and globally. Attention is given to key concepts and frameworks underpinning this area of practice.

intro: An introduction to the principles and practices of rehabilitation. The processes of rehabilitation are explored with particular reference to the International Classification of Functioning, Disability and Health (ICF).  Emphasis is placed on exploring a person-centred approach to rehabilitation.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 147101). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Whakapiri: Engagement in Mental Health and Addiction Work
intro: The course provides an introduction to mental health and addiction with a focus on Aotearoa New Zealand. Attention is given to the social context of mental health and wellbeing. Students are introduced to mental health as a human right, processes of engagement and brief intervention in mental health and addiction work.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 147102). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

Students will examine rehabilitation in relation to personal, social and environmental factors and be introduced to terms, concepts and models related to disability, age, gender, culture, legal and political contexts, family and society, advocacy and inclusion.

intro: A study of current theoretical issues and debates in social anthropology.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 146701). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: An introduction to the history of Medieval Europe examining the nature and long-term influence of its culture, society and institutions of power.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 148116). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: The Past as Entertainment: History Through Movies, Mini-series and Games
intro: The ways in which popular culture shapes our understanding of the past are examined through recent movies, mini-series and games.  The course explores the different ways historians and the makers of popular cultures interpret the past.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 148143). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Whakamana: Change Agency in Mental Health and Addiction
intro: Principles underpinning mental health and addiction work in Aotearoa New Zealand are applied through the whakapiri, whakamārama, whakamana framework to enable students to become agents of social inclusion, moving their work beyond knowledge to empowerment, action and sustainability. Students integrate and apply collaborative and reflective practice, critical thinking, coordinated responses, and mana-enhancing approaches to work with tangata whai ora, whānau and communities.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 147305). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Disability in Society
intro: This course examines the social and political context in which disability is created in contemporary society. Topics covered include models of disability, the disability industry, the disability rights movement, the body, cultural and media representations and the politics of disablement.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 147317). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: An experiential course including practical exercises, designed to provide students with knowledge and skills to conduct anthropological fieldwork in a local context using a range of ethnographic methods.

title: The Great War 1914-1918: a Military and Social History
intro: This course examines the social and military history of the First World War. It focuses on the tactics and technology used during the conflict and on the war's impact on society, culture, politics and economics.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 148244). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: The Histories of Aotearoa New Zealand
intro: This course is an introduction to Aotearoa New Zealand history, and debates about its history, from Māori and iwi perspectives to ideas of "New Zealand-ness".
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 148141). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: This course critically explores the ethnography of Aotearoa New Zealand. It considers ethnography as a way of “knowing” Aotearoa New Zealand and what that means for how Aotearoa New Zealand is analysed and represented.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 146309). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: A critical exploration of the role of theory in anthropology, key theoretical frameworks and the practice of theorising ethnography.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 146300). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: This course explores the food chain, from production, through consumption, to exchange. It considers the ways in which food is implicated in the reproduction of identities and inequalities.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 146209). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a report.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 146873). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: Students in this course will examine contemporary exemplary ethnographies and develop their own ethnographic writing practices.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 146707). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Endangered Cultures
intro: An introduction to the impacts of colonisation and capitalism on Indigenous peoples, and their enduring resistance to oppressive systems of power.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 146102). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (90 credits, 145875). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (45 credits, 145871). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 145897). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Anthropology of Ritual, Religion, and Witchcraft
intro: A cross-cultural study of selected topics in the anthropology of ritual and religion including rites of passage, witchcraft, sorcery, myth, magic, spirits, death, symbols, ancestors, altered states of consciousness, and shamanism.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 146210). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Taking Anthropology to the World
intro: This course aims to workshop and assemble a professional portfolio applying anthropological knowledge and skills to respond to real world/topical issues, local and global.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 146308). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Making the Self
intro: An exploration of how concepts of the self, person, and individual vary across cultures.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 146201). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: GIS Principles and Applications
intro: A conceptual and operational understanding of Geographic Information Systems (GIS).  This course provides a postgraduate-level introduction to fundamentals of spatial data creation, manipulation, management, visualisation and analysis.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 145739). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Food Technology
intro: An integrative study of food systems. Individual and group problem-based learning is used to understand political, economic, societal and technological forces shaping the global food industry. Additional aspects of the course focus on interactive project-based activities aimed at honing market awareness, product development and food production skills and competencies. Proficiency in the selection and application of appropriate tools and methodologies for quality assurance and evaluation will also be developed.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 141791). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Geopolitics
intro: An advanced study of geopolitics, emphasising geographical processes across a variety of topics.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 145318). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (45 credits, 145881). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report (30)
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 145799). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Frontiers in Human Geography
intro: An advanced examination and exploration of contemporary issues and debates in human geography.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 145712). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Foundations in Human Geography
intro: An advanced exploration of the history of geographical thought, critically assessing key debates that have shaped the field and examining geographical theory and methodology.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 145711). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Religion, Nature and Sustainability
intro: An anthropological study of a range of religions, understandings of nature, and practices of sustainability.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 146203). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Practicum II
intro: A written report on a period of practical work experience demonstrating an understanding of process technology in the meat industry and its application.
description: Foundation paper (0 credits, 142019). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Social Change and Environment
intro: Exploration of the theme of social change and environment with reference to world systems and imperialism studied from a historical geography perspective and focusing on New Zealand.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 145214). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Cartography and Data Visualisation
intro: A course in geographic information systems (GIS) focusing on the use of digital geospatial information for cartographic presentation and data visualisation.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 145300). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Working With Geographic Data
intro: An exploration of techniques for the collection, interpretation, analysis and representation of social and environmental geographic data using geographic information systems (GIS).
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 145202). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report English (60)
intro: A supervised and guided independent study.
description: Postgraduate Research Sociology paper (60 credits, 139873). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 2
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work
description: Postgraduate Research paper (45 credits, 145872). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Understanding Cultural Difference
intro: An introduction to key contemporary topics and concepts in the discipline.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 146101). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Consumption and Place
intro: This course explores consumption processes, practices and places. Using a number of themes it encourages students to engage with relationships between production and consumption, cultural and economic change, and matters of identity and ethics.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 145710). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: GIS Programming and Practice
intro: This course focuses on principles and practices of programming within the GIS context.  Issues in GIS project design and implementation are addressed.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 145740). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report: Food
intro: Research in a defined area of Food Science, Technology or Engineering.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 141803). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Dairy Science, Technology and Engineering
intro: Specialised aspects of dairy chemistry, microbiology, process technology, engineering. Project and plant management. Product evaluation, product and process development. Dairy industry structure, strategy, organisation and function. Legislation and safety. A practical course.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 141744). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Geographies of Globalisation
intro: The course explores processes of globalisation emphasising spaces and agents of global change, and global-local connections across a variety of topics.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 145311). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Geographies of Inequality
intro: This course will consider how economic and social inequality is reproduced across space.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 145203). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis
intro: Research in a defined area of Food Science, Technology or Engineering.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (120 credits, 141805). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 141806). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Public Policy
description: Doctoral paper (120 credits, 144900). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Food Engineering Design
intro: Students will design an innovative factory-scale food manufacturing process based on a product specification. The emphasis is on following a formal, systematic methodology that makes appropriate use of both mathematical modelling and empirical data, within a realistic commercial context. Critical evaluation of the design outcome and process from commercial, technical, and professional perspectives is an important component.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 141797). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Innovative Food Design and Development
intro: Students will commercialise an innovative food product from idea generation through to the business case for full-scale manufacture. The emphasis is on following a formal, systematic process that utilizes both qualitative and quantitative analysis techniques, within a realistic commercial context. Critical evaluation of the product development outcome and process from commercial, technical, and professional perspectives is an important component.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 141772). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Practicum I
intro: A written report on a period of practical work demonstrating an understanding of industry structure and also the legislative elements that affect the meat industry.
description: Foundation paper (0 credits, 142018). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Urban Environments
intro: An examination of aspects of the social, economic and political geographies of urban life, drawing upon various approaches in Human Geography.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 145216). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Practice in Human Geography
intro: An introduction to theoretical approaches in human geography, research design and qualitative methodology and techniques. There is a compulsory fieldwork component.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 145301). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 2
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 141807). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Auckland, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Added-Value Processing of Food Products
intro: A study of the latest manufacturing techniques applied to the food industry and their role in satisfying current and future needs for food manufacturers and consumers.  A course designed to integrate food science, process engineering, microbiology and food safety into today's food manufacturing environment with processing practices in order to offer novel methods to formulate foods and assure safety and quality.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 141755). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Food Business Innovation
intro: This course will comprise a series of case studies and business simulations of entire food value chains emphasising ingredient and FMCG information flows, the nature and mechanisms of FMCG retail channels and command of shelf space.  Strategies will be designed for coping with sector dynamics and changing regulatory constraints for innovation dominated by technical or by business process change.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 141725). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 2
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Sociology paper (60 credits, 139817). Prerequisite study in Sociology is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Society, Environment and Place
intro: An introduction to the diversity of human geography focusing on themes such as state and economy; geography of consumption; identity and exclusion; agrifood; migration; geographies of the lifecourse.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 145111). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Special Topic
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 141794). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Food Quality Safety and Innovation
intro: This course will cover risk assessment and management techniques that can be applied to any sector of the food industry, important in the development of a new food product to ensure the development of safe foods of high quality and appropriate cost.  The legal and commercial justification for ingredients, preservation, processing, packaging and distribution technologies will be taught.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 141724). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Food Technology
description: Doctoral paper (120 credits, 141900). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Dairy Products Technical Projects
intro: Acquirement and utilisation of specialised technical knowledge, research skills and competencies through the undertaking of projects pertaining to the investigation and critical analysis of dairy industry structure and its products and processes. A practical course.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 141751). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Resource Conservation and Sustainability
intro: Various distinctly geographical approaches to resource conservation are discussed. Topics include: environmental change, human impact, renewable and non-renewable resource conservation, and the role of the state.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 145213). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Dairy Chemistry
intro: An introduction to milk composition, the underlying chemistry of milk constituents and their properties in dairy ingredients and products. The effects of processing on the chemical properties of milk components, including deleterious reactions impacting milk quality will also be considered.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 141140). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Food Formulation Technology
intro: A study of the physico-chemical properties of food ingredients and their interactions in food systems.  Selection of suitable ingredients in food formulations, in particular, stabilisers, thickeners, gelling agents and emulsifiers.  Understanding of the destabilisation mechanisms of complex food systems in relation to the ingredients used.  A practical course.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 141362). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Nutrition and Food Choice
intro: Nutrient recommendations, nutrition and disease, New Zealand diet, functional foods, food choice, ethical and legal aspects of the food industry response in relation to nutrition claims and concerns.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 141358). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Dairy Processing
intro: An introduction to the processing principles underpinning the handling and treatment of milk, and with particular consideration of the design and specifications of the main processing systems and their integration as part of dairy ingredient and food manufacture.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 141144). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Food Technology Project
intro: An original investigation of a food industry problem or opportunity. The student works under academic supervision within an industrial research brief and learns from practice, systematic skills in problem analysis, research and communication. Consideration of ethical, legal and social environments. This major project integrates knowledge the student has already acquired.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 141759). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Dairy Science and Technology Research Project
intro: An original research project that encourages integration of knowledge and practice of skills gained in the other courses. A rigorous scientific investigation applied to delivering technical solutions to industry defined objectives.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (45 credits, 141848). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Dairy Processing
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 141127). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Strategic Food Product Development
intro: This course provides a strategic overview of the food product development process from the initial new product strategy and its influence on identification of product opportunities through to the actual launch of the finished food product and its influence on the launch strategies and tactics adopted by a company.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 141712). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Food Engineering Research Project
intro: Students apply their problem-solving skills and accumulated knowledge to a specific Food Engineering research problem. This is an individual, scholarly research project conducted under academic supervision. Projects are either sourced from industry or are related to ongoing research and development activities at the university.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 141749). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Food Packaging Engineering and Legislation
intro: The properties of packaging materials and requirements of labelling/legislation and the implications of choice on product shelf life, integration with processing, transport, traceability and information systems, and impact on consumer interaction with the product, sustainability and product cost are explored as part of this course.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 141710). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Dairy Products Technology
intro: Case studies in which the technology and control of the manufacture of appropriate dairy ingredients and products such as cheese, butter, milk powder, casein and whey protein are examined. A practical course.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 141746). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Food Microbiology and Safety
intro: A project-based course aimed at providing the skills and knowledge to select appropriate food processing, storage and testing methods necessary to understand the growth and control of microorganisms to ensure food safety and quality. Specific components of food analysis and risk assessment will be applied to develop analytical and problem solving skills in an industry relevant scenario.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 141311). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Dairy Product Technology
intro: An immersive study of the manufacture of dairy food products, including butter, UHT creams, yogurt, cream cheese and ice cream. Emphasis will be given to the role of formulation-process interactions in defining product properties, notably the functional role of dairy ingredients in related foods. Practical considerations for the characterisation of dairy foods in determining quality, safety and shelf life will also be explored.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 141245). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 2
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Sociology paper (45 credits, 139882). Prerequisite study in Sociology is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Sociology paper (60 credits, 139816). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Industrial Systems Improvement
intro: This course covers: innovation and operations management within food industry production and supply chain systems; design, planning, control and continuous improvement of processes in industrial systems; methods and measures for quality control and daily decision-making in food and related businesses; and leadership and management of teams in the workplace.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 141723). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Dairy Manufacturing Principles
intro:
A project-based course: students will apply their accrued knowledge of dairy 
technology to undertake an industry relevant technical project. Students will be 
required to define project objectives, evaluate relevant supporting literature, 
implement and execute an experimental plan and analyse findings in provision of 
appropriate recommendations and conclusions.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 141247). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Dairy Engineering
intro: An introduction to the key engineering fundamentals relating to the manufacture of dairy ingredients and food products, providing an exploration of thermal, mechanical and energetic principles involved in the handling of milk streams, and the application of these as part of process design and plant operation.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 141141). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Food Characterisation
intro: A project-based course developing the selection and utilisation of food characterisation methodologies in assessment of food/ingredient function, quality and stability. The course will focus on instrumental and sensory methods of assessing structure, appearance, flavour and texture of a variety of food products. Assessment and characterisation tools will be used to develop analytical and problem solving skills in industry relevant scenarios.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 141312). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Food Ingredient Functionality
intro: A course based on the physico-chemical properties of food ingredients impacting on the: (i) stability and sensory properties (technofunctionality) and (ii) health properties (biofunctionality) of foods.   A good understanding of the food destabilization mechanisms, and how to control key food reactions, will be gained.  Special emphasis will be given to dairy products (dispersions, emulsions, foams and gel systems).
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 141715). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Sociology paper (45 credits, 139881). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Emerging Technologies for the Food Industry
intro: In depth case-studies of the principles and modelling of novel food processes, including an appraisal of the advantages and disadvantages compared with established processes. New developments in the preservation of foods, the structuring of foods, the separation of food materials and packaging, storage and handling of foods can be studied.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 141709). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Food Chemistry
intro: A practical approach to the physical, chemical, biochemical and functional properties of major and minor food constituents (water, proteins, carbohydrates, lipids, vitamins, minerals, pigments, flavours, toxins) and food groups (dairy, meat, eggs and plants). Chemical and biochemical reactions causing deterioration in foods and some methods of control. A laboratory course.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 141395). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Food Product Development Principles
intro: The development of new and improved products is a key role of most practising food technologists. This course provides the structured process and tools required for successful product development in the context of an applied project.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 141211). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Food Packaging, Preservation and Storage
intro: Application of a multi-disciplinary approach drawing from microbiology, chemistry, reaction kinetics, process engineering and packaging technology to the development, evaluation and optimisation of preservation processes, packaging technologies, and storage and supply chain systems for fresh and manufactured  food products.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 141708). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Dairy Ingredient Technology
intro: This course continues the study of the key processing operations and handling of milk streams in the specific production of dairy ingredients systems, i.e. milk and protein powders and anhydrous milk fat. Emphasis will be given to the design and manufacture of functional ingredient systems, as well as the optimisation of processing operations for ensuring requisite quality and safety of dairy ingredients.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 141243). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Early Modern Drama: Form and Performance
intro: An advanced study of the drama of Early Modern England and the significant social, artistic, political and religious forces that shaped it. The course will examine plays by Shakespeare and his contemporaries, investigating their material and cultural contexts using critical approaches that highlight questions of dramatic production and reception.
description: Postgraduate Sociology paper (30 credits, 139728). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Dairy Microbiology and Food Safety
intro: An introduction to the role of microbiology in the handling of milk streams and the manufacture of dairy materials. The course will provide an exploration of the classification and identification of bacteria, their reproduction and growth; pathogens and food poisoning micro-organisms; bacteriology of milk and milk products; thermal processes for controlling micro-organisms and the application cleaning-in-place technology.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 141242). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD English
description: Doctoral Sociology paper (120 credits, 139900). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report (30)
intro: A supervised and guided independent study.
description: Postgraduate Sociology paper (30 credits, 139799). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Food Manufacturing Principles
intro: The design, development and on-going operation of manufacturing processes is central to the daily activities of most food technologists. This course explores the key variables that impact the design, development and operation of food manufacturing processes within the context of an applied project.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 141212). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Contemporary New Zealand Writers in an International Context
intro: An advanced exploration of contemporary New Zealand fiction and poetry and its relationship to international aesthetic practices, in the context of globalisation and postcoloniality.
description: Postgraduate Sociology paper (30 credits, 139750). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Postcolonial Theory and Writing
intro: An advanced introduction to theories of postcolonial ethics and identity, and their relationship to selected works of literature from a range of postcolonial contexts.
description: Postgraduate Sociology paper (30 credits, 139758). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Cheese and Whey Technology and Manufacturing
intro: An introduction to the industrial manufacture of cheese, including the transformations of raw materials through processing, microbiological and biochemical transformations and their impact on product quality and properties. The role of technologies associated with the manufacture of whey and other cheese byproducts will also be explored.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 141246). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Postcolonial Literature
intro: A study of late-nineteenth and twentieth century novels in English, from diverse cultures, paying special attention to the ways in which these address the consequences of European colonisation. Recent postcolonial theory will provide a frame for textual analysis.
description: Year 3 Sociology paper (15 credits, 139352). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Literary Revolutions: Romantic and Victorian Literature
intro: A study of literary culture in Britain and the Pacific during the Romantic and Victorian periods. This course explores relationships between poetry, novels, travel writing and political treatises in the period, focusing on the distinctive nature of literary engagements with contemporary social and political upheavals.
description: Postgraduate Sociology paper (30 credits, 139724). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 2
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Physiotherapy paper (45 credits, 134882). Prerequisite study in Physiotherapy is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: The Gothic Imagination

Students gain theoretical and critical concepts and techniques key to process and experimentation notating musical ideas through a diverse range of graphic and natural scores. Students employ strategies of critical reflection, collaboration, and praxis to analyse their situatedness within the social, legal, and industrial landscapes of the contemporary music industries, and engage with the symbiotic relationship between politics and the economy as it shapes their own experiences and professional outlooks.

The course explores a range of contemporary challenges currently facing the music industry in New Zealand and internationally, and examines new and emerging music industry models.

Through exploring theoretical positions to develop tools for the analysis of their own practices, students develop a robust critical understanding of the intersections of music, ethical conduct, and professional practice as these inform their own positionality within creative communities.

description: Year 2 Paramedicine paper (15 credits, 133260). Prerequisite study in Paramedicine is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Musical Interface and Interaction 1
intro: In this course students are introduced to design and representation techniques for musical interaction. Students develop an understanding of how performers and consumers interact with a range of music hardware and software, and apply this knowledge to design and develop new musical instruments, controllers, web and mobile music interfaces and experiences.
description: Year 1 Paramedicine paper (15 credits, 133186). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Music and Sound Engineering 1
intro: In this course students gain a theoretical understanding of the key concepts, techniques and principles of sound. Technical and practical knowledge of sound are applied in studio and live environments to recording and mixing. Practical applications include analogue and digital signal processing, microphone workings and placement, monitoring, and planning and operating live music systems.
description: Year 1 Paramedicine paper (15 credits, 133185). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Vocal Performance and Production
intro: Learn the fundamentals of vocal techniques and production skills relevant to a broad range of styles. Students explore the basic anatomy of the voice, vocal health, and live skills alongside studio techniques and etiquette. The course does not require an audition or existing vocal training, and comprises lectures as well as practical group-based vocal and production workshops.
description: Year 1 Paramedicine paper (15 credits, 133179). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: This course focuses on a particular aspect or aspects of creative arts. Offerings change from year to year and the course may not be offered in a particular year.
description: Year 1 Paramedicine paper (15 credits, 133190). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Creative Arts Special Topic 2B
intro: This course focuses on a particular aspect or aspects of creative arts. Offerings change from year to year and the course may not be offered in a particular year.

intro: In this course students engage in advanced study of their musical practice. Through a combination of instrument specific and mixed ensemble groups, students are supported in achieving an advanced level of technical and artistic skill. Musical materials will be delivered and appropriate to the students musical discipline and chosen genre(s).
description: Year 3 Paramedicine paper (15 credits, 133375). Prerequisite study in Paramedicine is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: In this course students gain practical experiences working with physical computing and DSP to develop a range of new music technologies. Students are also supported in practical workshops to interface with a range of input and output devices with microcontrollers, to develop creative ideas and designs into working prototypes. Students combine software development with hardware and electronics and rapid prototyping tools.
description: Year 2 Paramedicine paper (15 credits, 133281). Prerequisite study in Paramedicine is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Ableton Live
intro: Students will learn techniques for music production on the Albeton Live digital audio workstation and relevant music technologies. Students will gain basic technical and theoretical skills in music production via Ableton Live, as well as practical understanding of how Ableton Live is used in a range of musical applications and contexts.
description: Year 1 Paramedicine paper (15 credits, 133178). Available at: Wellington. Offered: SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Major Project Pre-Production
intro: In this course students generate and evaluate innovative concepts for a music major project and carry out detailed investigation into planning and resourcing requirements along with analysis of cultural, social and economic values of the proposed project. Students work individually and as a team in and across major specialisms to agree the scope and roles of the proposed project.
description: Year 3 Paramedicine paper (15 credits, 133350). Prerequisite study in Paramedicine is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.
