intro: In this course students further develop production and composition techniques to support linear and non-linear musical outcomes. Students compose, arrange and record original sound and music works using a range of tools, technologies, systems, architectures and technical frameworks used for various media platforms.
description: Year 2 Paramedicine paper (15 credits, 133276). Prerequisite study in Paramedicine is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Music Project 2
intro: In this course students apply technical skills and new creative approaches for the development of strategic, sophisticated and unique experiences and works in the music industry. In addition to developing new technical skills, students will apply and critique social, cultural and economic roles and trends associated with the dissemination of unique musical experiences through their creative practice. Multidisciplinary teams will explore innovative creative narrative and communication skills to develop, manage, produce, and iterate musical content, technical production and promotion.
description: Year 2 Paramedicine paper (30 credits, 133253). Prerequisite study in Paramedicine is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: In this course students will be introduced to the concept of artist development from the perspective of management, record labels and music publishers, including their role in discovering, aligning and project-managing artists. Students will also examine these concepts in relation to content and asset creation, digital music distribution, considering issues of quality and product development practices and strategy in New Zealand and internationally.
description: Year 1 Paramedicine paper (15 credits, 133168). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

Students are supported in the development of commercial and experimental outcomes via practical, creative and technical approaches.

intro: In this course students engage in the development of their musical practice. Through a variety of instrument specific and mixed ensemble groups, students will be supported to achieve technical and artistic skills. Musical materials will be appropriate to a range of popular music practices.

title: Professional Practice Studio
intro: The Professional Practice Studio is designed to mobilise the student's prior learning and professional practice capacities in the context of evolving rationalities, crises and prospects of contemporary planning practice. The studio activities blend scholarship, employability, and being Te Tiriti-led with current and comparative planning debates and the role of planning to explore a selected studio theme. Independent projects are evolved through exchanging ideas, exploring possibilities, and evolving a shared responsibility for championing change within a community of practice.
description: Honours Midwifery paper (15 credits, 132419). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Tūhono Taiao: Foundations in Māori Planning
intro: This course introduces students to the Māori Planning world. Students will learn and apply Māori concepts, processes and practices, and critically explore the relationship between Māori and settler-coloniser planning to equip themselves to work competently with Māori communities on Planning issues.
description: Year 3 Midwifery paper (15 credits, 132304). Prerequisite study in Midwifery is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: An interdisciplinary approach to the cultural, philosophical, legal, institutional and practical issues involved in the strategic planning and management of New Zealand's natural heritage, including analysis of appropriate New Zealand and international case-studies.
description: Year 3 Midwifery paper (15 credits, 132305). Prerequisite study in Midwifery is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: An introduction to the role of planning in building sustainable and hazard- and climate-resilient communities that have flourishing futures in the face of disruptive change.
description: Year 2 Midwifery paper (15 credits, 132217). Prerequisite study in Midwifery is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Planning Theory
intro: The philosophical and theoretic foundations of planning and the principles of urban design are identified and analysed using studios and seminars. Business, other disciplines and indigenous approaches to environmental planning challenge traditional views about 'public interest'. The role of planners in collaborative and communicative approaches to urban and environmental planning is identified, along with the effect of jargon in communication. The future of planning, including changes to codes of ethics and practice given a global focus on sustainable management is discussed.
description: Postgraduate Midwifery paper (30 credits, 132732). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.

title: Planning in the Anthropocene: Imagining Sustainable Futures in Times of Uncertainty and Change
intro: A holistic and critical introduction to the concept of "the Anthropocene", its key narratives, and the role of planning in shaping hopeful, decolonised, and sustainable futures in times of uncertainty and rapid change.
description: Year 1 Midwifery paper (15 credits, 132112). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: Students will establish an advanced understanding of Māori planning by exploring key Māori planning concepts in relation to the environment and relevant planning situations.

Students explore basic music media analysis and apply theoretical concepts to the production of online music and video content.

Develop and apply planning processes and tools to assess hazard vulnerability, reduce hazard risks, improve disaster readiness, develop effective response capabilities, and facilitate recovery.
description: Postgraduate Midwifery paper (30 credits, 132751). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

As an integral part of the course, students will critically analyse how this work can be understood in relation to current literature on the SDGs and sustainable development.

title: Environmental and Planning Law in Aotearoa New Zealand
intro: An introduction to Aotearoa New Zealand's Environmental Planning law with a specific focus on the Resource Management Act 1991. We consider the interactions between environmental state law (statutes and common law) and tikanga, as well as the administration of Aotearoa's legal system. The place of law in the sustainability debate, property rights and legal aspects of Te Tiriti o Waitangi are also covered.
description: Year 3 Midwifery paper (15 credits, 132312). Prerequisite study in Midwifery is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: This course provides students with foundational knowledge of planning, the profession (including ethics), and planning processes/planning practice (including plan and policy development, and development control) as mandated under relevant legislation, and applies this knowledge in practice-like settings using emerging/topical planning issue(s).
description: Year 2 Midwifery paper (15 credits, 132212). Prerequisite study in Midwifery is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: This course introduces students to the field of Development Studies and to some of the Big Issues facing people living in the Global South or ‘developing’ countries. We talk about the Big Ideas that influence the approaches taken to try and solve these issues and we illustrate these with case-studies from many parts of the world. The key Big Issues we focus on in this course are Food Security and Hunger and Migration/Refugees.

Attitudes towards disaster preparation are studied together with the nature of disasters and their effects on human beings. The organisation and control of human behaviour during disasters will be studied together with rehabilitation interventions, particularly for the alleviation of stress for those involved.

HFE applies theories, principles, and methods to design systems in diverse environments including work, organisations, products and leisure. This course covers fundamental HFE principles; analytical methods; physical, cognitive and psychosocial capacity and limitations of people.

It will introduce how a knowledge of organic reactions and mechanisms can be used to synthesise biologically relevant molecules, and will include organic and metal-based catalysis as well as heterocyclic chemistry. The laboratory training develops expertise in a range of organic chemistry experimental techniques.

description: Year 3 Chemistry paper (30 credits, 123332). Prerequisite study in Chemistry is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Chemical Energetics
intro: Molecular processes are inherently random and yet we can meaningfully predict the yield or the rate of a chemical reaction. In this course we discover that this apparent paradox is explained by the idea that although single molecules behave randomly, large numbers of molecules and atoms do behave in a predictable manner. We develop the principles of thermodynamics and kinetics from this idea and apply these principles to physical, chemical, biochemical and industrial processes. The lab course focuses on broadly applicable skills in measurement, analysing and presenting physical chemistry data, understanding sources of uncertainty in physical measurements and written communication skills.
description: Year 2 Chemistry paper (15 credits, 123201). Prerequisite study in Chemistry is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Contemporary Topics in Chemistry
intro: We will discuss and analyse the grand challenges and breakthroughs of modern chemistry. This discussion will lead to an understanding of how and why chemistry is central to the most pressing global challenges. Topical questions will vary from year to year. You will learn how to critically evaluate sources of scientific information and present scientific and chemical concepts using different media and to a variety of audiences.
description: Year 3 Chemistry paper (15 credits, 123305). Prerequisite study in Chemistry is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Biochemistry
description: Doctoral Environmental Management paper (120 credits, 122900). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Molecules to Materials
intro: The chemistry of materials under-pins all chemical processing industries. This course facilitates a fundamental understanding of aqueous solutions, organic, inorganic and polymer chemistry relevant to material science, including soft materials such as gels and colloids. The laboratory training develops skills in a range of synthesis, separation and analysis techniques relevant to materials chemistry.
description: Year 2 Chemistry paper (15 credits, 123271). Prerequisite study in Chemistry is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Molecular Chemistry
intro: The world is built up from molecules. Viewing systems - from the human body to the newest satellite - as composites of molecules is a powerful approach to understanding the world. In this course we will deconstruct seemingly complex systems into their simpler molecular components. We will understand their structures and develop ways they can be synthesized from the ground up. This is a lab course focussed on making molecules which will build synthetic skills.
description: Year 2 Chemistry paper (15 credits, 123207). Prerequisite study in Chemistry is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Gene Regulation
intro: The course explores gene regulation in eukaryotes from nuclear organisation to cytosolic control, with a focus on animals and humans. The molecular mechanisms of transcription within a chromatin environment will be discussed, along with the role of RNA processing and post-transcriptional regulation in gene expression, and their importance to health and disease. A lecture and problem-based tutorial course complemented by a hands-on laboratory project undertaking experimental methods used to study gene expression.
description: Year 3 Environmental Management paper (15 credits, 122303). Prerequisite study in Environmental Management is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Chemical Analysis
intro: Determining the structure of matter and how much of it is present is the focus of analytical chemistry. In this course we introduce the chemical and physical principles that underpin modern analytical chemistry methods and illustrate how analytical chemistry plays a key role in fields such as forensic and environmental science. You will obtain hands-on experience with modern analytical instrumentation in the laboratory and use skills in handling chemicals and data analysis to solve problems in analytical chemistry. We develop skills in error analysis and scientific writing.
description: Year 2 Chemistry paper (15 credits, 123208). Prerequisite study in Chemistry is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Chemistry for Modern Sciences
intro: An introductory course in chemistry suitable for students with little previous experience in the subject. The basic concepts of chemistry, including atoms and molecules, the periodic table, bonding, equilibria, chemical reactions, and chemical energetics, are taught with contextual references to everyday life.
description: Year 1 Chemistry paper (15 credits, 123103). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Chemistry and the Physical World
intro: An examination of how the properties of atoms and molecules determine the properties and behaviour of matter. The transfer of energy that occurs during chemical and physical processes and the rates of these processes are discussed and rationalised using atomic and molecular properties. Techniques for characterising matter and materials are introduced.
description: Year 1 Chemistry paper (15 credits, 123105). Prerequisite study in Chemistry is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Chemistry for Biological Systems
intro: Building on basic chemical principles, this course provides the atomic and molecular foundations for understanding chemistry and the life sciences. Starting from the structure of the atom and an understanding of Gibbs energy, it builds a chemical model for bonding, the composition of molecules, non-covalent interactions, chemical equilibria, acids/bases, chemical reactivity, and biological macromolecules. The theory is supported by practical experiments.
description: Year 1 Chemistry paper (15 credits, 123104). Prerequisite study in Chemistry is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Cell Biology
intro: The course explores concepts and methods in advanced molecular cell biology and protein biochemistry. Molecular mechanisms responsible for the signalling and biochemical responses of cells will be discussed with a focus on both cell and protein structure and functions. Advanced studies in protein function, cell signalling and cellular processes are taught in a context of molecular disease mechanisms. The course teaches modern experimental methods in microscopy, cell biology and protein functional analysis.
description: Year 3 Environmental Management paper (15 credits, 122301). Prerequisite study in Environmental Management is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Landscape and Human Ecology
intro: A systems approach to understanding socio-ecological interactions at the landscape level. Content includes fundamental ecological principles, the quantification of ecological services and the importance of recognising the diverse social and cultural considerations that shape socio-ecological systems including Matauranga Māori.
description: Year 2 Geography paper (15 credits, 121214). Prerequisite study in Geography is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Environmental Science paper (60 credits, 120897). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Environmental Management paper (60 credits, 122898). Prerequisite study in Environmental Management is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Environmental Management paper (60 credits, 122897). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Topics in Biochemistry
intro: The course will involve use of the current literature to critically examine the experimental systems used to advance knowledge in Biochemistry.
description: Postgraduate Environmental Management paper (15 credits, 122713). Prerequisite study in Environmental Management is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Genes and Gene Expression
intro: This course covers fundamentals of molecular biology including nucleic acid structure, DNA replication, repair and transcription, with reference to health and disease. Methods used to study and manipulate genes will be covered and applied in practice. A lecture and problem-based course, complemented by practical laboratory experience.
description: Year 2 Environmental Management paper (15 credits, 122231). Prerequisite study in Environmental Management is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Catchment Solutions
intro: The course covers the principles and methodology of river resource management.  It integrates ecology, hydrology, geomorphology and water quality to understand how river ecosystems function and how they may be managed to maximise societal and environmental benefit.
description: Year 3 Geography paper (15 credits, 121313). Prerequisite study in Geography is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Environmental Issues
intro: A consideration of major environmental issues that affect, or may affect natural and built ecosystems into the future. Topics include climate change mitigation and adaptation, the ‘Ozone Hole’, land degradation and soil contamination, increasing water scarcity and pollution, loss of biodiversity and fisheries, sustainability of production and consumption systems, and the sustainability and liveability of major urban centres.
description: Year 2 Geography paper (15 credits, 121210). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Plant Biology
description: Doctoral Environmental Science paper (120 credits, 120900). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: The Molecular Cell
intro: This intermediate level course develops molecular and cellular concepts in biology. The relationship between protein structure and activity with cellular structure and processes is explored. Studies include the life-cycle (translation, trafficking, modification and folding), structure and activity of proteins. The course also investigates the characteristics and functions of key cellular components. The learning activities are centred on lectures and lab classes introducing techniques and concepts in molecular cell biology.
description: Year 2 Environmental Management paper (15 credits, 122201). Prerequisite study in Environmental Management is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Land and Water
intro: How river catchments work and how they are affected by human activity. The environmental effects of land and water management practices, including land use, irrigation, drainage and nutrient loss, on river channels, flows and water quality, framed within an understanding of catchment geomorphology and hydrological processes.
description: Year 2 Geography paper (15 credits, 121213). Prerequisite study in Geography is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Environmental Solutions
intro: A consideration of technology and innovation that can be used to measure, manage and mitigate environmental risks to soil and water. Theory and practical exercises based on New Zealand case studies equip students with numerical skills necessary to monitor and evaluate environmental quality. A student-led project allows in-depth study of a New Zealand environmental issue of particular relevance to community.
description: Year 3 Geography paper (15 credits, 121310). Prerequisite study in Geography is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: The Dynamic Cell
intro: Energy metabolism in higher eukaryotes from the perspective of life on earth and the necessary adaptation of living organisms from an anaerobic to aerobic environment. Carbohydrate, lipid and nitrogen metabolism in the context of health and disease. Integration and regulation of carbohydrate, lipid and amino acid metabolism. A lecture and problem-based tutorial course complemented by a hands-on project-based laboratory course.
description: Year 2 Environmental Management paper (15 credits, 122202). Prerequisite study in Environmental Management is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Biochemistry
intro: An introductory biochemistry course covering the fundamental concepts of protein structure and function as well as metabolism of carbohydrates, lipids and amino acids. The focus will be on energy transactions in a physiological context including glucose homeostasis and muscle metabolism in mammals. A lecture and problem-based tutorial course involving specific case studies will be complemented by a hands-on laboratory course.
description: Year 1 Environmental Management paper (15 credits, 122102). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 2 (60 credits)
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Environmental Science paper (60 credits, 120874). Prerequisite study in Environmental Science is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Topics in Evolutionary Genetics
intro: The course covers the history of landmark discoveries and progression of theory in genetics over the last century.
description: Postgraduate Environmental Science paper (30 credits, 120715). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Plant Improvement
intro: Traditional and modern methods by which plants can be modified to provide new genetic material for use in agriculture, horticulture, forestry and industry. This course links basic and applied science and focuses on how natural and induced genetic variation can be harnessed for human use. Emphasis is on the dramatic progress being made in plant breeding, QTL analysis, marker-assisted selection, tissue culture and recombinant DNA technology. The course includes discussion of environmental, ethical and regulatory issues.
description: Year 3 Environmental Science paper (15 credits, 120306). Prerequisite study in Environmental Science is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Business Administration paper (60 credits, 119890). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Diversity of Plants
intro: This survey of the plant kingdom covers the evolution, diversity, and use of plants. A comparative approach is taken to study the morphological, anatomical, and reproductive features of major plant groups. Both native New Zealand and important agricultural/horticultural plants are used as examples. Modern principles of taxonomy and systematics are described, especially to address current biodiversity and biosecurity issues.
description: Year 2 Environmental Science paper (15 credits, 120201). Prerequisite study in Environmental Science is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Plant, Cell and Environment
intro: This course examines developmental and physiological responses that enable plants to cope with a changing environment and that occur daily, seasonally and over longer timescales; e.g. global climate change. Major topics include plant responses to light, carbon dioxide levels, mineral nutrition and abiotic and biotic stresses such as drought and pests. Emphasis is on mechanisms by which changes in the environment are perceived, signalling processes that are induced, and cellular and developmental changes that help the plant cope with the changed environment.
description: Year 2 Environmental Science paper (15 credits, 120217). Prerequisite study in Environmental Science is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 2
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Business Administration paper (45 credits, 119886). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Special Topic
description: Postgraduate Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119792). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Problem Solving in International Agriculture
intro: Case studies on solving agricultural and horticultural problems around the world, including in New Zealand, with an emphasis on complex, interdisciplinary projects.  Case studies include agricultural development, and incorporation of advanced technologies and environmental mitigation.  Emphasis will be on the application of knowledge of agricultural productions systems to problem solving and communication.
description: Year 3 Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119302). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Methods in Animal, Veterinary and Agricultural Sciences
intro: Research methods for students planning to undertake research in animal, veterinary, agricultural, horticultural or applied sciences, with a focus on experimental research designs. Topics include: philosophy of science; research ethics; literature search and review techniques; project development and management; scientific writing; oral presentation skills; vocabulary of statistics; introduction to quantitative data analysis; and completion of a research proposal.
description: Postgraduate Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119728). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Decision-Making in Primary Industry
intro: A practical approach to the management of strategy, finance and tactics in primary industry. An exploration, through in-field case studies and business analysis, of planning under uncertainty.
description: Year 3 Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119381). Prerequisite study in Business Administration is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Plants and People
intro: Plants as sources of food and beverage, medicine, fibres and dyes, with emphasis on their origin, domestication and the role of plant breeding to improve plants for human use. The physiological effects of active plant compounds on the body. The cultural and geographic origins of commercially important plants.
description: Year 2 Environmental Science paper (15 credits, 120219). Prerequisite study in Environmental Science is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 2
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Business Administration paper (60 credits, 119891). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Plant Diversity
intro: The evolution of plant lineages has many potential outcomes, ranging from extinction to diversification and speciation. This course explores the processes that influence how lineages evolve and how we investigate and interpret patterns of diversity to better understand plant evolution and speciation. Lectures consist of critical discussion of topics including species concepts, local adaptation and diversification, morphological and molecular evolution, mating systems, and the roles of hybridization and polyploidy in plant evolution and speciation.
description: Year 3 Environmental Science paper (15 credits, 120303). Prerequisite study in Environmental Science is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report
description: Postgraduate Research Business Administration paper (60 credits, 119887). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Topics in Agricultural Extension and Consultancy
intro: This course focuses on extension and consultancy theory and its application in commercial farming and horticulture. The design, implementation and evaluation of extension and consultancy programmes; managing an agricultural consultancy business; codes of practice (ethics); current and emerging extension and consultancy issues. Case studies of local agricultural consultancies.
description: Postgraduate Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119731). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Nutrient Management in Arable Systems
intro: An advanced integrated course of study on the environmental and management factors, including soils, establishment systems and fertiliser application, influencing the cycling of nutrients in, and loss of nutrients from, arable production systems. Crop growth models. Crop decision support systems for nutrient management and fertiliser recommendations.
description: Postgraduate Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119711). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Policy, Regulation and Government in Primary Industry
intro: Legal responsibilities and compliance associated with primary production; obligations under Te Tiriti o Waitangi and examples of legislation including resource management, hazardous substances and new organisms, animal welfare, health and safety, food safety, biosecurity and employment. Central and local government, and industry systems. Implications for owners, managers and employees in the rural sector.
description: Year 2 Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119270). Prerequisite study in Business Administration is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Practicum I
intro: Industry experience for at least ten consecutive weeks in a public or private business related to the land-based industries. A descriptive report demonstrating satisfactory observational, analytical and reporting skills is required.
description: Year 1 Business Administration paper (0 credits, 119150). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Integrative Studies
intro: An integrative course designed to emphasise the importance of the broad knowledge base gained in the degree and the value of multidisciplinary teams in addressing the issues and problems of importance to the industries served by the degree. Emphasis will be placed on the application of knowledge to problem-solving, group learning and problem-solving strategies and communication in the context of applied science. Real-world problems will be used.
description: Year 3 Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119373). Prerequisite study in Business Administration is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Agricultural and Horticultural Infrastructure
intro: Overview of agricultural and horticultural properties detailing the structures, electricity supply, water systems, site layout and wastewater management.
description: Year 2 Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119231). Prerequisite study in Business Administration is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Farm and Horticultural Management
intro: Studies related to the principles of farm and/or horticultural management using case studies of farm and/or horticultural businesses; risk analysis and management at the strategic, tactical and operational levels; development of business plans; financial management and the application of whole farm planning techniques.
description: Postgraduate Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119752). Prerequisite study in Business Administration is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Agricultural and Horticultural Systems
intro: An interdisciplinary study of agricultural and Horticultural systems to describe and analyse the relationships between system components. Analysis of the inter-relationships within these systems, including the sub-systems for primary production, processing, marketing and policy, and the relationships of these systems within their environment.
description: Year 2 Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119258). Prerequisite study in Business Administration is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Topics in Epidemiologic Data Analysis
intro: The course focuses on advanced quantitative methods used to analyse data from epidemiologic studies.
description: Postgraduate Research Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118854). Prerequisite study in Human Resources is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Plant Biology
intro: This course is an integrated introductory study of plants. Major themes include: plant form and function (anatomy, morphology, photosynthesis, respiration, transport systems, mineral nutrition); regulation of growth and development, especially in response to the environment; plant diversity (systematics, evolution, life cycles, New Zealand flora); and plants and people (crop domestication, plant breeding and production, Māori plant use).
description: Year 1 Environmental Science paper (15 credits, 120101). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Applied Agriculture Research Report
intro: Candidates will undertake an applied research project within the subject area in which the Master of Agriculture is being completed. A project report must be submitted to and approved by the candidate's supervisor.
description: Postgraduate Research Business Administration paper (60 credits, 119820). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118791). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Methods
intro: Research methods for students whose research focuses on the human-science interface and human applications of science. The course includes foundations of science, ethics, the scientific method, and systems approaches to problem-solving and research; selection of appropriate research methods, including survey methods, case studies, enterprise/project analysis, and modelling; analysis and presentation of research results and communication skills.
description: Postgraduate Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119729). Prerequisite study in Business Administration is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

description: Year 2 Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119292). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.
