intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Human Resources paper (60 credits, 118897). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Farm and Industry Study
intro: An introductory study of farm inputs and products such as meat, milk and wool, and the application of farm practical experience, technical data, and industry procedures and practices, to contemporary issues facing farmers.  Focused both within and beyond the farm gate, this course involves individual and group learning and expression.
description: Year 1 Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119106). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Agriculture and the Environment
intro: Throughout the history of agriculture, it has both provided food security and created environmental challenges. This course will examine both contemporary environmental challenges including agricultural greenhouse gases and climate change, water resources and pollution, soil salinization and erosion, and the impact of natural environmental processes on agriculture.
description: Year 2 Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119203). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Farm Production Systems
intro: An interdisciplinary study of farm systems. Case studies are used to integrate students’ knowledge of the whole farm system, to identify strategic choices and evaluate these choices relative to the current farm system.
description: Year 3 Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119358). Prerequisite study in Business Administration is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report
intro: A detailed examination of a specific topic within the field of study of the candidate, approved by the Chief Supervisor in advance, which may include aspects of original research, problem investigation, and/or study of pre-existing data or published literature.
description: Postgraduate Research Human Resources paper (60 credits, 118811). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Farm Management
intro: An introduction to farm management principles and the application of techniques for planning, implementation, and control of, agricultural systems. A study of New Zealand farms as social, economic and business units. Decision-making about land, labour, and capital, in the context of farming uncertainty. Visits to farms provide case-studies for aspects of farm management
description: Year 1 Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119116). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Canine and Feline Soft Tissue Surgery
intro: The principles and practice of soft tissue surgery in dogs and cats for veterinarians. Pathophysiology, diagnosis and management of soft tissue diseases.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118764). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 2
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Human Resources paper (60 credits, 118898). Prerequisite study in Human Resources is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advances in Ruminant Nutrition for Veterinarians
intro: An advanced course on ruminant nutrition and feeding practices for veterinarians. Principles of microbial digestion in the rumen, absorption and utilisation of nutrients.  Practical nutrition for enhancement of animals’ performance and health, including critical study of the evaluation of feeds, feed intakes and nutrient requirements. Practical assessments of animal and feed resources; ration formulation. Current developments in ruminant feeding technologies, including genetic modification of feeds and rumen flora.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118771). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Māori Value Systems in Science
intro: An introduction to the value systems of Māori, especially as they apply to science, the environment and food products.  Topics covered include whakapapa, tikanga, kaitiakitanga, iwi/hapu/whanau structures and their relationships to present-day systems.  The impacts of Te Tiriti o Waitangi and selected legislation on Māori and science will also be discussed.
description: Year 1 Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119170). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Nutrient Management in Grazed Pasture Systems
intro: An advanced, integrated study of the environmental and management factors influencing the cycling of nutrients in grazed pasture production systems. Contemporary production systems are analysed and strategies devised to maximise the efficiency of nutrient use (including fertiliser recommendations) in pasture and supplementary feed production and animal nutrition. Financial risks associated with farm management strategies that minimise environmental risk are assessed.
description: Postgraduate Business Administration paper (30 credits, 119710). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Opportunity Analysis in Primary Industry
intro: An in-depth study of opportunities for agricultural/horticultural investments. An exploration, through in-field case studies, of alternative pathways to ownership, on-farm investment analysis and farm borrowing and lending. Relevant legislation affecting agricultural and horticultural businesses.
description: Year 3 Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119382). Prerequisite study in Business Administration is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Farm Production Systems
intro: A detailed analysis of whole-farm systems involving a number of field visits. An analytical approach to problem-solving is developed. Field visits to case farms. This course must be passed to qualify for the award of the Diploma in Agriculture
description: Year 1 Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119101). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Practicum II
intro: Industry experience for at least ten consecutive weeks in a public or private business related to the land-based industries. A detailed, investigative report identifying and analysing opportunities, problems, policies and technical and research issues is required.
description: Year 2 Business Administration paper (0 credits, 119250). Prerequisite study in Business Administration is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Profession Practice in Veterinary Science
intro: A supervised placement in an approved setting of veterinary practice. Students will observe the work of experts in the selected area of veterinary practice, undertake some work under supervision, and gain experience in using the empirical literature to guide evidence-based decision making and writing reports in the discipline.
description: Postgraduate Research Human Resources paper (45 credits, 118853). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Applied Veterinary Epidemiology
intro: Students will gain the skills necessary to design an epidemiological study in a clinical setting and investigate an outbreak of disease. Examples and case studies will be drawn from a range of species including production animals, wildlife, aquatic species, companion animals and horses.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118786). Prerequisite study in Human Resources is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Canine and Feline Oncology
intro: The principles of oncology of dogs and cats for veterinary graduates. Pathophysiology, diagnosis and management of neoplastic disease.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118753). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Experience in Agriculture
intro: The course integrates prior discipline knowledge in agriculture with soft skills required by employers by critically evaluating the objectives and operations of agricultural organisations and reflecting on the experience of working in an agricultural organisation.
description: Year 3 Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119303). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: International Agricultural Production Systems
intro: Agriculture provides food, fibre, fuel and other goods for people.  This course covers the evolution of agriculture, the major agricultural systems in the world, including New Zealand, and case studies of selected agricultural production systems covering arable crops, livestock and horticulture in temperate, arid and tropical climates.
description: Year 2 Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119202). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Agribusiness
intro: An introduction to the organisation, structure and governance of New Zealand agribusiness in a global context. Examines the international and domestic macro environment and its impact on New Zealand agribusiness. Explores New Zealand farm systems within an agribusiness context.
description: Year 1 Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119180). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Special Topic
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (60 credits, 118793). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Decision Tools for Primary Industries
intro: Application of decision tools for farm systems analysis at the tactical level.  Analytical frameworks to assist decision-making in finance, human resources, production and marketing.
description: Year 2 Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119281). Prerequisite study in Business Administration is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Plants for Agriculture and Horticulture
intro: An introduction to the agricultural and horticultural species important to New Zealand's economy. Growth and development of agricultural and horticultural plants; their responses to the environment and management, and implications for production. Identification and biology of agricultural and horticultural plants, including weeds, pests and pathogens important to plant production.
description: Year 1 Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119120). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Foundation Farm and Industry Study
intro: A foundation study of farm inputs and products such as meat, milk and fibre, and the application of farm practical experience, technical data, and industry procedures and practices, to contemporary issues facing farmers. Primarily focused within the farm gate, this course involves individual and group learning and expression.
description: Foundation Business Administration paper (15 credits, 119006). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Veterinary Clinical Reproduction and Fertility
intro: An advanced course on clinical reproduction for veterinarians. Comparative veterinary reproductive anatomy, physiology, and technology across a range of species.  In-depth study of reproductive management in a nominated elective species. Use of scientific literature to inform decision making on reproductive issues.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118777). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report (MVM) (45 credit)
intro: A detailed examination of a specific topic within the field of study of the candidate, approved by the course co-ordinator in advance, which may include aspects of original research, problem investigation, and/or study of pre-existing data or published literature.
description: Postgraduate Research Human Resources paper (45 credits, 118851). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Lameness Investigation and Control for Cattle Veterinarians
intro: An advanced course on cattle lameness for veterinarians. This course will provide in-depth study of lameness diagnosis, treatment and control within the herd. It will address the epidemiology of disease, lameness prevention, herd biosecurity, welfare and productivity.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118776). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Mastitis Management for the Production Animal Veterinarian
intro: An advanced course on mastitis for production animal veterinarians. This course will provide in-depth study of mastitis causing agents and their treatment and control within the herd and at an individual level. It will address the epidemiology of disease, mastitis prevention, herd biosecurity, herd/animal health, welfare and productivity.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118775). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Calf Medicine, Health and Management
intro: An advanced course on specific aspects of calf rearing, health management and medicine for veterinarians. A critical study of the systems and environments utilised in calf rearing, nutrient and health requirements with respect to disease prevention, herd biosecurity, health, welfare and productivity.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118772). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Human Resources paper (120 credits, 118899). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Special Topic
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (30 credits, 118792). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Veterinary Science
description: Doctoral Human Resources paper (120 credits, 118900). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report (MVM) (60 credit)
intro: A detailed examination of a specific topic within the field of study of the candidate, approved by the course co-ordinator in advance, which may include aspects of original research, problem investigation, and/or study of pre-existing data or published literature.
description: Postgraduate Research Human Resources paper (60 credits, 118852). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Cardiorespiratory Medicine for Small Animal Veterinarians
intro: The principles and practice of cardiorespiratory medicine in dogs and cats for veterinary graduates. Pathophysiology, diagnosis and management of cardiac and respiratory disease.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118757). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Ophthalmology in Small Animal Practice
intro: The principles of ophthalmology in dogs and cats for veterinary graduates.  Pathophysiology, diagnosis and management of diseases of the eye.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118759). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Studies in Veterinary Practice II
intro: An advanced course in a selected discipline of veterinary practice that includes supervised investigation, diagnosis and management of complex cases. Students will source and critically evaluate existing knowledge to make evidence-based clinical decisions; will integrate theory with critical reflection of practice; and will apply and synthesise advanced knowledge to evaluate and manage complex clinical cases.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (30 credits, 118713). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Clinical Pathology in Small Animal Practice
intro: The principles of clinical pathology in dogs and cats for veterinary graduates.  It includes the indications, application and interpretation of haematological, biochemical, urine, cytological and special testing of dogs and cats.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118755). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Canine and Feline Orthopedic Surgery
intro: The principles and practice of orthopaedic surgery in dogs and cats for veterinarians.  Pathophysiology, diagnosis and management of orthopaedic diseases.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118763). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Evidence-Based Practice
intro: Students will study the principles of veterinary epidemiology to provide them with the skills necessary to critically evaluate the literature for the purposes of evidence-based medicine. Examples and case studies will be drawn from a range of species including production animals, wildlife, companion animals and horses.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118785). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Animal Science
description: Doctoral Management paper (120 credits, 117900). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report
description: Postgraduate Management paper (30 credits, 117799). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Dermatology for Small Animal Veterinarians
intro: The principles of dermatology in dogs and cats for veterinarians. Pathophysiology, diagnosis and management of skin diseases.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118762). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Analysis of Epidemiologic Data
intro: This course provides students with the basic skills necessary to undertake analysis of data in the health and biosecurity context. Throughout the course students will work with data collected during epidemiological studies (complete with missing data) and will learn how to interpret results in the light of bias, confounding, effect modification and model error.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (30 credits, 118716). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Canine and Feline Endocrinology
intro: The principles of endocrinology of dogs and cats for veterinary graduates.  Pathophysiology, diagnosis and management of diseases of the endocrine glands.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118752). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Veterinary Practice I
intro: An advanced course in a specified discipline of veterinary practice that includes supervised investigation, interpretation, diagnosis and management of cases. Students will develop a thorough understanding of knowledge within the discipline; the ability to source and critically evaluate existing knowledge to make evidence-based clinical decisions; and the ability to integrate theory with critical reflection of practice.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (60 credits, 118710). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 2 (60 credits)
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Management paper (60 credits, 117874). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Spatial and Temporal Analysis of Epidemiologic Data
intro: This course provides students with the basic skills necessary to undertake the analysis of epidemiologic data that is clustered in space and time. Throughout the course students will work with data collected via epidemiologic studies or as part of surveillance and will learn how to visualise spatial and temporal patterns, describe and quantify those spatial and temporal patterns and explain the observed patterns.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118706). Prerequisite study in Human Resources is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Avian Medicine
intro: The principles and practice of avian medicine in companion birds and aviary collections, for veterinarians.  Pathophysiology, diagnosis and management of diseases of birds.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118758). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Veterinary Public Health
intro: The application of veterinary science to the promotion of human health. Interrelations between agricultural practices and the environment significant to animal and human health.  Food safety including undesirable residues and the HACCP concept.  Food-borne diseases, zoonoses and emergence of diseases from animal reservoirs.  The application of epidemiological principles to the investigation, prevention and control of diseases.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (60 credits, 118726). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 2
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Management paper (60 credits, 117898). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Management paper (120 credits, 117899). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Canine and Feline Neurology
intro: The principles of neurology in dogs and cats for veterinary graduates.  Pathophysiology, diagnosis and management of diseases of the nervous system.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118756). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Studies in Animal Production
intro: Advanced studies of major physiological systems and functions in animals and their relevance to animal production. These studies can be undertaken in any of the following topics: nutrition, growth, breeding, genetics, animal welfare and equine science.
description: Postgraduate Management paper (15 credits, 117785). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Metabolism and Endocrinology
intro: An advanced course in the metabolism of carbohydrate, fat and protein in ruminants and/or non-ruminants. Endocrine control and nutrient flows in various physiological states.
description: Postgraduate Management paper (15 credits, 117768). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report
description: Postgraduate Research Management paper (60 credits, 117887). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Diagnostic Imaging for Small Animal Veterinarians
intro: The principles of diagnostic imaging in small animal practice for veterinary graduates. Principles of radiography and other imaging modalities. Radiologic interpretation of all body systems, primarily of dogs and cats, and integration with case management.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118754). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Veterinary Practice II
intro: An advanced course in a selected discipline of veterinary practice that includes supervised investigation, interpretation, diagnosis and management of complex cases. Students will develop an advanced understanding of knowledge within the discipline; apply advanced diagnostic and technical skills; and critically reflect on their own practice and identify knowledge gaps.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (60 credits, 118711). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Nutrition, Growth and Meat Science
intro: An advanced course in the physiology and application of nutrition, growth and meat science in domestic animal species
description: Postgraduate Management paper (15 credits, 117711). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Studies in Veterinary Practice I
intro: An advanced course in a specified discipline of veterinary practice that includes supervised investigation, diagnosis and management of cases within the discipline. Students will develop a thorough knowledge of principles and practice in the relevant discipline, including the ability to make evidence-based clinical decisions.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (30 credits, 118712). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Current Topics in Epidemiological Methods and Data Analysis
intro: An in-depth view of topics in epidemiology. Students will focus on issues related to the design and management of observational studies and learn one or more multivariable modelling approach for non-linear data.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118708). Prerequisite study in Human Resources is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Canine and Feline Gastroenterology
intro: The principles of gastroenterology of dogs and cats for veterinary graduates.  Pathophysiology, diagnosis and management of diseases of the alimentary system.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118751). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 1 (30 credits)
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Management paper (30 credits, 117873). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Genetics and Breeding
intro: An advanced course in animal breeding and genetics designed to introduce aspects of breeding value estimation, index selection and maternal effects across herd/flock genetic evaluations, genotype by an environment interaction, major genes, applied molecular genetics, inbreeding, crossbreeding, physiological genetics and genomic selection.
description: Postgraduate Management paper (15 credits, 117765). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Studies in Animal Production
intro: Advanced studies of major physiological systems and functions in animals and their relevance to animal production. These studies can be undertaken in any of the following topics: nutrition, growth, breeding, genetics, animal welfare and equine science.
description: Postgraduate Management paper (30 credits, 117786). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Management paper (60 credits, 117897). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Decision Making with Animal Health Data
intro: This course covers topics related to utilising health and economic data to make decisions around the control of animal health. The focus is on applying the principles of production monitoring, information management and decision-making processes at farm level in a practical way. A broader context is provided of the structure and operation of veterinary services, industry-level process control, and the importance of animal health economics.
description: Postgraduate Human Resources paper (15 credits, 118705). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90 Credit Part 2
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Management paper (45 credits, 117872). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Reproduction, Lactation and Fertility
intro: An advanced course in the physiology and application of reproduction, lactation and fertility in domestic animal species
description: Postgraduate Management paper (15 credits, 117710). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Biometrics for the Animal and Nutritional Sciences
intro:
Study in applied statistics with emphasis on animal and nutritional sciences. Introduction to and extensive use of statistical packages, regression and multiple regressions analysis, analysis of variance of standard experimental designs, 
covariance analysis and general linear models
description: Postgraduate Management paper (15 credits, 117709). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Animal Production
intro: The impacts of breeding, nutrition, reproduction and lactation in New Zealand animal production. A modularised course requiring students to select three topics from dairy cattle production, animal, fish & insect protein production, sheep production, pig & poultry production, beef cattle production and the working dog.
description: Year 3 Management paper (15 credits, 117371). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Animal Metabolism
intro: A study of animal metabolism at the whole-body level. The emphasis will be on integrative aspects of metabolism, including exploring nutrient and energy flow from the food to organs, tissues and products. Mechanisms controlling body metabolism in general and the intrinsic and extrinsic factors affecting them will be emphasised.
description: Year 3 Management paper (15 credits, 117348). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Genetics for Livestock Improvement
intro: The relative influence of genetic and environmental factors on quantitative traits. Methods of calculating breeding values or indices to exploit genetic variation in quantitative traits. The use of computers to utilise pedigree and performance records. Selection for disease resistance, the use of new reproductive technologies to enhance genetic gain and the potential importance of molecular genetics in livestock improvement. Maternal influences and methods of selecting for maternal components. Crossbreeding and inbreeding as selection tools.
description: Year 3 Management paper (15 credits, 117345). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Solving Problems in Animal Production
intro: The critical evaluation of specific aspects of breeding, nutrition, reproduction, lactation, meat/growth and health on animal production and the design and development of improved production systems, focusing on the underlying science.
description: Year 3 Management paper (15 credits, 117381). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Animal Growth and Meat Production
intro: Patterns of growth and development of farm animals will be described with emphasis on those characteristics of growth with economic implications for meat production, such as the rate, efficiency and composition of growth. Structural and functional aspects of muscle, bone and fat as they relate to animal well-being as well as carcass and meat quality will be considered. In describing factors that can affect animal growth, carcass composition and meat quality, particular attention will be given to on-farm factors.
description: Year 3 Management paper (15 credits, 117344). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Companion Animal Science
intro: This course is an integration of the scientific and theoretical aspects of companion animal science. The main topics covered include the nutrition, behaviour, welfare, breeding and genetics of dogs, cats and horses. The emphasis will be placed on the scientific foundation for the promotion of animal welfare and longevity.
description: Year 3 Management paper (15 credits, 117361). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Animal Reproduction and Lactational Physiology
intro: An in-depth study of aspects of the physiology of reproduction and lactation in monogastric and ruminant animals.  The emphasis is placed on applying the understanding of the physiological processes to practical animal systems and will involve practical classes in which technologies related to animal reproduction and milk production are demonstrated.  Students will advance their skills in reading and evaluating scientific literature, develop skills in analysing problems and in constructing a logical argument.
description: Year 3 Management paper (15 credits, 117343). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Animal Reproduction and Lactation in Livestock
intro: A study of reproductive and lactational physiology in livestock with emphasis on identifying possible means of altering those processes in production animals.  The course will include exploration of the anatomy and histology of the gonads, placenta and mammary gland.
description: Year 2 Management paper (15 credits, 117243). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Dilemmas in Animal Welfare
intro: Exploration of animal welfare as one of the various factors that influence our use of, and interactions with, animals. Integration of legal, scientific, ethical and practical considerations for making real-world improvements in animal welfare. Emphasis on student interaction with various stakeholders to define real-world animal welfare problems and explore solutions.
description: Year 3 Management paper (15 credits, 117331). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Principles of Animal Breeding and Genetics
intro: An introduction of the basic principles of using bioinformatics to find genes.  The principles to enable the calculation of genetic parameters, such as heritability.  An introduction to the prediction of breeding values and the calculation of selection indices and genomic selection.
description: Year 2 Management paper (15 credits, 117245). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Animal Welfare Science
intro: Understanding animal welfare is important to optimise animals’ health and productivity, to improve their quality of life and to safeguard the reputation of New Zealand’s animal production industries. This course explores how measurements of animal behaviour and physiology can be used to understand mental experiences such as pain, breathlessness, thirst, nausea and fear and thus the welfare states of domestic animals. These concepts will be applied to enable students to construct robust strategies for practical assessment of animal welfare in various production systems.
description: Year 3 Management paper (15 credits, 117332). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Performance Animal Nutrition
intro: Develop understanding of the important drivers underpinning the relationship between nutrition and performance, and how these can be maximized to achieve the desired performance outcomes.  Knowledge will be gained on the major animal nutrition and performance issues facing New Zealand working and sport animals.
description: Year 2 Management paper (15 credits, 117226). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Ruminant Animal Nutrition
intro: Advanced principles of animal nutrition as related to ruminant livestock. An in-depth coverage of the processes of feed intake, digestion, absorption and metabolism, and the factors affecting them. The determination of the nutritive value of feedstuffs for ruminant, with particular emphasis on forages. Gain in-depth knowledge of schemes for estimating the ruminant animal requirements for energy, protein and minerals and the principles of ration formulation. Design practical feeding regimes and their implementation.
description: Year 3 Management paper (15 credits, 117303). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Animal Feed Science and Technology
intro: An examination of feedstuffs and their physical and chemical characteristics and nutritive values. The exploration, through appropriate examples of the different feed preservation processes and feed processing technology employed in animal production.
description: Year 2 Management paper (15 credits, 117224). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: The Research Proposal
intro: Students will be introduced to the components and requirements of a research proposal that will support progression into Part B of the DBA and the undertaking of their DBA research
description: Doctoral Business paper (30 credits, 115910). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 90-Credit Part 2
intro: Candidates are required to conduct a piece of independent research under supervision and to produce a thesis for examination.
description: Postgraduate Research Business paper (45 credits, 115892). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Animal Production and Health
intro: An overview of the sheep, dairy and beef cattle industries in New Zealand, and an introduction to the deer and intensive livestock industries.  Topics covered include: principles of reproduction, genetics, nutrition, welfare and health of farmed livestock.
description: Foundation Management paper (15 credits, 117006). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Applied Analytics Project Part 2
intro: Under the supervision of academic staff, students work with an external organisation on the application of computer-based analytics tools to a project in the domain of business analytics, healthcare system analytics, or public policy analytics. Special attention is given to privacy and ethical considerations, and to the (visual) communication of results.
description: Postgraduate Research Business paper (30 credits, 115803). Prerequisite study in Business is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Animal Biosecurity and Health
intro: The importance of biosecurity to New Zealand and its relationship to human and animal health, and farm productivity will be highlighted. Identification, prevention and treatment of selected companion and production animal diseases in New Zealand.  Knowledge of biosecurity will be used to develop biosecurity plans for animals with recent biosecurity failures used to illustrate concepts.
description: Year 3 Management paper (15 credits, 117301). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Animal Production through to Carcass Classification
intro: Current and future practices for meat production from farm to carcasses leaving the slaughter floor. Topics include animal growth and development, genetics and breeding, on-farm opportunities to influence carcass and meat quality, pre-slaughter handling of meat-producing animals, slaughter and carcass dressing procedures, carcass classification, animal welfare, occupational safety and health, and factory farming.
description: Year 2 Management paper (15 credits, 117225). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Livestock Production Science
intro: Developing understanding of the important drivers underpinning New Zealand’s livestock and companion animal industries and how these can be altered to achieve the desired outcomes.  Knowledge will be gained on the major animal welfare and environmental issues facing New Zealand agriculture and how these might be mitigated against.
description: Year 2 Management paper (15 credits, 117201). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Writing a Literature Review
intro: Students will initially be introduced to the purpose and structure of a literature review and its role in the identification and specification of a research problem. Argumentative and critical writing are covered.
description: Doctoral Business paper (30 credits, 115903). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Monogastric Animal Nutrition
intro: The principles of monogastric nutrition are developed and applied to improve livestock productivity. An in-depth coverage of the digestive and absorptive processes and factors which affect them. The utilisation of absorbed nutrients is addressed, particularly in relation to the determination of nutrient requirements. Techniques central to the study of nutrition are emphasised. The science of nutrition is related back to the practice of feeding animals to optimise productivity and wellbeing. Students will be become familiar with nutrients partitioning computer models used in a practical context.
description: Year 3 Management paper (15 credits, 117302). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Animal Science
intro: An exploration of scientific principles underpinning animal husbandry practices. Emphasis will be on examining biological systems in relevant animals, how they are purposely or inadvertently manipulated, and the impact of these manipulations on production, welfare, and the environment.
description: Year 2 Management paper (15 credits, 117202). Prerequisite study in Management is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Business Sustainability
intro: This course examines business practices that make organisations sustainable and resilient, with a particular focus on how to balance continuous innovation with good governance and risk management.
description: Postgraduate Business paper (15 credits, 115792). Available at: Auckland. Offered: S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Critical and Contextual Thinking Skills for Postgraduate Study
intro: This course aims to enable students to develop the critical and analytical thinking skills essential for studying at the final year of undergraduate and at postgraduate level. Students will learn to critically engage with academic reading material to examine and evaluate the argument of others. Students will learn to identify the difference between critical analysis and description and be able to better formulate an argument effectively in their own writing. Students will participate in interactive workshops to develop confidence in their ability to apply critical thinking strategies.  Students will analyse differing viewpoints, challenge the views of others and clearly communicate their own position.

title: Skills and Communication for Academic Success
intro:
This course develops many of the thinking, writing and oral presentation skills that students need for success in academic studies and in employment.

The course develops competence in the skills essential to writing for assessment, including summarising texts, utilising appropriate sources and evidence, critiquing arguments, taking notes, and referencing. At the same time, students will be introduced to practices and approaches that contribute to successful academic and workplace environments, including: writing summaries, analysing problems, and using reflection as a tool for professional development. They will also learn how to deliver a persuasive oral presentation.

description: Year 2 Marketing paper (15 credits, 114241). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Organisational Behaviour
intro: A theoretical and practical examination of the behaviour of people in the work environment. Students develop a basic understanding of individual behaviour and team dynamics, exploring issues of motivation, communication, leadership, decision making, power and organisational change.
description: Year 2 Marketing paper (15 credits, 114240). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Food and Agribusiness Value Chains
intro: The study of the flow of food and agricultural products from the farm to the final consumer. Emphasis on successful value chain management applied to food and agribusiness. The course utilizes field trips to study local value chains.
description: Year 2 Finance paper (15 credits, 112248). Prerequisite study in Finance is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Financial Accounting and Reporting
intro: Theory and application of current financial reporting standards in contemporary business.
description: Postgraduate Research Accounting paper (15 credits, 110804). Prerequisite study in Accounting is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Financial Accounting
intro: A comprehensive study of financial reporting, including current New Zealand GAAP and recognition of revenue, assets and liabilities.
description: Postgraduate Accounting paper (15 credits, 110702). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Cooperative Governance and Management
intro: An advanced study of the theory and practices of cooperatives and mutuals. Emphasis is placed on the governance and management of cooperatives and their subsequent performance in meeting the  demands placed on them through shareholder/supplier or shareholder/buyer complexities.  The competitiveness of cooperatives in a global environment is explored through appropriate case studies.
description: Postgraduate Finance paper (15 credits, 112743). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Decent Work
intro: An exploration of the principles, practices and issues surrounding decent work in contemporary work organisations.
description: Year 3 Marketing paper (15 credits, 114332). Prerequisite study in Marketing is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Contemporary Work and HRM
intro: An exploration of contemporary trends and issues relating to work, employment, human resource management, and employment relations in national (NZ) and international contexts.
description: Year 3 Marketing paper (15 credits, 114331). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Strategic Management Accounting
intro: Strategic applications of cost and management accounting tools and techniques.
description: Postgraduate Research Accounting paper (15 credits, 110805). Prerequisite study in Accounting is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Taxation
intro: A study of advanced topics and contemporary issues in taxation, including tax policy, international taxation and tax administration.
description: Year 3 Accounting paper (15 credits, 110389). Prerequisite study in Accounting is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Accounting
description: Doctoral Accounting paper (120 credits, 110900). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Practice
intro: Candidates will undertake professional practice within the subject area in which the Master of Management is being completed. Professional Practice may be conducted either individually or in groups and assessment of the outcome of the investigation may be in a variety of ways including written and oral presentations and case studies.
description: Postgraduate Research Finance paper (60 credits, 112896). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Accounting Systems
intro: Accounting systems and the role of accountants in a systems environment.
description: Postgraduate Accounting paper (15 credits, 110701). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Auditing
intro: An introduction to the basic principles of auditing, including the function of audits and the concepts that govern audit processes and practices.
description: Year 2 Accounting paper (15 credits, 110279). Prerequisite study in Accounting is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Accounting Information Systems
intro: A study of systems for the measurement and reporting of information for internal and external decision-making, including the emerging technologies that lend support for this. The accountant’s role in the formulation, design, implementation and management of these systems, including risk management and controls will also be addressed.
description: Year 2 Accounting paper (15 credits, 110249). Prerequisite study in Accounting is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: International Food and Agribusiness Strategies
intro: Contemporary New Zealand and international agribusiness issues and strategies.  Emphasis is placed on the dynamics of agribusiness strategies, structures and institutions in the context of agricultural trade and environmental issues, food and fibre markets and supply chains.  Case studies are used extensively throughout the course.
description: Postgraduate Finance paper (15 credits, 112747). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research Accounting paper (60 credits, 110897). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: International Agri-Food Marketing Strategies
intro: An advanced study of international agri-food business and marketing. Emphasis is placed on international consumers and agri-food exports, including external environment, terms of trade, transportation, packaging, documentation, methods of payment, risk management, intercultural communication and negotiation.
description: Postgraduate Finance paper (15 credits, 112702). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Auditing
intro: A study of advanced topics and contemporary issues in auditing.
description: Year 3 Accounting paper (15 credits, 110379). Prerequisite study in Accounting is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Forensic Accounting
intro: A study of forensic accounting, fraud auditing and investigation.
description: Year 3 Accounting paper (15 credits, 110369). Prerequisite study in Accounting is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Farm Management
intro: An introduction to land based production systems. Particular emphasis is placed on the unique characteristics of farm/horticulture businesses and the tools and frameworks by which to assess them. This course is designed for students who do not have prior tertiary training in farm management and is relevant for those with farming experience.
description: Postgraduate Finance paper (30 credits, 112701). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: International Food and Agribusiness Strategies
intro: Application of a broad range of business concepts - economic, financial and marketing - to international agri-food business and marketing. Emphasis is placed on international consumers and agri-food exports, including external environment, terms of trade, transportation, packaging, documentation, methods of payment, risk management and negotiation.
description: Year 3 Finance paper (15 credits, 112301). Prerequisite study in Finance is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Accounting Theory
intro: This course is designed for students who wish to continue their search for truth in accounting at an advanced level. They will be introduced to various conceptual issues espoused in the accounting literature such as the historical development of accounting, the relationship between philosophy of science and accounting, and the theoretical foundation of some of the important issues currently facing accounting as a profession.
description: Postgraduate Accounting paper (30 credits, 110711). Prerequisite study in Accounting is required. Available at: Auckland. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Employment Relations
intro: An exploration of the theoretical frameworks used to analyse employment relations, including institutional processes and practices crucial to the workings of contemporary employment relations in New Zealand.
description: Year 2 Marketing paper (15 credits, 114254). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Auditing and Assurance
intro: The role of external auditing and assurance including the application of professional, ethical and technical requirements, and current auditing research issues.
description: Postgraduate Research Accounting paper (15 credits, 110806). Prerequisite study in Accounting is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Methods in Accounting
intro: This course explores a range of different conceptual approaches to researching accounting issues and examines tools and techniques that are available for research in accounting. As an integral part of the course, students are required to prepare and present a research proposal.
description: Postgraduate Accounting paper (30 credits, 110717). Available at: Auckland. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Report
intro: Candidates are required to conduct a piece of independent research under supervision and to produce a report of the research for examination.
description: Postgraduate Research Accounting paper (60 credits, 110894). Available at: Auckland. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Management Accounting
intro: A study of advanced topics and contemporary issues in management accounting in a range of industry settings, including strategic management and performance management.
description: Year 3 Accounting paper (15 credits, 110329). Prerequisite study in Accounting is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Food and Agribusiness Strategies
intro: Extensive use of food industry and agribusiness case studies. Emphasis is placed on strategic analysis and decision making applied to New Zealand and international agribusiness
description: Year 3 Finance paper (15 credits, 112302). Prerequisite study in Finance is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Tax
intro: A comprehensive examination of key aspects of New Zealand's tax regime from a domestic and international perspective.
description: Postgraduate Research Accounting paper (15 credits, 110807). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Contemporary Issues in Financial Accounting
intro: An in-depth study of selected contemporary financial accounting and external reporting issues through a review of the relevant extant literature.
description: Postgraduate Accounting paper (30 credits, 110710). Prerequisite study in Accounting is required. Available at: Auckland. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Taxation
intro: An introduction to the basic principles of taxation, the elements of taxation and the concepts that govern tax practice. The course will also provide an awareness of the economic implications of taxation at both local and global levels.
description: Year 2 Accounting paper (15 credits, 110289). Prerequisite study in Accounting is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Financial Accounting
intro: A study of advanced financial accounting topics and contemporary professional and conceptual issues relevant to financial reporting.
description: Year 3 Accounting paper (15 credits, 110309). Prerequisite study in Accounting is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introductory Financial Accounting
intro: An introduction to measurement concepts in accounting and to the preparation and presentation of financial statements and reports, including the compilation of primary financial data.
description: Year 1 Accounting paper (15 credits, 110109). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Integrative Accounting
intro: An integrated study of accounting sub-disciplines and other related disciplines to create an appreciation of how accounting operates in practice. Emphasis will also be placed on integration of expanded competencies with knowledge-based skills.
description: Year 3 Accounting paper (15 credits, 110303). Prerequisite study in Accounting is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Electronic Circuits
intro: This course covers the designing and building of advanced electronic circuits and systems. The approach to design is through advanced application of sensor interfacing, instrumentation and low-noise amplifiers, oscillators and filters, MOSFET circuits, power amplifiers, and power supply design.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 281780). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Physics Fundamentals
intro: Physics Fundamentals is an introductory course which will equip you with the knowledge of basic physics concepts. The course covers the foundational themes of motion, forces, energy, electricity, fluids and waves in preparation for entry into further studies.
description: Foundation Foundation Studies paper (15 credits, 247023). Available at: Auckland. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: General Mathematics
intro: This course equips students with knowledge of mathematical concepts and tools to foster their problem-solving skills. This unit covers the foundational themes of algebra, consumer and financial arithmetic and statistics to give students a preparation for entry into further studies, particularly in business disciplines.
description: Foundation Foundation Studies paper (15 credits, 247024). Available at: Auckland. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Mathematics Fundamentals
intro: Mathematics Fundamentals is a foundational course which will develop the core mathematical skills necessary for many of the 100-level science courses that require fundamental algebraic fluency and statistical literacy. Your studies will combine fundamental mathematical and statistical methods designed to provide highly sought-after skills in mathematical reasoning, problem solving and numeracy.
description: Foundation Foundation Studies paper (15 credits, 247022). Available at: Auckland. Offered: S1 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Biology Fundamentals
intro: Biology Fundamentals is an introductory course which will equip you with the knowledge of basic biology concepts. The course covers the foundational themes of life processes, ecology, and evolution in preparation for entry into further studies.
description: Foundation Foundation Studies paper (15 credits, 247020). Available at: Auckland. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Chemistry Fundamentals
intro: Chemistry Fundamentals is an introductory course which will equip you with the knowledge of basic chemistry concepts in preparation for entry into further studies.  The course covers the foundational themes of the structure, properties and changes of matter, and how chemistry is used in society.
description: Foundation Foundation Studies paper (15 credits, 247021). Available at: Auckland. Offered: S1 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: International Communication
intro: This course explores the role of media systems in shaping international politics, security, societal activism, and trans-border business and cultural practices.
description: Year 2 English Literature paper (15 credits, 219213). Prerequisite study in English Literature is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Intermediate Financial Accounting
intro: A detailed study of financial reporting, with particular emphasis on corporate regulatory requirements, both professional and statutory.
description: Year 2 Accounting paper (15 credits, 110209). Prerequisite study in Accounting is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Data Analytics for Accounting and Business
intro: Develop an ability to use business data and analytics to gain insights that will improve current accounting and business decisions.
description: Year 3 Accounting paper (15 credits, 110304). Prerequisite study in Accounting is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Management Accounting
intro: A study of cost accumulation and allocation processes used for internal routine reporting to managers for cost management and operational planning and control; internal non-routine reporting to managers for strategic and tactical decisions; inventory valuation and income determination for external reporting.  Where appropriate, examples and applications in primary industries and the public sector will also be considered.
description: Year 2 Accounting paper (15 credits, 110229). Prerequisite study in Accounting is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Portfolio I - Global Management
intro: A collection of reflective evidence-based learnings in contemporary work environment/s.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 295703). Available at: Auckland. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Portfolio II - Global Leadership
intro: A collection of reflective evidence-based learnings in relation to leadership practice in contemporary work environment/s.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 295704). Available at: Auckland. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Marautanga o Te Aho Matua I
intro: He tīmatanga tēnei ki te wherawhera i ngā ariā me ngā āhuatanga ako, me te whakaako ki ngā akoranga ā tini e hangai pū ki ngā marautanga e whakaakohia ana i roto i ngā kura tuatahi tae atu hoki ki ngā kura taiao Māori. Mā te ako i ngā āhuatanga e pā ana ki ngā Pou Toi e whai kiko, e whai tikanga, e whakatinana ai ēnei āhuatanga katoa.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 268132). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Taiao Mō Te Aho Matua o Ngā Kura Kaupapa Māori I
intro:
He timatanga ki te titiro ki te tū ngaiotanga o te kaiako, ki ngā akoranga taiao Māori, whakahaere ā-akomanga, te ako takitahi, ā-rōpū, ā-akomanga, ā-marautanga hangai pū ki Te Aho Matua.

An introduction to the professional role of the teacher with reference to Te Aho Matua and Māori medium professional practices, classroom organisation, learning of individuals and groups, learning environments and key curriculum areas.
description: Year 1 paper (30 credits, 268106). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Interpersonal Communication
intro: This course examines the importance and practice of building thriving communities and relationships, both in and out of the workplace. Students will examine how communication is used to connect with others, to express and uphold identities, and to manage challenging interactions and relationships.
description: Year 2 English Literature paper (15 credits, 219211). Prerequisite study in English Literature is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Global Communication
intro: This course examines the role of communication in diverse global contexts, the interplays between global processes and transformations in communication, and the role of communication in addressing contemporary global business challenges.
description: Year 2 English Literature paper (15 credits, 219212). Prerequisite study in English Literature is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Mathematics and Structural Principles
intro: The course is designed to improve student competency in construction-based mathematical calculations and tasks. It introduces basic structural principles and computations for structural integrity in building elements.
description: Year 1 Creative Writing paper (15 credits, 218104). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Applied Geography Project
intro: Engagement in a self-directed geography project with supervision.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 145790). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Whakamahi: Introduction to Mental Health and Addiction Placement
intro: A 75-100 hour supervised placement in a mental health and addiction service, with a focus on understanding and illustrating the Foundation (Essential) Competencies outlined in the Drug and Alcohol Practitioners Association of Aotearoa New Zealand (DAPAANZ) Addiction Intervention Competency Framework.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 147204). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Food Quality
intro: Critical reviews, case studies, advanced study and/or research into selected aspects of food quality.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 141720). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Applied Global Governance and Responsibility
intro: A reconciliation of global best practices in governance and ethical decision-making with corporate reality, and including a practical assessment of boards in varied organizational structures and settings.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 295702). Available at: Auckland. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Managing Financial Decisions
intro: Critical examination of the context, process, and implications of integrated management of financial decisions at an executive level.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 295701). Available at: Auckland, N. Offered: S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Audio
intro: Gain an advanced knowledge and understanding of creative and technical processes, including sound and music recording, editing, and mixing. Students will also advance their knowledge of audio production for interactive platforms.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 289341). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Studio and Location Audio
intro: Gain knowledge and understanding of audio production tools in the studio and on location. Students will also be introduced to working with interactive audio platforms and environments.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 289340). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Cultures 4
intro: Develop professional skills for working on large scale productions and showing leadership. Students will be further supported in portfolio development and preparing for employment or self-employment in the creative industries. Students will also be supported in developing professional communication and presentation skills and to continue to strengthen personal growth and engagement in learning at this level.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 289750). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Virtual Volume Project
intro: This course introduced the Virtual Volume as a multi-use creative space. Students are encouraged to explore its possibilities for designing speculative environments, interactive soundscapes, and abstract visual systems. This course culminates in a collaborative small project rendered on the Virtual Volume.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 289226). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Creative Arts Special Topic 3D
intro: This course focuses on a particular aspect or aspects of creative arts. Offerings change from year to year and the course may not be offered in a particular year.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 289391). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Cultures in Screen Arts 3
intro: Develop professional skills in preparation for a career in the creative industries. Tauira will be supported in developing professional communication and presentation skills and to continue to strengthen personal growth and engagement in learning at this level.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 289350). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Political Power in Aotearoa New Zealand
intro: A critical study of politics and political power in Aotearoa New Zealand, with an emphasis on political institutions, the formal and informal exercise of power by political actors, and the interplay between politics and economics in policy development.
description: Year 2 Classics paper (15 credits, 200262). Prerequisite study in Classics is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Special Topic
intro: An advanced course of study designed to meet the needs of a student at 800-level.
description: Postgraduate Research Business paper (30 credits, 115815). Available at: Auckland. Offered: SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Cultures in Screen Arts 2
intro: A further orientation to the screen industry and environment, with specific emphasis on deepening creative practice and practicing within the context of Te Tiriti O Waitangi.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 289250). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Screen Arts Major Project
intro: Work to an advanced technical, creative and professional standard in a collaborative production environment to complete a Major Project. Students will put into production the projects developed in the Major Project Development course managing deadlines and schedules to ensure project completion.
description: Year 3 paper (30 credits, 289333). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Audio Production
intro: Gain a knowledge and understanding of industry standard tools for audio production. Students will also gain a creative and technical understanding of audio production processes and develop their knowledge and application of aesthetics and form.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 289240). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Game Project
intro: Further extend knowledge of industry standard tools for game development. Students will apply techniques, creative approaches, and methodologies to the production of a small game project.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 289230). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Major Project: Production and Practice
