description: Year 2 Politics paper (15 credits, 222265). Prerequisite study in Politics is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Visual Communication Design 2.2: Visualising Information
intro: In this course, students will learn narrative and data visualisation techniques to aid audience interpretation of information in compelling and accessible ways. Students will consider different audiences and produce responses across multiple media. This course provides an opportunity to apply and extend typographic, graphic design and illustration skills, and consider how interactivity and narrative can enhance the communication of complex information.
description: Year 2 Politics paper (15 credits, 222264). Prerequisite study in Politics is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Visual Communication Design 2.4: Brand Communication
intro: This course explores a designer’s role in creating, developing, positioning and implementing a brand. Students will create a visual identity system applied across print, packaging and promotional touchpoints. Learn about brand positioning, audience profiling and persuasive strategies alongside processes for logo identity design.
description: Year 2 Politics paper (15 credits, 222266). Prerequisite study in Politics is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Visual Communication Design 2.1: Visual Systems
intro: In this course students will explore strategies for developing a visual language using type and image. Students will design a printed publication using flexible and cohesive graphic systems for the meaningful, logical and consistent organisation of information on a page. This course develops skills that can be applied to any aesthetic, project or media across print and digital outputs.
description: Year 2 Politics paper (15 credits, 222263). Prerequisite study in Politics is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Visual Communication Design 1.3: Introduction to User Experience Design
intro: In this course students will be introduced to interaction design, and develop understanding of user experience design (UX) through the design of user interfaces (UI) in a web design context. Students will gain knowledge of prototyping and user testing practices through designing a digital interface, learning how information can be effectively communicated in online media. This course will be useful for all students interested in graphic design for interactive environments.
description: Year 1 Politics paper (15 credits, 222159). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Practicum of Blind & Low Vision (Developmental Orientation & Mobility)
intro: A professional and field-based practicum in the area of Blind and Low Vision, with a specific focus on Developmental Orientation & Mobility.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 249711). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Theory and Practice of Blind and Low Vision (Developmental Orientation & Mobility)
intro: An advanced study of the theory and practice of specialist teachers in the area of Blind and Low Vision, with a specific focus on Developmental Orientation & Mobility.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 249710). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Photography Studio 3.1: The Photobook and Narrative
intro: In this course we investigate the photographic book as a vehicle for exploring visual narrative. You will develop a self-directed theme into a photographic project, experiment with materiality, and construct a photobook. Visual language will be informed by encounters with innovative published work and consideration of audience. The course covers methods and strategies for book design, material considerations and production.
description: Year 3 Philosophy paper (15 credits, 221367). Prerequisite study in Philosophy is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Photography Studio 3.2: Post Photography
intro: Engaging with practice-based learning informed by research and experimentation you will extend photographic technologies and their possibilities. You will creatively engage with a self-selected photographic technology, considering both historical and current photographic approaches. Digital, moving image, lighting studio and analogue technologies can all be used within individual projects. Advanced analogue photography workshops will be delivered.
description: Year 3 Philosophy paper (15 credits, 221368). Prerequisite study in Philosophy is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Photography Studio 2.1: Attributes of Light
intro: By engaging with the attributes of light you will become articulate in understanding and influencing photographic meaning. You will develop skills in conventional and experimental photographic lighting technologies, within and beyond the lighting studio. Through photographic or moving image work, you will employ this knowledge in a self-directed project.
description: Year 2 Philosophy paper (15 credits, 221263). Prerequisite study in Philosophy is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Photography Studio 2.3: The Living Archive
intro: Engaging with practice-based learning informed by research and experimentation you will explore the photographic archive as a cultural repository. Investigating social, political, personal, and historical uses of photography you will respond to an archive through a creative photographic project. Content includes principles of archival collection and management, film scanning and creative use of archival material.
description: Year 2 Philosophy paper (15 credits, 221265). Prerequisite study in Philosophy is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Photography Studio 2.4: Photography, Representation and the Gaze
intro: How can creative practitioners formulate positions on critical issues associated with photographic representation and agency? You will develop strategic and ethically based explorations for a practical photographic outcome honouring the complexities of your chosen subject. You consider issues specific to making photographs in 21st century Aotearoa, including Te Tiriti, the gendered gaze and representation of people and whenua.
description: Year 2 Philosophy paper (15 credits, 221266). Prerequisite study in Philosophy is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Photography Studio 2.2: Fact and Fantasy
intro: You will explore how ongoing developments in digital technologies within wider visual culture affect photography and lens-based practice. Working with Photoshop and related technologies, you will apply research and experimentation to your own and others work. You will consider how an understanding of the discourse around digital photographic technologies and how photography functions as a language will impact your practice.
description: Year 2 Philosophy paper (15 credits, 221264). Prerequisite study in Philosophy is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Photography Studio 1.2: Introduction to the Darkroom
intro: How are analogue darkroom technologies employed in contemporary photographic practice? Through experimentation with materials and engagement with the wider world you will develop a practical project. You will be introduced to selected darkroom and wet-based analogue photographic techniques and consider how you will make them relevant to your practice.
description: Year 1 Philosophy paper (15 credits, 221158). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Cultures in Commercial Music 3
intro: Develop skills and knowledge for a future career in commercial music.  Students will be supported in developing professionalism within the context of the music industry.
description: Year 3 Paramedicine paper (15 credits, 133380). Prerequisite study in Paramedicine is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Applied Communication
intro: Students will develop communication theory and Te Tiriti informed responses to address a problem or challenge that faces a community, group, or organisation.
description: Year 3 English Literature paper (15 credits, 219346). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PR Consultancy
intro: An examination of the key skills, knowledge, and practices of public relations consultancy to both internal and external clients.
description: Year 3 English Literature paper (15 credits, 219320). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Pūtaiao: Mātauranga and the Environment
intro: This course will explore common Te Ao Māori values associated with Māori environmental management and provide practical experiences with assessing and monitoring our unique environments from a Māori and Iwi perspective. Students will be provided with opportunities to understand the holistic nature of Māori cultural concepts and engage with cutting edge Pūtaiao experiences.
description: Year 2 Foundation Studies paper (15 credits, 247220). Prerequisite study in Foundation Studies is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Public Policy Communication
intro: The course will cover the application of communication theories in the design, development, and implementation of public policies. Students will learn to engage in authentic community consultation to develop policies. They will critically examine public policies, analyse public opinion, and co-design community engagement. Attention will be given to incorporating the provisions and principles of Te Tiriti in public policy communication.
description: Year 2 English Literature paper (15 credits, 219229). Prerequisite study in English Literature is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fundamentals of Applied Communication
intro: Focusing on core global challenges around the Sustainable Development Goals, the course explores the nature and role of communication in addressing these challenges. Students learn to design and implement Te Tiriti-led strategic communication applications developed through culture-centred community partnerships.
description: Year 2 English Literature paper (15 credits, 219228). Prerequisite study in English Literature is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Commercial Music 120 credit Thesis Part 1
intro: Preparation of a thesis consisting of an advanced Commercial Music-led research project and an exegetical component to satisfy the requirements of the Master of Commercial Music qualification.
description: Postgraduate Research Paramedicine paper (60 credits, 133811). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: International Public Relations
intro: An examination of the practice and theory of public relations interculturally and internationally, overviewing the global industry structure and introducing specialist intercultural communication skills for internationally mobile public relations practice.
description: Year 2 English Literature paper (15 credits, 219219). Prerequisite study in English Literature is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Commercial Music 120 credit Thesis Part 2
intro: Continuing preparation of a thesis consisting of an advanced Commercial Music-led research project and an exegetical component to satisfy the requirements of the Master of Commercial Music qualification.
description: Postgraduate Research Paramedicine paper (60 credits, 133812). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Foundation Applied Sciences
intro: An introduction to the core principles and methodologies in mathematics, chemistry, and physics, equipping students with the necessary knowledge, skills, and interdisciplinary perspectives to succeed in their future undergraduate studies.
description: Foundation Foundation Studies paper (15 credits, 247010). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Foundation Natural Sciences
intro: An introduction to areas within natural sciences by providing a foundational understanding of the interactions between biology, chemistry, and the environment, and fostering the development of essential skills for understanding current issues including climate change, invasive species, sustainable practices and protecting native environments.
description: Foundation Foundation Studies paper (15 credits, 247011). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Foundation Analytical Thinking and Numeracy
intro: An introduction to essential skills and knowledge in analytical thinking, numeracy, and algebra to effectively interpret, analyse, and solve problems across various academic disciplines and real-world contexts.
description: Foundation Foundation Studies paper (15 credits, 247009). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Climate Science Consciousness
intro: An introduction to the scientific history and evidence for climate change. Evidence of anthropogenic influence on the climate will be explored by examining data gathered across all fields of science and across a range of environments including terrestrial, marine, and atmospheric. Students will develop strategies to understand, interpret and communicate scientific information in the context of climate change.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 246100). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Toi Atea 2
intro: The development of individual work that grows out of the investigation of a select range of Māori concepts, art forms, imagery, values and approaches that reflect a Māori world view.
description: Year 2 Applied Statistics paper (15 credits, 298263). Prerequisite study in Applied Statistics is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Cultures in Commercial Music 2
intro: A further orientation to the music industry and environment, with specific emphasis on working with and within communities, and practicing within the context of Te Tiriti O Waitangi.
description: Year 2 Paramedicine paper (15 credits, 133280). Prerequisite study in Paramedicine is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Māori Research Methodologies for Creative Practice
intro: Students will consider tikanga and methodologies relevant to their own creative practice, which may incorporate the use of customary and/or new technologies, innovations, and knowledge. This will include developing an understanding of tikanga, ethics and accountabilities around the use of mātauranga toi Māori, mātauranga-ā-iwi, mātauranga-ā-hapū and mātauranga-a-whānau in the development of their own creative outputs. Students will be supported to locate and articulate their own creative practice in a continuum of mātauranga toi Māori.
description: Postgraduate Applied Statistics paper (15 credits, 298730). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Toi Atea 4: Mana Whakaaturanga
intro: An advanced course to focus and refine visual language and personal tikanga through negotiated projects that explore topics of direct reference to Māori people and their communities.
description: Postgraduate Applied Statistics paper (15 credits, 298753). Prerequisite study in Applied Statistics is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Foundation Business Studies
intro: An introduction to fundamental principles and theories in business studies. Additionally, the course aims to equip students with the ability to apply key foundation finance, marketing, management, economic and accounting concepts to practical scenarios, fostering their ability to analyse, interpret, and propose solutions in various contexts.
description: Foundation Business paper (15 credits, 115030). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Toi Atea 3.1: Mana Whenua
intro: Developing visual responses to the exploration of issues (social, political, environmental, global) significant to Māori people and their communities.
description: Year 3 Applied Statistics paper (15 credits, 298367). Prerequisite study in Applied Statistics is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Mātauranga Toi Māori 3: Creative Innovation and Community Leadership
intro: This course propels students into the realm of Māori Arts Innovation and Community Leadership. By synthesizing foundational knowledge with advanced theoretical frameworks, innovative artistic projects, and community leadership principles, students will refine their skills as Māori Arts Practitioners and actively contribute to the evolution of Māori arts practices within a contemporary context.
description: Year 3 Applied Statistics paper (15 credits, 298360). Prerequisite study in Applied Statistics is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Mātauranga Toi Māori 2: Māori Creative Practices and Cultural Continuity
intro: Building upon the foundational concepts introduced in Māori Practices as a Māori Creative Practitioner, this course delves deeper into the intricate relationship between Māori creative practices and cultural continuity within Te Ao Māori. Through an advanced exploration of theoretical frameworks, hands-on creative projects, and community engagement, students will further refine their understanding of the nuanced role of Tikanga Māori in shaping contemporary Māori creative practice.
description: Year 2 Applied Statistics paper (15 credits, 298260). Prerequisite study in Applied Statistics is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Kōrero Tuku Iho | Cosmological Narratives within Māori Creative Expression
intro: This advanced-level course delves into the intricate relationship between Māori cosmological narratives and their dynamic manifestation within contemporary Māori creative expression. Through an interdisciplinary exploration that encompasses art, design, music, theatre, and dance, students will analyse the evolving role of cosmological narratives, connecting traditional wisdom to modern artistic innovation.
description: Year 3 Applied Statistics paper (15 credits, 298330). Prerequisite study in Applied Statistics is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Toi Ātea 1
intro: An introduction to the development of personal forms of expression though an engagement with the values, concepts, traditions, art/design forms and structures of the whare whakairo.
description: Year 1 Applied Statistics paper (15 credits, 298157). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Mātauranga Toi Māori 1: Māori Practices as a Māori Creative Practitioner
intro: This introductory course provides students with a foundational understanding of customary practices and values within Te Ao Māori and their relevance in contemporary Māori creative practice. Through a combination of theoretical exploration and practical engagement, students will gain insights into the cultural context shaping Māori creative expression and its significance in fostering personal and communal growth.
description: Year 1 Applied Statistics paper (15 credits, 298160). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Concept Design 3.2: Design for Game and Location-Based Experience
intro: In this studio course, students will develop a proof of concept design proposal for a given text through the comparative lens of Concept for Game and Location-Based Experience design. Students will establish a critical position through individual and collaborative creative exploration and articulation to expand on concept design concepts, techniques, skills and processes in the production of their creative work.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 296368). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Concept Design 3.1 Design for Film and Animation
intro: In this studio course, students will develop a proof of concept design proposal for a given text through the comparative lens of Concept for Film and Animation. Students will establish a critical position through individual and collaborative creative exploration and articulation to expand on concept design concepts, techniques, skills and processes in the production of their creative work.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 296367). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Cultures in Commercial Music 1
intro: An introduction to the music industry and environment, with specific emphasis on culture, ethics, learning, and positionality.
description: Year 1 Paramedicine paper (15 credits, 133180). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Concept Design 2.2: Prop and Creature
intro: In this studio course, students will explore and articulate creative responses to design challenges through critical awareness of prop and creature design and production. Students will develop and apply core 2D and 3D concept design skills, processes and techniques to create original prop and creature designs for an entertainment license.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 296264). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Concept Design 2.3: Visualisation
intro: In this studio, students will collaborate to explore and articulate the relationship between narrative and design for an animation or game design context. Students will engage critically through tone, genre and aesthetic studies, applying these through their creative responses using core concept design techniques. The final output will be a curated presentation of these finished ideas with consideration of both audience, client and medium.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 296265). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Concept Design 2.4 Design for Fabrication
intro: In this studio, students will explore the design process from concept through to fabrication. Students will critically engage with the role of designer and fabricator using core concept design techniques, methods of manufacture, digital and physical fabrication techniques, and production drawings to communicate concept intention and realisation. The design will be resolved in the form of a tangible output.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 296266). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Concept Design 2.1: Character and Environment
intro: In this studio course, students will explore and articulate creative responses to design challenges through critical awareness of character and environment design, and production. Students will develop and apply core 2D and 3D concept design skills, processes and techniques to create original character and environment designs for an entertainment license.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 296263). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Concept Design 1.2: Worldbuilding
intro: In this studio course, students will experiment with creative and analytical thinking skills, idea generation and visualisation central to concept design as a practice. They will develop specific techniques, skills and processes for investigating and defining worldbuilding techniques through storytelling and the creative practice of iterative digital modelling, prototyping and digital matte painting.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 296158). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Engage, Motivate, Persuade: Global Leadership Communication in Action
intro: Students will develop practical and critical skills in key facets of effective executive communication, including persuasion, motivation, and responding to disparate views.  Students will develop their understanding of their own communication style and develop skills in planning and delivering impactful presentations in diverse contexts. Practical insights into gaining and retaining attention and responding to resistance will be developed.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 295705). Available at: Auckland, N. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Creative Practice Research Methods
intro: Students will advance their understanding of approaches to creative practice research, methods and theory in relation to their specific practice orientation.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 293732). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Explorations in Image and Meaning
intro: Explorations in Image and Meaning explores how creative practitioners capture, construct and manipulate two-dimensional imagery. The course provides students with foundational skills and techniques to construct and analyse two dimensional images.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 293126). Available at: Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S1 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Explorations in Colour and Context
intro: This course provides students with the foundational knowledge and skills to understand and apply colour in their creative practice. Students will experiment with mixing pigments in analogue and digital environments and consider colour’s cultural, communicative, and evocative potential.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 293127). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Screen Arts 120 credit Thesis Part 2
intro: Continuing preparation of a thesis consisting of an advanced Screen Arts-led research project and an exegetical component to satisfy the requirements of the Master of Screen Arts qualification.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 289812). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Explorations in Narrative
intro: Explorations in Narrative introduces students to the processes of analysing, interpreting, and creating stories with the intent of realising the material through various creative practices. With the option of producing written or visual narratives, students will gain a foundation in storytelling that can be applicable to diverse creative practices.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 293125). Available at: Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Master of Screen Arts 120 credit Thesis Part 1
intro: Preparation of a thesis consisting of an advanced Screen Arts-led research project for partial fulfillment of the 90 credit or 120 credit MSA thesis options.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 289811). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Topics in Agronomy
intro: This course offers students modules covering different aspects of agronomy from which they need to select two. Modules: Weed control, farm forestry, seed technology and crop production. Each module runs for 6 weeks and includes laboratory or field work.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 283310). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Foundation Health Studies
intro: A multidisciplinary introduction to factors that affect the health and wellbeing of ourselves and our communities. Suitable for students transitioning into discipline areas that require a broad understanding of the fundamentals of health.
description: Foundation paper (15 credits, 214004). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2 and SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fine Arts Research Project: Honours
intro: In this studio research course students will advance their exploration of content, context, methods and the role of critical dialogue in the production and dissemination of contemporary art. In consultation with lecturers, students refine and present a substantial, critically reflexive practice- based research project.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 213830). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fine Arts Research and Development: Honours
intro: Advance the exploration of content, context, methodology and the role of critical dialogue in the production of contemporary art. In consultation with lecturers, students develop a substantial, innovative research project that engages in critically reflexive practice.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 213729). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Cultures in Art 3
intro: Learn skills and knowledge to develop sustainable art practice within Aotearoa.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 213370). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fine Arts Studio 3.1: Project
intro: In this studio course students will explore new possibilities for the production of contemporary art. They will investigate content, processes, context and critical dialogue to develop a body of work that extends their current practice.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 213343). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fine Arts Studio 3.2: Public Exhibition
intro: In this studio course students will situate their practice by investigating the relationship between the production and dissemination of art. They will develop artwork that extends their current practice and work in a collaborative group to present this work in a public exhibition.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 213344). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Cultures in Art 2
intro: A further orientation to developing artistic practice, with specific emphasis on working with and within communities, and practicing within the context of Te Tiriti O Waitangi.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 213270). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fine Arts Studio 2.1: Project
intro: In this studio course students will explore new possibilities for the production of contemporary art. They will investigate ideas, processes, context and critical thinking to develop an independent project that extends their current practice.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 213243). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fine Arts Studio 2.2: Exhibition
intro: In this studio course students will explore new possibilities for the production and exhibition of contemporary art. They will investigate ideas, processes, context and critical thinking to develop an independent project that extends their current practice.  They will present their work in a group exhibition.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 213244). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research in Sport, Exercise and Health
intro: This course will provide students with skills and knowledge for conducting and disseminating research in sport, exercise and health.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 234728). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Cultures in Art 1
intro: An introduction to developing artistic practice, with specific emphasis on ethics, fine art cultures, learning, and positionality.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 213170). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fashion Design 3.1: Industry, Markets and Processes
intro: Students will design a collection for a proposed brand and a specified consumer market, building on their fashion industry knowledge and production development skills.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 212367). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fashion Design 3.2: Tailoring Methods
intro: Students will build on their structured garment skills through design refinement and tailoring methods to realise tailored garments.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 212368). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fashion Design 2.2: Creative Contour and Drape
intro: Explore drape and pattern processes for innovative designs through application of woven fabric directly on the 3-dimensional form. Increase your understanding of fabric behaviour and its relationship to the body, fit, and relevant sampling, pattern making and garment construction methods, in an experimental fashion design brief resulting in garment realisation.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 212266). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fashion Design 2.1: Reimagined Fashion
intro:
Develop your critical inquiry and fashion design and technical skills through examination of historical and contemporary topics. 

You will focus on fashion research, idea generation, drawing, pattern making and construction, and applied fashion industry methods for woven fabrics, resulting in garment realisation.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 212265). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fashion Design 2.3: Inclusive Fashion
intro: Develop your fashion design understanding of stretch knit fabrics through critical analysis, design principles and problem solving while learning fashion industry methods and processes of drawing, pattern making and construction. You will apply individual and collaborative practice for an inclusive design project leading to garment realisation.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 212263). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fashion Design 2.4: Digital Creation and Application in Fashion
intro: Explore the intersection of digital technology and traditional fashion through innovative processes that enhance research and production while extending to essential manual techniques, including garment sizing and grading for industry-standard sizing. You will learn digital skills in apparel software for design explorations, pattern making, and sampling and garment development
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 212264). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fashion Design 1.2: Introduction to Fashion Practice
intro: This course provides students with introductory patternmaking and garment construction skills, to gain understanding of the garment development process for realising design ideas.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 212158). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Derivatives and Risk Management
intro: This course considers the roles of financial risk management in reducing risk and/or increasing returns in an organisation. Students will be exposed to derivatives and risk management from both theoretical and empirical perspectives.
description: Postgraduate Financial Planning paper (15 credits, 125710). Prerequisite study in Financial Planning is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Professional Programme Preparation
intro: This course provides a comprehensive overview of the CFA level I examination curriculum and the annually updated materials.
description: Postgraduate Financial Planning paper (15 credits, 125718). Prerequisite study in Financial Planning is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Earth Surface Processes and Landforms
intro: Erosion and sediment transport pose significant challenges for Aotearoa New Zealand's landscapes. This course delves into the processes driving changes in mountains, hillslopes, rivers, coastal, and marine environments as sediment is eroded, transported, and deposited. Moreover, we will explore the potential impacts of climate change on these processes and landforms. The course equips students with methods to analyse and forecast landform changes and sediment transport, laying the groundwork for effectively managing geomorphic issues that impact society and the environment.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 233215). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Earth's Changing Climate
intro: This course will focus on analysing recent climate reconstructions to gain insights into our current environment and to predict future changes. Understanding these trends is crucial for effective climate adaptation in environmental management. The course will delve into the investigation and reconstruction of past environments across diverse geological time frames and settings.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 233309). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Natural Hazards and Climate Change
intro: This course will delve into an in-depth analysis of the generation and occurrence of natural hazards, their impacts, and effective risk management strategies. This course also includes the consideration of how climate change is modifying natural events. Students will explore and analyse natural hazards and climate change that impact our environment and future global risk reduction and sustainability initiatives with a focus on creating solutions that mitigate the impact of hazards on society.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 233303). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Earth’s Critical Resources
intro: This course offers essential insights into how Earth’s resources shape our environment, emphasising the critical importance of natural resources and environmental management. It delves into the composition of planet Earth, exploring the processes that govern its formation and the distribution of important minerals. This course explores the fundamental principles of the dynamic processes that drive the evolution of the surface of our planet, and the pivotal role of minerals in sustaining life on our planet.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 233209). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Special Topic in Veterinary Science 4.2
intro: This semester two special topic course for BVSc4 students will be utilised for students progressing from the old BVSc curriculum to the new BVSc curriculum to ensure that all appropriate content is covered.
description: Honours Development Studies paper (60 credits, 227444). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Capstone Project: Design Practice Synthesis
intro: Development and completion of a creative project that synthesises the learning undertaken within the taught qualification.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (30 credits, 197816). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Design Research Development
intro: This course guides students through processes and practices for developing a postgraduate research project in design.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 197753). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Creative Arts Internship
intro: Learn about professional creative practice in an internship, alongside a creative professional, researcher, hapu/iwi or indigenous framework that you connect with.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 293710). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Upskilling and Practice-based knowledge
intro: This course is specifically designed for students who are new to postgraduate study within the College of Creative Arts.  It allows you to upskill in key curriculum areas to support your creative practice.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 293705). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Integrated Design Studio
intro:
Using a combination of personally developed integrated design strategies and those that are drawn from well established modes of design, students will identify and create methods, processes and practices through a research investigation. This will inform and contribute towards their individually developed integrated design approach. Students will conduct a facilitated research project of their choice.

This course continues the processes established in Integrated Design Practice offered in Year 2.
description: Year 3 paper (30 credits, 293358). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Integrated Design Practice
intro: This studio based course explores how interdisciplinarity - the interplay of knowledge and methods from different disciplines - might benefit the formation of independent creative practice. Students will develop systems and processes through the examination of social and creative practices that are revealed and sourced from within design and beyond.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 293263). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Explorations in Toi Auaha – Paint and Creative Expression
intro: Explore mātauranga Māori, cultural perspectives and indigenous ways of knowing as a foundation for creative expression through paint and multi-media. This course will enable you to develop your own practice in paint and extend this practice to explore multi-media artworks inspired by mātauranga Māori, cultural narratives and indigenous ways of seeing the world.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 293128). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Explorations in Te Aho Pūoro Māori – An Introduction and Insight into Traditional Māori Instruments
intro:
In this course you will be introduced to taonga pūoro Māori through hands on engagement coupled with an insight to tikanga and mātauranga connected to this practice.

This course will encourage creative exploration with taonga pūoro, with potential to add another dimension to your creative practice. Through this mahi toi, you will gain greater connection and understanding of te ao Māori.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 293129). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Explorations in Pacific Positionalities
intro: Explore the concept of Pacific Island diaspora within the context of Aotearoa. You will be introduced to a range of unique perspectives, experiences, and cultural identities that have been embodied, and disseminated through the Pacific arts in Aotearoa. The goal of this course is to develop a deeper understanding of diverse Pacific narratives through creative practice and storytelling.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 293124). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Veterinary Science 4.1
intro: This integrated course builds on concepts and content from the first three years of the programme and provides students with specific information about the diagnosis and treatment of a range of conditions or presentations focussed on the main domestic species encountered in Aotearoa New Zealand.
description: Honours Development Studies paper (60 credits, 227441). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Special Topic in Veterinary Science 4.1
intro: This semester one special topic course for BVSc4 students will be utilised for students progressing from the old BVSc curriculum to the new BVSc curriculum to ensure that all appropriate content is covered.
description: Honours Development Studies paper (60 credits, 227443). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Veterinary Science 4.2
intro: This integrated course expands on content taught in BVSc4, semester one and builds on concepts and content from the first three years of the programme and provides students with information about the diagnosis and treatment of a range of conditions or presentations that either need to be explored further or were not covered in 227.441 focussed on the main domestic species encountered in Aotearoa New Zealand.
description: Honours Development Studies paper (60 credits, 227442). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: He Hokinga Mahara: Hauora theory to praxis
intro: This course focusses on national and/or international Indigenous health perspectives and approaches. Students will compare and contrast theoretical and philosophical viewpoints of indigenous health and wellbeing development. This will include an examination of various indigenous health and wellbeing practices. Students will engage in innovative, creative and self-determining actions to (re)design practical health and wellbeing solutions within a community and/or organisations.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 150704). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Hangarau: Māori and technology
intro: An analysis of technology, its history and contemporary applications as it relates to te ao Māori and the implications of technology use for whānau, hapū and iwi development. A critical examination of the role of technology in advancing Māori and indigenous rights and future aspirations.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 150740). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Amokura: Contemporary Leadership in Māori Development
intro: A critical examination of traditional and contemporary forms of Māori professional, personal and cultural leadership and the role of iwi, community and tribal organisations in governance.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 150741). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: He Tātai Arorangi: Māori Astronomy
intro: An advanced exploration of Māori and Indigenous astronomy, examining epistemological foundations, historical applications, and modern relevance. Students critically analyse how Māori and Indigenous celestial knowledge contributes to timekeeping, ecological understanding, environmental sustainability, ritual, ceremony and contemporary practices.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 150703). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Education and the Law
intro: This course explores the intersection of law and education in Aotearoa New Zealand. It offers educators with non-legal backgrounds opportunities to inquire into topical legal issues relating to education.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 254720). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Design Thesis 90 Credit Part 1
intro: Preparation of a thesis consisting of an advanced design-led research project to satisfy the requirements of the Master of Design qualification.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (30 credits, 197824). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Financial Technology
intro: This course examines key concepts relevant to emerging challenges in the financial sector and explores technologies that support Financial Technology (FinTech) applications.
description: Postgraduate Research Financial Planning paper (30 credits, 125821). Prerequisite study in Financial Planning is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Design Thesis 90 Credit Part 2
intro: Preparation of a thesis consisting of an advanced design-led research project to satisfy the requirements of the Master of Design qualification.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 197825). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Capstone Project: Documentation and Reflection
intro: This course supports the students' contextualisation and design practices for their capstone projects.  Informed by primary and secondary research, students locate their practice in relation to national and international examples, models, and mentors.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (30 credits, 197815). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Case Studies in FinTech and Sustainable Finance
intro: This course utilises case study analysis to explore the integration of FinTech and sustainable finance, examining innovative financial technologies that promote sustainability, enhance financial inclusion, and address global challenges.
description: Postgraduate Research Financial Planning paper (30 credits, 125820). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: A World at War, 1900-1945
intro: This course focuses on the origins, courses, and legacies of the First and Second World Wars. It adopts a broad perspective on the conflicts, considering their military, political, and social aspects.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 148302). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Tiriti in History
intro: This course explores Te Tiriti in the context of nineteenth-century wars, raupatu, continuing conflict over land, and decolonial historical scholarship in the present.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 148209). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Tātai whetū – Māori and Indigenous Astronomy
intro: This course will provide students with an understanding of the origins, understandings and evolution/growth of Māori and Indigenous astronomy. With a focus primarily on Māori astronomy the key areas of learning focus on celestial knowledge, including uses, practices, movement and applications of the sun, moon and stars and how they relate to the environment as a whole and ecological systems.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 150113). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Te Pouhere Kōrero: Māori and Indigenous Histories
intro: This course focuses on methods, theories, tikanga and ethics at the forefront in the mahi and research of iwi/Māori and other Indigenous historians.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 148346). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Studies in Design: Selected Topics 2
intro: Students select and complete taught modules of learning from a range of practice and theoretically focused options.  Students demonstrate their understanding of the modules through contextual-creative engagement that explores the selected subjects as well as the relationships between them.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 197746). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Pacific Peoples in New Zealand
intro: An introduction to the distinctive cultures of Pacific  Peoples in New Zealand. Students will develop an understanding of core values, traditions, cultural protocols, social processes and world views that are characteristic of Pacific cultures in the New Zealand context.
description: Year 1 Psychology paper (15 credits, 175104). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced studies in design: Selected topics 1
intro: Students select and complete taught modules of learning from a range of practice and theoretically focused options.  Students demonstrate their understanding of the modules through contextual-creative engagement that explores the selected subjects as well as the relationships between them.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 197745). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Voyages of Ancestors: Atlantic and Pacific Journeys 800-1800
intro: The course compares reasons and effects of maritime journeys by Polynesians with those of Europeans, from the Vikings onwards. It deals with encounters with other peoples in the Americas, and with colonialism.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 148202). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Shaping the Modern World: Global History Since 1945
intro: This course develops an understanding of the contemporary world by examining the people, events, ideas and forces that have shaped the world since 1945.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 148201). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Making History
intro: An introduction to the multiple ways the past has been imagined, represented, used and misused in different societies, from medieval to modern, "Western" to Māori.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 148102). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Design Project: Ideation and Development
intro: Students develop their understanding of a range of design-focused practices and research methods through the creation of a creative portfolio.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 197744). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Integration of Food Safety and Nutrition
intro: This course will explore the integration of food safety and nutrition from a range of perspectives including policy, food regulation, food formulation and consumer choice and health.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 141732). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Toxins and Food Safety
intro: An exploration of the variety of toxins in foods from different sources and their management in the food industry.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 141730). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Allergens and Food Intolerance
intro: The growing concern with food allergies and intolerance is the issue of food allergens.  This course examines the mechanisms of food allergy and intolerance, the identification of allergenic compounds, and the management of product development and production to ensure sensitive individuals are not exposed to such components.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 141731). Available at: Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Climate Science Connections
intro: In this course, students will experience a capstone work-integrated learning opportunity to engage with external partners from industry, commercial and governmental departments in multidisciplinary teams to grapple with wicked challenges, and use their discipline knowledge, critical and creative thinking to undertake a project, with mentoring from external partners, adjunct and academic mentors, to develop advanced professional skills, preparing them for employment after their degree.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 246300). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Climate Science in Context
intro: Students will apply disciplinary literacies developed in prior courses to examine recent efforts in climate science research. Scientific literature will be examined to identify contemporary approaches and methods in use to observe, interpret and mitigate the effects of climate change. Students will further develop their digital literacies, critical thinking skills and scaffold their disciplinary knowledges to wicked problem solving in the context of sustainable climate systems.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 246200). Prerequisite study is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Prescribing and Advanced Practice for Nurse Practitioners 2
intro: This course is a supervised practicum designed to meet Nursing Council of New Zealand standards and competencies for advanced clinical practice and work as an autonomous independent Nurse practitioner.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (30 credits, 168871). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Prescribing and Advanced Practice for Nurse Practitioners 1
intro: This course prepares nurses to meet Nursing Council of New Zealand standards and competencies for registered nurses who are trainee Nurse Practitioners (NPs).  The course focuses on building diagnostic reasoning skills and knowledge, and prescribing competence at the Nurse Practitioner scope of practice.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (30 credits, 168870). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Spanish at Work
intro: This course both introduces students to the basics of the Spanish language and exposes them to specialised structures, vocabulary and cultural knowledge applied to professional environments. No prior knowledge of Spanish is required.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 245104). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Reproducible Visualisation for Statistics
intro: A study of the key processes and concepts underlying reproducible data visualisation in statistics. Applications include mapping, time series forecasting and visual inference across all domains.
description: Year 3 Statistics paper (15 credits, 161307). Prerequisite study in Statistics is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Bayesian Computational Statistics: Towards Causal Thinking
intro: This course covers the ideas underlying statistical modelling in science through the lens of causal thinking. We cover the implementation of these ideas through Bayesian computational methods and links to practical applications including those in Ecology, Genetics, Nutrition Science and Psychology.
description: Year 3 Statistics paper (15 credits, 161308). Prerequisite study in Statistics is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Statistical Modelling for Experiments
intro: This is a core course that provides essential grounding in statistical inference and modelling relating to science and food technology. Students will learn how to design, conduct, and analyse the results of controlled experiments.
description: Year 2 Statistics paper (15 credits, 161228). Prerequisite study in Statistics is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Research Project (60 credits)
intro: An applied research project involving the critical evaluation and application of selected research methodologies to address communication-related challenges.
description: Postgraduate Research English Literature paper (60 credits, 219856). Available at: Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Construction Design Management Capstone
intro: This course provides students with the opportunity to consolidate and apply the design management knowledge and skills they have developed over the course of the degree.
description: Year 3 Creative Writing paper (15 credits, 218307). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Building Physics and Construction Services
intro: This course covers the engineering design and integration of mechanical, electrical, and plumbing (MEP) systems in buildings and other construction facilities. Students will learn sustainable solutions for energy efficiency using smart building technologies.
description: Year 3 Creative Writing paper (15 credits, 218305). Prerequisite study in Creative Writing is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Natural Language Processing and Multi-Agent Reasoning
intro: Advanced study of machine-learning methods for natural language processing and large language models. The course includes neural networks and deep learning methods for extracting information from practical datasets. The course has a practical focus on using modern machine learning tools for real-world applications. Ethical, legal and cultural responsibilities regarding data and implications of AI tools for society are discussed.
description: Year 3 Computer Science paper (15 credits, 159323). Prerequisite study in Computer Science is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Community Theatre
intro: What role does theatre have in the community? Is there a valid place for community theatre in a secular society? If so, what stories need to be told through theatre? How might we tell them? The exploration of these questions will involve, initially, the examination of a range of historical and contemporary models of community theatre. Students will then engage in exploratory workshops, in community research, writing, rehearsals and theatrical performance.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 299763). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 1
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 299861). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Computer Vision
intro: An advanced study of computer vision and image processing with a focus on the use of artificial intelligence and machine learning techniques. The course covers key concepts such as image representation, feature detection, and image classification.
description: Year 3 Computer Science paper (15 credits, 159324). Prerequisite study in Computer Science is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Built Environment Design for Excellence (DfX)
intro: This course introduces the concept of Design for Excellence (DfX) in construction projects, incorporating methodologies such as Design for Manufacture and Assembly (DfMA), Design for Sustainability (DfS), and Design for Reliability (DfR). Students will explore how DfX principles can be utilised to improve project coordination and efficiency in construction projects.
description: Year 3 Creative Writing paper (15 credits, 218303). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: PhD Creative Communication
description: Doctoral paper (120 credits, 299901). Available at: Auckland, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: Full Year. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Construction Design Solutions
intro: This course focuses on construction design from conception to detailed design to drawings ready for construction. Students will be taught the concept of universal building design for accessibility and inclusivity.
description: Year 3 Creative Writing paper (15 credits, 218306). Prerequisite study in Creative Writing is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Planning and Digital Built Environment for Design Management
intro: Students develop construction project planning and scheduling skills and apply these to the production of design-related information for decision-making. This includes the practical application of design information to the digital simulation of design, construction and operation of buildings.
description: Year 2 Creative Writing paper (15 credits, 218206). Prerequisite study in Creative Writing is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Creative Writing Workshop
intro: An advanced study and practice of creative writing in its myriad contemporary forms.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 299765). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Construction Parametric and Generative Modelling
intro: This course explores computational design methodologies, focusing on parametric modelling and generative algorithms powered by AI for construction design projects. Students will be exposed to the use of digital tools to develop adaptive and performance-driven construction designs.
description: Year 3 Creative Writing paper (15 credits, 218304). Prerequisite study in Creative Writing is required. Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Integrated Construction Design Management
intro: This course develops the skills for managing multidisciplinary construction design teams, using building information modelling (BIM) execution plans to support integrated building decision-making. Emphasis will be placed on managing complex, full-federated BIM models, enabling students to lead collaborative design processes in real-world construction environments.
description: Year 2 Creative Writing paper (15 credits, 218205). Prerequisite study in Creative Writing is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Thesis 120 Credit Part 2
intro: A supervised and guided independent study resulting in a published work.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 299862). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S1 & S2 and S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Theatre for Innovation and Communication
intro: An advanced, practical exploration of theatrical improvisation techniques in relation to enhancing creativity, innovation, leadership, teamwork, and communication performance, with an emphasis on the application of theatrical techniques to communication and innovation challenges.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 299764). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Writing Science
intro: An in-depth exploration of the practical skills needed to write for disciplinary academic and/or public audiences. Particular emphasis will be placed on the rhetorical context of science, audience analysis, literacy expertise, reading science, and narrative use of data and analogies when writing in a public or professional context.  Students may focus their assignments on writing for disciplinary and/or public audiences.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 299749). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Creative Writing III: Starting Your Manuscript
intro: An advanced study of the process of writing that consolidates knowledge of creative writing craft, expands understanding of genre, and analyses aesthetic and/or cultural aspects of published manuscripts across genres. The first portion of an envisioned full-length creative manuscript will be drafted with an emphasis on the critical evaluation of its aesthetic and/or cultural implications.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 299380). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Methods in Machine Learning
intro: An introduction to machine learning including key concepts, methods and algorithms. The course has a focus on neural networks and training methods and includes the practical application and implementation of machine learning algorithms as well as modern tools and libraries. The ethical and responsible use of data and the impact of artificial intelligence tools on society is discussed.
description: Year 2 Computer Science paper (15 credits, 159224). Prerequisite study in Computer Science is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Advanced Fiction Writing
intro: An exploration of the poetics and politics of experimentation and subversion in contemporary fiction and metafiction including analysis of the work (both creative and critical) of major practitioners, theorists and original student compositions.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 299329). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Critical and Creative Research
intro: An introduction to practices of research with creative texts. Students will learn practical techniques for planning and producing research in critical and/or creative modes. Fostering a critical voice and debating the cultural and social value of aesthetic communication will be integral to the course.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 299703). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Rhetoric, Composition and the Teaching of Writing
intro: An advanced introduction to rhetoric and composition as a framework for writing research and writing instruction.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 299710). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Creativity in the Community
intro: The course provides an applied service learning project in the disciplines of expressive arts and media studies.  Working collaboratively, students apply skills in  theatre, performance, film-making, creative writing, media practice or mixed media to developing a creative response to a social issue or community need.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 299333). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Artificial Intelligence Methods
intro: An introduction to the core concepts and methods of Artificial Intelligence. The course includes the implementation and application of a range of problem-solving, knowledge representation, and reasoning algorithms. Students will gain hands-on programming experience and a foundation in AI methodologies.
description: Year 2 Computer Science paper (15 credits, 159223). Prerequisite study in Computer Science is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Script Writing
intro: An in-depth study of the skills, formats, technique and terminology of professional script writing, with emphasis on the adaptation of traditional approaches across the diversity of contemporary media.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 299323). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Theatre in Production
intro: This course studies theatre as an expressive art, with a focus on processes of adaptation, particularly the creation of new texts from old.  It combines critical and practical research, including the presentation of a developed stage work, to examine how design, music and acting generate meaning in the performance of a text.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 299320). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Writing the Environment
intro: A study of the relationships between creative writing and ecological concerns, covering a range of contemporary forms from eco-fictions, nonfictions, or poetry, to nature writing, to animal stories. It engages students in the workshopped production of original creative work.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 299309). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Fiction Writing
intro: An intermediate-level study of the craft of fiction, investigating a range of forms by means of creative production, workshopping and peer review.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 299285). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Modern Drama
intro: A study of innovative modern plays, by means of investigative workshops, theatrical performances, lecture/demonstrations and seminar presentations.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 299303). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Theatre for Change
intro: An applied introduction to the varied ways in which theatre is used for social and personal change.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 299220). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Creative Writing II: Creative Nonfiction
intro: An intermediate-level introduction to the craft of nonfiction writing in a variety of genres, with a particular focus on the application of techniques usually associated with fiction and poetry to nonfiction material.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 299280). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.
