title: Making Plays for Theatre
intro: Students will receive a grounding in the skills of writing experimental theatre and an opportunity to employ these skills in the creation of original scripts.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 299224). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Writing Poetry
intro: A creative writing course in which students develop and advance poetry skills within the major modes of lyric poetry and within the context of a more advanced engagement with fundamental elements of craft. In addition to reading poetry and critical essays on the genre, students will write original poetry and critically review their own work and the work of peers.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 299229). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Writing for Children
intro: An intermediate-level introduction to the craft of writing targeted at ‘middle readers’ – roughly 9-13 years – across a variety of forms.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 299225). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Creative Processes
intro: An investigation of human creativity that involves the study of creative practice and the making of original works of performance, film and writing.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 299223). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Speaking: Theory and Practice
intro: A study of the theoretical and practical aspects of public speaking. Attention will be paid to building a rapport with an audience, to the preparation of material for spoken delivery and to the technical elements of voice production.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 299209). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Creative Writing I
intro: An exploration of the processes involved in writing poetry and short stories.  Students learn the fundamental elements of craft, such as metaphor, structure and plot, through the close reading of published poetry and fiction, through their own practice as creative writers, and through providing and receiving workshop feedback.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 299123). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Manawatū, Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Practical Software Engineering
intro: This course is designed to provide a comprehensive primer to programming and software engineering principles. Focusing on a mainstream programming language, the course builds on programming concepts that complement software engineering practice.
description: Postgraduate Information Systems paper (15 credits, 158710). Available at: Auckland. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Creative Communication
intro: An introduction to the dynamics involved in creative communication. Students explore creative communication through work in, and analysis of, three creative forms: creative writing, theatre and digital media production.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 299133). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online, Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Toi Ātea 3.2
intro: A capstone project developing creative responses to the exploration of issues (social, political, environmental, global) significant to Māori people and their communities
description: Year 3 Applied Statistics paper (30 credits, 298358). Prerequisite study in Applied Statistics is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1 & S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Drama in Performance
intro: An exploration of three plays in performance. Students will study theories and conventions of performance and will participate in theatrical presentations. No previous experience of theatre is required.
description: Year 1 paper (15 credits, 299104). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Intelligence-led Policing
intro: This course provides a critical examination of the precept and practice of intelligence-led policing and its derivative intelligence adjacent practices in policing and other policy and enforcement domains.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 294710). Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: The Future of Security and Warfare
intro: This course provides an exploration of the key global trends that are shaping the future of security and warfare and examines how these trends interact to impact at the individual, societal, state, and international level.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 294310). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Distance/Online. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Transformative Creative Practice Topic 6
intro: Explore how creative practice intersects with pressing social, cultural and environmental challenges. This course invites students from all creative backgrounds to consider how their practice addresses these challenges.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 293306). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Transformative Creative Practice
intro: Explore how creative practice intersects with pressing social, cultural and environmental challenges. This course invites students from all creative backgrounds to consider how their practice addresses these challenges.
description: Postgraduate paper (15 credits, 293707). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Master of Screen Arts 90 Credit Thesis Part 2
intro: Continuing preparation of a thesis, focusing primarily on post-production of an advanced Screen Arts-led research project to satisfy the requirements of the Master of Screen Arts qualification.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (30 credits, 289821). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Transformative Creative Practice Topic 4
intro: Explore how creative practice intersects with pressing social, cultural and environmental challenges. This course invites students from all creative backgrounds to consider how their practice addresses these challenges.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 293304). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Transformative Creative Practice Topic 2
intro: Explore how creative practice intersects with pressing social, cultural and environmental challenges. This course invites students from all creative backgrounds to consider how their practice addresses these challenges.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 293302). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Transformative Creative Practice Topic 5
intro: Explore how creative practice intersects with pressing social, cultural and environmental challenges. This course invites students from all creative backgrounds to consider how their practice addresses these challenges.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 293305). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Transformative Creative Practice Topic 1
intro: Explore how creative practice intersects with pressing social, cultural and environmental challenges. This course invites students from all creative backgrounds to consider how their practice addresses these challenges.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 293301). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Transformative Creative Practice Topic 3
intro: Explore how creative practice intersects with pressing social, cultural and environmental challenges. This course invites students from all creative backgrounds to consider how their practice addresses these challenges.
description: Year 3 paper (15 credits, 293303). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Project Distribution and Exhibition
intro: Students prepare for the distribution and exhibition of their thesis project.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (30 credits, 289822). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Master of Screen Arts 90 Credit Thesis Part 1
intro: Continuing preparation of a thesis, focusing primarily on production of an advanced Screen Arts-led research project to satisfy the requirements of the Master of Screen Arts qualification.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (60 credits, 289820). Available at: Wellington. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: 3D Digital Arts
intro: This course builds upon foundational 3D skills to develop proficiency in advanced techniques for game development, animation, spatial, concept and visual effects (VFX) for screen. Students will deepen their understanding of 3D digital asset creation while working within industry-standard pipelines. The course emphasizes practical applications, creative problem-solving, and technical workflows to prepare students for professional projects in the creative technology industries. Students will gain extended creative and technical understanding of 3D digital processes and develop their knowledge and application of aesthetics and form.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 289231). Prerequisite study is required. Available at: Wellington. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Principles of Horticultural Production Systems
intro: The course provides a balanced overview of the major production systems and practices of modern commercial horticulture through both theoretical learning and hands-on experience. Students will explore in depth the fundamental principles and innovative practices and technologies involved in the production of horticultural crops, including plant propagation, crop establishment and management. Emphasis will be given to the relationship between horticulture and the environment and the integration of sustainable production practices.
description: Year 2 paper (15 credits, 284210). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Innovative Business Solutions
intro: Using a design thinking approach to solving business problems, transdisciplinary teams will tackle managerial problems in conversation with industry partners, via a live-action learning project with real impact for global business.
description: Postgraduate Research paper (30 credits, 152810). Available at: Auckland. Offered: SS. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: The Contemporary Workplace: Business Management and Professional Skills
intro: An introduction to business management in Aotearoa New Zealand, and the skills, tools and techniques needed for success in professional business practice. Students will engage with core concepts, examples, and case studies relevant to a range of business functions.
description: Postgraduate paper (30 credits, 152716). Available at: Auckland, Distance/Online. Offered: S1 and S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Mauri Ora: Co-Planning for a Flourishing World
intro: This course explores Mauri Ora/Flourishing as a set of embodied values and practices that students can apply in relation to themselves and in partnership with tangata whenua to make a positive contribution to Planning in a grounded context.
description: Honours Midwifery paper (15 credits, 132400). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Planning Healthy Futures Studio
intro: This course explores the evolving and challenging dynamics between urban planning, public health, and wellbeing for healthier futures for people, places, and the planet. It recognises the vital role that planners and the profession have in designing and delivering healthy places and relationships that connect and strengthen communities in everyday and extraordinary contexts.
description: Year 3 Midwifery paper (15 credits, 132310). Prerequisite study in Midwifery is required. Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S2. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Business in the Digital Age
intro: Examines the essential roles of data, technology, and information credibility in contemporary business. Students will develop critical skills in evaluating and applying digital tools, and will explore the ethical use of data and information sources to support business decision making.
description: Year 1 Business paper (15 credits, 115117). Offered: see Massey website. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.

title: Introduction to Urban Planning
intro: An introduction to urban planning, which introduces the historical, contemporary and futuristic dynamics of professional urban planning as conceived and practised in Aotearoa/New Zealand and internationally. Students are encouraged to analyse existing cities in the context of a sustainable and decolonised future.
description: Year 1 Midwifery paper (15 credits, 132103). Available at: Distance/Online, Manawatū. Offered: S1. Visit massey.ac.nz for full course details and learning outcomes.
